  • Provide LEV for dry product addition sites, drum handling, manways, and spills in wet areas. The use of charging isolators or booths to minimize the flammable and potent/hazardous material exposure in the room should be considered.
  • Aqueous chemistry usually does not require once-through air; the AF&ID for OSD may be applied in these areas.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：API 濕段 HVAC 設計邏輯

API Wet End（原料藥濕段）是化學合成的反應段，通常涉及溶劑使用、化學反應和中間體處理。HVAC 設計的核心考量是安全性（溶劑爆炸風險）和人員保護（高效化合物暴露）：

  • 開放操作 + 溶劑 → 必須 Once-through（100% 排風）
  • 密閉製程 → 可以迴風，但需要 LEL 監控作為安全網
  • 水相化學 → 無溶劑風險，可用一般 OSD 級別的 HVAC

MERV 7 + MERV 13 是「Good Housekeeping」級別的過濾配置——不要求 HEPA，因為 API 濕段通常不是潔淨區。

比喻說明 Analogy

API Wet End 的 HVAC 就像加油站的通風設計：

  • 開放加油區（開放操作）：必須全通風、不迴風，因為汽油蒸氣有爆炸風險
  • 密封油槽區（密閉製程）：可以允許部分迴風，但需要氣體偵測器
  • 洗車區（水相化學）：只用水，無易燃風險，普通通風即可

重點提示 Key Notes

Charging Isolator/Booth（投料隔離器/投料亭）是本節的關鍵概念。在 API 濕段，最危險的操作之一是「加料」——打開反應器人孔蓋投入固體原料。此時溶劑蒸氣大量逸出、粉塵也可能擴散。使用投料隔離器或投料亭可以把這個高風險操作限制在密閉空間內，大幅降低房間級別的 HVAC 負擔。

低位回風口是 API 區域的標配：因為溶劑蒸氣比空氣重（大多數有機溶劑的蒸氣密度 > 1），會沉降到地面附近，低位回風口可以有效排除。

實務應用 Practical Application

CDMO 在接案評估 API 合成案時，必須確認製程中使用的溶劑種類和用量。如果主要反應是水相化學（如生物觸媒反應），HVAC 改造成本很低。但如果涉及大量有機溶劑（如 THF、DCM、甲醇），就需要 Once-through 系統、LEL 監控、防爆電氣和緊急排風——這些都是重大資本投入。務必在報價階段就將 HVAC 改造成本納入。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為什麼 API 濕段只要 MERV 7 + MERV 13 過濾而非 HEPA？從產品風險角度思考。
  2. 「Dilution ventilation on emergency power may minimize electrical classification requirements」——這句話的意思是什麼？為什麼充分稀釋通風可以降低防爆等級要求？
3.5 Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) – Dry End（原料藥—乾段）

原文內容 Original Text

Figure 3.2: Final API Dry-End Schematic (Once-Through for Solvents or Potency) (AFD schematic — not available for embedding)

3.5.1 System Design Considerations

  • Oral dosage ("dry") API product handling areas (i.e., centrifuge, dryer, blender, mill, pack-off rooms) do not require classified cleanrooms; however, they should be designed in a manner consistent with CNC, OSD design concepts as described in various ISPE Baseline Guides (Reference 13, Appendix 12) (similar to European Grade D – capable of meeting ISO 8 AT REST, but not monitored).
  • Areas serving final bulk product that will become sterile product (including bulk biopharmaceuticals) should meet Grade 8 (Grade C). This generally requires HEPA filtration on the supply air, low returns, pressure airlock (to keep exposed powders in the room), and instrumentation for verification of room conditions. Final drug substances areas may need to meet the requirements for dispensing of API in the finishing facility. For sterile or API that will become sterile, the ISPE Baseline Guide on Sterile Manufacturing Facilities (Reference 13, Appendix 12) should be consulted (may be Grade C or D with air filtration, DP control, airlock, etc.)
  • Manufacturing rooms should be fitted with low or combination high/low returns.
  • LEV should be provided for dry product addition, drum handling, manways, and where spills are likely in wet areas.
  • See Chapter 3 of this Guide (API-Wet) for considerations if solvents are used in the process.
  • Closed intermediate product processes are usually in temperature-controlled vessels and have no area requirements. See the ISPE Baseline Guide on APIs (Reference 13, Appendix 12).
  • In general, where flammability is not an issue, recirculated HVAC is possible.
  • AHU filtration should be MERV 7 followed by MERV 13. Final filter grade should assess potential product size and potency. See note above for final aseptic API steps.
  • Potent API containment exhaust should not be returned to the room. Scrubbers or HEPA filters are recommended in exhaust for potent products.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：乾段的雙軌設計原則

API Dry End（原料藥乾段）是 API 的固態處理階段（離心、乾燥、混合、研磨、包裝）。設計邏輯取決於最終用途：

  • 口服固體製劑用 API → CNC 等級（≈ EU Grade D capable，ISO 8 靜態），不需要分級認證
  • 最終成為無菌產品的 API（包括生物製劑原液）→ Grade C（ISO 8），需要 HEPA、低位回風、氣閘（Airlock）、儀表監控

這就是「產品最終用途決定設計等級」的具體體現。

比喻說明 Analogy

API 乾段的設計等級就像食品加工廠的衛生等級：

  • 做餅乾用的麵粉（口服 API）：工廠要乾淨，但不需要手術室等級
  • 做嬰兒配方奶粉的原料（將成為無菌產品的 API）：必須在接近手術室的環境中操作，因為最終產品會直接注射到人體

同樣是處理粉末，因為終端用途不同，HVAC 投資可能差 3-5 倍。

重點提示 Key Notes

Airlock（氣閘）在 API 乾段有雙重功能：

  1. 防止粉塵外洩：氣閘形成壓力緩衝區，開門時粉塵不會擴散到走廊
  2. 維持壓差穩定：沒有氣閘時，開門瞬間壓差歸零，如果走廊有其他產品的粉塵就可能被吸入

Containment Exhaust 的關鍵規則：高效 API 的密閉排風絕對不能回到房間。排風中含有高濃度活性成分，如果經過迴風系統再送回房間，操作人員會持續暴露。必須用 HEPA 或洗滌器處理後排到大氣。

實務應用 Practical Application

CDMO 在規劃 API 乾段設施時，最重要的決策是：這個設施未來只做口服 API，還是也要服務注射劑 API？如果一開始只做口服 API（CNC 等級），後來想升級服務注射劑客戶（Grade C），改造成本非常高——需要增加 HEPA、氣閘、壓差監控系統。建議在初始設計階段就預留這些「升級路徑」：預留 HEPA 安裝空間、氣閘位置、管路接口等。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為什麼口服 API 的操作區域只需要「capable of meeting ISO 8 AT REST, but not monitored」？「capable but not monitored」的實務含義是什麼？
  2. 高效 API 的排風為什麼推薦用 HEPA 而非只用洗滌器？從 OEL（職業暴露限值）角度思考。
  3. 如果一個 CDMO 同時生產口服 API 和注射劑 API，兩條產線的 HVAC 系統設計有哪些關鍵差異？
3.6 Biologics（生物製劑）

原文內容 Original Text

Figure 3.3: Biologics Upstream Processing System Schematic (AFD schematic — not available for embedding)

Figure 3.4: Biologics Downstream Processing System Schematic (AFD schematic — not available for embedding)

(Note: The source text for Section 3.6 includes only the figure references for upstream and downstream biologics processing schematics. Detailed design considerations for biologics are covered in subsequent sections of this chapter and in the ISPE Baseline Guide on Biopharmaceutical Manufacturing Facilities.)

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：生物製劑 HVAC 的上下游差異

Biologics（生物製劑）的 HVAC 設計分為兩大段，需求截然不同：

  • Upstream（上游 — 細胞培養/發酵）：
    • 主要在密閉生物反應器中進行
    • 環境需求：CNC 或 Grade D 等級即可
    • 關鍵考量：保護產品免受微生物污染、溫度控制（影響細胞生長）
    • 通常可使用迴風系統（無溶劑風險）
  • Downstream（下游 — 純化/濃縮）：
    • 包括離心、色譜、過濾、濃縮等步驟
    • 環境需求：Grade C 或 D，取決於是否有開放操作
    • 關鍵考量：如果涉及病毒滅活/去除步驟，Pre-viral 和 Post-viral 區域必須物理隔離，使用獨立 AHU

比喻說明 Analogy

生物製劑的上下游就像啤酒廠的「釀造」和「過濾/裝瓶」：

  • 上游（釀造/發酵）：在密閉桶中進行，環境要求較低，但溫度控制很重要
  • 下游（過濾/裝瓶）：產品暴露的機會增加，需要更乾淨的環境
  • 病毒去除步驟 = 啤酒的巴氏殺菌——處理前後的產品必須完全分開，否則「未殺菌的」可能污染「已殺菌的」

重點提示 Key Notes

Pre-viral vs Post-viral 隔離是生物製劑 HVAC 設計中最關鍵的要求之一。這不僅是空氣隔離，而是全面的物理隔離：

  • 獨立的 AHU（不能共用任何風管）
  • 獨立的走廊和人員動線
  • 壓差方向：post-viral 區域壓力 > pre-viral 區域（防止病毒從「髒側」逸出到「乾淨側」）

這個要求直接參照 3.1 節介紹的「分區隔離」原則，但在生物製劑中執行得更為嚴格。

實務應用 Practical Application

對 CDMO 而言，生物製劑設施的 HVAC 投資通常是化學 API 設施的 2-3 倍，主要原因是：(1) 更多分區需要獨立 AHU；(2) 更高的潔淨等級要求；(3) Pre-/Post-viral 隔離的雙套系統。在設施規劃初期就必須確定是否要提供病毒去除/滅活能力——這會根本性地改變 HVAC 佈局和投資規模。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為什麼生物製劑上游培養區通常不需要 HEPA 過濾的高等級潔淨室？密閉反應器在其中扮演什麼角色？
  2. 如果一間 CDMO 想要同時生產 mAb（單株抗體）和 ADC（抗體藥物複合體），HVAC 設計需要額外考量什麼？（提示：ADC 的 payload 通常是高效化合物）
  3. 生物製劑下游純化步驟常使用色譜（chromatography）系統——這些系統是密閉的還是開放的？對 HVAC 等級需求有什麼影響？

本節重點回顧 Section Summary

  • HVAC 隔離三策略：嚴格過濾、全外氣（Once-through）、專用 AHU（Dedicated AHU），層層組合防止交叉污染
  • 溶劑與高效化合物：溶劑開放操作必須 Once-through + LEL 監控；高效化合物需要 LEV + 密閉 + Bag-in/Bag-out；兩者排風都不能回到房間
  • 壓差控制：Pressure Cascade（壓力梯度）是防止交叉污染的核心機制，風門調整需防篡改，分級區域需持續監控
  • 設計簡約原則：每增加一個控制元件都要證明其必要性——「The need for complexity and its added cost should be justified」
  • API 濕段 vs 乾段：濕段重安全（防爆、防溶劑暴露）；乾段重防塵與潔淨等級（取決於產品最終用途：口服 vs 注射）
  • 生物製劑：上游可在較低等級操作（密閉反應器），下游視開放程度提高等級，Pre-/Post-viral 必須物理隔離
  • 外氣預處理：在高濕氣候中可節省 30-50% 冷卻能耗，但前提是室內潛熱負荷低
  • 決策層級：HVAC 設計決策永遠以「無菌保證/產品安全」為最高優先，其次是「人員安全」，最後才是「能效與成本」
ISPE Good Practice Guide: HVAC | p56 - p80

本章學習目標

  • 理解口服固體劑型（OSD）設施中非強效與強效化合物的 HVAC 設計差異，特別是交叉污染控制策略
  • 掌握無菌製程（Aseptic Processing）設施的 HVAC 關鍵設計要素：HEPA 過濾、壓差串聯、單向氣流（Unidirectional Airflow）
  • 了解實驗室（Laboratory）VAV 系統設計、排煙櫃整合策略，以及不同類型實驗室的空氣處理要求
  • 認識包裝/標籤、倉庫、取樣/秤量等輔助區域的 HVAC 基本需求
  • 理解隔離器（Isolator）與 RABS 對背景環境分級的影響，以及製程設備整合（去熱原隧道、凍乾機、壓蓋）的特殊考量
  • 認識醫療器材（Medical Device）製造設施的 HVAC 要求如何隨患者風險等級調整
3.7 Oral Solid Dosage — Non-Potent Compounds（口服固體劑型——非強效化合物）

原文內容 Original Text

In Figure 3.5, return air filters are located at the rooms, which can cause room DP relationships to change with filter loading. For this reason, CVDs are provided on return air to keep airflow constant.

[Figure 3.5: OSD Non-Potent System Schematic (Sample)]

[Figure 3.6: OSD System Schematic with Pretreatment (Sample)]

3.7.1 System Design Considerations

  • Further discussion for Oral Solids Dosage facilities is covered in the ISPE Baseline® Guide on Oral Solid Dosage Forms (Reference 13, Appendix 12).
  • Oral dosage facilities usually do not require manufacturing activities to take place in areas with assigned cleanliness classifications. While many processing rooms are designated CNC and are capable of meeting EU Grade D (ISO9 or ISO8 at rest), they are not normally monitored for particles. Facilities designed in alignment with WHO 937 Annex 2 may be classified by the Owner and periodically monitored for airborne particulate. If aligning with WHO guidance, these facilities may be designed to maintain a DP of 10-15Pa from adjacent, unclassified, spaces.
  • However, process and process support areas require critical parameters to be controlled and maintained to protect the product from contamination, whether from another product in a multi-product facility or from external or personnel contamination. Airlocks or anterooms are suggested to enhance segregation.
  • Cleanliness of open processing areas should be maintained via control of airflow between product handling area or airlock and surrounding spaces.
  • Isolation via a clean airlock (pressure bubble or pressure sink) into the area of highest contamination is strongly recommended. Where solvents are used, this configuration is recommended to prevent migration of flammable vapors to the building.
  • Monitoring and alarming of direction of airflow (through DP, hotwire velocity sensors, air balance, flow tracking, etc.) to surrounding rooms is recommended.
  • AHU filtration – MERV 7 followed by MERV 13/14 filtration is recommended.
  • Final filtration – 95% DOP/PAO efficiency is recommended in exposed Oral Solid Dose and dry bulk (non-Aseptic) product areas, but terminal HEPA filters may be more practical for multiple product facilities. Where terminal HEPA filters are employed for cross-contamination control, 95% pre-filtration can help to maximize terminal filter life.
  • Large facilities may consider preconditioning outside air for distribution to local recirculated AHUs, each dedicated to one product suite.
  • Low RH may be required; desiccant dehumidification is not uncommon.
  • Return or exhaust air grilles may be equipped with easily removable 30% "dust stop" filters. The effect of filter loading on room pressurization or direction of airflow should be considered.
  • Recirculation systems with adequate filtration may be applied in multi-product areas where solvents are not present. Precautions are covered in the ISPE Baseline® Guide on OSD Facilities.
  • Airflow and makeup air delivery should be directed to flow from the operator's breathing zone and the room entrance toward the source of airborne dust.
  • Non-aspirating supply diffusers are recommended to minimize air disturbances, eddies, and re-entrainment of dust.
  • LEV for open operation (i.e., open coating, tableting, and capsule fill) should be designed and engineered according to ACGIH standards.
  • If risk to workers is assessed to be low, recirculation of LEV exhaust within a production room requires HEPA filtration. The ACGIH Industrial Ventilation Manual decision analysis and design criteria should be consulted for guidance on when recirculation is up to standard.
  • Recirculation of LEV exhaust to the AHU or the general building is not acceptable.
  • Multi-product concurrent manufacturing may require dual HEPA filtration (one supply and one return) for recirculation systems.
  • Multi-product concurrent manufacturing typically uses pressure bubble or pressure sink airlocks to avoid contamination of the common corridor.
  • Single-product or multi-product campaign facilities may employ a pressurized (bubble) common corridor as an airlock to the process.
  • Multi-product concurrent manufacturing facilities may be organized in single product suites to employ the pressurized corridor/airlock concept described in this section.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：OSD（口服固體劑型）的 HVAC 特性

OSD (Oral Solid Dosage，口服固體劑型)：指錠劑、膠囊、粉劑等口服固體藥品。HVAC 設計的核心挑戰不是無菌，而是粉塵控制與交叉污染預防。

CNC (Controlled Not Classified，受控非分級)：房間的環境參數（溫濕度、氣流方向）受控管，但不需要正式的潔淨度分級認證。這是 OSD 設施最常見的設計基準。

CVD (Constant Volume Device，定風量裝置)：安裝在回風管路上，確保即使過濾器逐漸阻塞，房間的風量仍維持恆定，從而穩定壓差關係。

比喻說明：OSD 粉塵控制就像廚房抽油煙

想像一個開放式廚房（OSD 製程區）。你不需要手術室等級的潔淨度，但你一定不希望油煙（粉塵）飄到餐廳（走廊）去。解決方案是：

  • 抽油煙機（LEV 局部排氣）：在粉塵源頭直接抽走
  • 門簾/隔間（氣閘/前室）：阻隔廚房與餐廳之間的空氣流通
  • 保持廚房微負壓：讓空氣永遠從餐廳流向廚房，不反向飄散

ISPE 建議的「氣流從操作員呼吸帶流向粉塵源」就是「讓空氣從乾淨處吹向油煙處」的原理。

重點提示：多產品併行 vs 單產品輪換

多產品併行（Concurrent）製造是 CDMO 的商業核心——同時生產不同客戶的產品。但它要求更高規格的 HVAC：

  • 每個產品套間需獨立 AHU 或雙重 HEPA 過濾
  • 走廊必須使用壓力泡（Pressure Bubble）或壓力沉（Pressure Sink）氣閘
  • LEV 排氣絕對不可回流到其他房間

單產品輪換（Campaign）製造較簡單，可用加壓走廊作為共用氣閘，但需要完整的換線清潔驗證。

實務應用：CDMO 的 OSD 設施投資決策

如果你的 CDMO 只做單一客戶的 OSD 產品，CNC 環境 + MERV 13/14 過濾就足夠了。但如果要承接多客戶併行生產（商業價值更高），你需要投資：

  • 獨立產品套間（各有專屬 AHU）
  • 終端 HEPA 過濾（交叉污染防線）
  • 壓力氣閘系統（物理隔離屏障）

額外投資約 30-50%，但產能利用率和營收可能翻倍。這是典型的「商業與彈性 > 短期成本」決策。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為什麼 OSD 設施的 LEV 排氣不可以回流到 AHU？如果回流會產生什麼風險？
  2. 一個同時生產三種不同 OSD 產品的 CDMO 設施，走廊應該使用「壓力泡」還是「壓力沉」氣閘？為什麼？
  3. MERV 7 + MERV 13/14 的兩段式過濾為什麼比直接使用單一 HEPA 過濾更實際？
3.8 Oral Solid Dosage — Potent Compounds（口服固體劑型——強效化合物）

原文內容 Original Text

The typical AFD shown below introduces a number of key concepts that apply to non-potent as well as potent OSD HVAC:

[Figure 3.7: Potent OSD System Schematic (Sample)]

3.8.1 System Design Considerations

Potent OSD facilities should follow the guides above for OSD with the following exceptions:

  • A minimum filtration of ASHRAE 85% (MERV 13 or 14) in the Supply Air if a 100% once-through system.
  • Closed containment is the primary means of airborne contamination control for this class of material. If processes are proven closed, recirculated air should include HEPA filtration. If the process is not proven closed, once-through air or double HEPA filtration should be considered.
  • LEV should be provided at locations where containment is opened for introduction or removal of materials or in conjunction with other technologies, as required.
  • LEV should be used for solvent extraction only where containment is not technically feasible. Where LEV is used with any possibility of duct contamination by solids, HEPA filters should be installed near the room, before the AHU, preferably in a bag-in/bag-out enclosure in the return duct.
  • Isolation via active control of direction of airflow (using DP, hotwire velocity sensor, flow tracking) into the area of highest contamination from surrounding areas is strongly recommended.
  • Audio-visual alert on loss of airflow or containment should be transmitted to the controlled space for personnel safety.
  • Room air locks/anterooms are recommended for powder handling areas to provide a barrier that maintains a positive airflow differential with respect to the corridor and the processing room (this may also serve as a gowning area).
  • Airflow into de-gowning areas should be negative with respect to the corridor and processing area to contain particles shed from clothing.
  • A secondary control against the spread of active materials is direction of airflow within the room. Supply air should be directed to flow across the operator's breathing zone before crossing the source of dust. Wherever possible, supply air should be directed to flow from a location near the room entrance toward the source of dust and finally out low returns mounted on the far wall.
  • A dedicated HVAC system is recommended for the controlled space where product is exposed.
  • It is recommended that air leaving an open-processing room is not recirculated. Main air systems for these rooms should be designed for 100% exhaust, once-through supply. However, most processes are enclosed and recirculation with HEPA filtration may be justified.
  • Recirculation of air from the controlled space into other areas is not recommended.
  • Recirculation of local exhaust (LEV) from equipment back to the room is not recommended.
  • Filtration of exhaust from potent dry product handling areas and LEV through HEPA filters, scrubbers, or other equivalent treatment methods prior to release outdoors may be required.
  • Where containment equipment is provided and PPE is not required, HEPA filters can protect the AHU and facility in case of an accidental release. These may be a room-accessible type with PPE used for change-out, if needed.
  • Exhaust/return filters should be located as near to processing area as possible to reduce the length of potentially contaminated air ducts. Control dampers may be needed to offset the rise in room pressure due to dirty exhaust/return filters.
  • Exhaust/return HEPA filters not located within a room or where high levels of airborne powders are expected should be safe-change type with Bag-In/Bag-Out housing and bubble tight dampers.
  • Terminal HEPA supply filters can protect against backflow to ductwork if product containment should fail because of AHU failure.
  • An annual testing and preventive maintenance program to ensure the integrity of HEPA filtration systems is suggested. Filters on processes requiring PPE should be tested more frequently.
  • Appropriate monitoring and interlocking of HVAC with process equipment should be considered to maintain containment integrity and to control cross contamination and emissions. Performance of isolator protection (DP) should be monitored.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：強效化合物的 HVAC 設計原則

Potent Compound（強效化合物）：指在極低劑量即產生藥理作用的藥物活性成分，如荷爾蒙、細胞毒性藥物等。OEL（Occupational Exposure Limit，職業暴露限值）通常 <10 μg/m³。

HVAC 設計從「保護產品」升級為同時保護產品與操作人員。核心策略轉變：

  • 首選：密閉容器（Closed Containment）——從源頭杜絕暴露
  • 次選：100% 直排（Once-through）——不讓污染空氣有任何回流機會
  • 底線：雙重 HEPA 過濾——即使空氣回流，也確保安全

Bag-In/Bag-Out (BIBO) 過濾器：一種安全更換型 HEPA 過濾器外殼，讓維護人員在不接觸濾芯表面的情況下完成更換，避免二次暴露。

比喻說明：強效化合物處理如同核電廠的放射性物質管控

強效化合物的 HVAC 設計邏輯跟核電廠管理放射性物質很類似：

  • 密閉操作（Closed Containment）= 密封反應爐——最好永遠不要打開
  • 負壓控制（Pressure Sink）= 放射性區域永遠維持負壓——萬一有洩漏，空氣只會往裡流
  • BIBO 過濾器 = 更換放射性廢棄物時使用的密封袋——保護維護人員
  • 聲光警報 = 輻射洩漏警報——立即通知人員撤離或穿戴防護

重點提示：穿脫衣區的壓差設計常被忽略

本節有一個常被忽略但非常關鍵的設計要求：脫衣區（De-gowning Area）必須維持負壓。

為什麼？操作人員離開製程區時，衣物上附著的強效化合物粉塵會在脫衣過程中釋放。如果脫衣區是正壓，這些粉塵會飄向走廊，造成人員二次暴露。

壓差設計應為：走廊（正壓）→ 穿衣區（中壓）→ 製程區（負壓）→ 脫衣區（最負壓）

氣流方向設計原則

【房間內氣流方向】
    供氣口（靠近入口/操作人員呼吸帶）
         ↓  乾淨空氣向下吹
    操作人員呼吸帶
         ↓  跨過
    粉塵產生源
         ↓  帶走粉塵
    低位回風口（遠端牆壁底部）

原則：人 → 塵 → 排，永遠保護操作人員！

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 如果一個強效化合物的製程已被驗證為「密閉」（proven closed），是否還需要 100% 直排系統？根據本節內容，可以採取什麼替代方案？
  2. 為什麼 HEPA 過濾器要安裝在「盡可能靠近製程區」的位置？如果安裝在遠端的 AHU 機組裡有什麼風險？
3.9 Aseptic Processing Facility（無菌製程設施）

原文內容 Original Text

[Figure 3.8: Aseptic Processing System Schematic (Sample)]

3.9.1 System Design Considerations

  • Considerable background on product requirements and the design of HVAC systems is covered in the ISPE Baseline® Guide for Sterile Manufacturing Facilities.
  • The impact of closed barrier devices and open isolators (such as RABS) is covered in the ISPE Baseline® Guide on Sterile Manufacturing Facilities and Chapter 3 of this Guide.
  • Area classification requirements for bioburden-controlled processing (biotech) are covered in the ISPE Baseline® Guide on Biopharmaceutical Manufacturing Facilities.
  • Closed wash equipment may not require room exhaust. Room pressure control may be needed if the exhaust fan can be turned on and off.
  • Revisions to EU GMP Annex 1 which went into effect in 2009 suggest that capping be accomplished under Grade A conditions or if outside the aseptic space, under Grade A "airflow." Some may interpret this as meaning that capping equipment should be in a Grade 7 (EU Grade B) room under Grade A hood. The ISPE Baseline® Guide recommends that "Grade A airflow" is provided by a unidirectional airflow module, which provides HEPA filtered air directly to the uncapped vials until the crimp is complete.
  • EU Annex 1 requires exhaust from the crimping operation. This should be located as near as possible to the crimping head(s) to minimize aluminum particulate in the capper. Contamination from the air conditioning supply system should be eliminated with properly installed and integrity tested ceiling mounted terminal HEPA filters for Grade 7/8 (EU Grade B/C).
  • AHU filters – MERV 7 followed by MERV 13/14. A HEPA filter in the AHU should extend the life of the terminal HEPA filters. Draw-through AHU is acceptable if the final filter is after the fan and cooling condensate trap has sufficient height.
  • RH is normally controlled by condensing cooling or by humidification. Desiccant systems are often used for dew points below 40°F (5°C).
  • Outside air may be pre-conditioned and distributed from a central AHU, because of high airflow (air change) requirements.
  • Infiltration of contamination from uncontrolled spaces should be minimized by the use of room pressure differentials and airlocks between air classes.
  • Continuous room pressure monitoring with alarms and recording devices that indicate out of specification conditions are recommended. Rooms with stringent environmental parameters or where the product is exposed to the environment may require continuous monitoring.
  • Consider automatic pressure controls to keep the spaces within specified pressure limits where process exhaust volumes can change or where doors and hatches are frequently opened or door seal integrity changes over time.
  • Desiccant dehumidification with post cooling coils should be considered for low humidity room control. Low humidity and desiccant dehumidification usually are not needed, as most products are liquid.
  • A dedicated air handling system is recommended to serve only the aseptic areas and to remain operational to maintain pressure control when other building systems are shut down during unoccupied periods.
  • HVAC systems for classified spaces should operate 24 hours/day, 7 days/week. After system shutdown or setback, a protocol for returning a room to proper operating condition should be developed with QA. This does not remove the possibility of reduced airflow during idle periods.
  • Ductwork should be designed per SMACNA standards and should be constructed for 4 inch or greater water gauge duct static pressure and SMACNA seal Class "A."
  • Ductwork should be galvanized steel except where exposed in production areas or subject to moisture, in which case it should be a minimum 304SS stainless with cleanable finish.
  • Air to an aseptic area should be supplied through ceiling mounted terminal HEPA filters. These terminal HEPA filters become part of the aseptic boundary and protect the room from outside contamination. The use of only remote bank mounted HEPA filters in the supply duct is not recommended. Access ports to introduce and monitor PAO (test aerosol) challenge materials upstream on the non-aseptic side of the HEPA diffusers are suggested for filter integrity testing.
  • Air supplied through ceiling mounted terminal HEPA filters should be returned at floor level through multiple return duct drops. Return air in the air handling unit should be filtered through MERV 7 pleated and MERV 13 or 14 bag filters to extend HEPA filter life. Recirculation HEPA/fan units mounted below the ceiling as terminal HEPA units are not recommended, as they require service within the aseptic area and do not normally use low returns.
  • The return air openings in the aseptic area should be located near the floor, preferably on at least two (2) walls and along the long dimensions of a room to ensure maximum uniformity of airflow. More return openings are better than too few. Equipment and furniture should not block return openings.
  • Differential air pressure is needed to minimize infiltration of contaminants from outside the controlled space. The aseptic area should be designed for a positive pressure with all doors closed in relation to less clean adjacent areas outside the controlled space. Gowning areas are treated as airlocks with supply and return air, and are maintained at a negative pressure relative to the controlled aseptic area and at a positive pressure relative to the outside and uncontrolled spaces. DPs are measured across airlocks.
  • Each area should have an air supply and return with dampers to permit proper balancing. The room with exposed product is to be maintained most positive; while anterooms leading to this room are to be maintained successively less positive down to the zero reference level of uncontrolled spaces. Where potent product is open-filled and may become airborne, a high-pressure containment airlock that meets the filling room air grade may have pressure higher than the aseptic filling room. A control range should be established for each room pressure level.
  • The manual/remote gauges and controls or automatic controls should be mounted in a common panel outside the controlled space. An audible alarm may be provided to indicate loss of area pressure control.
  • Unidirectional airflow serves as a barrier between product and microbial and particulate contamination generated by the equipment and personnel within an aseptic area. Where components and equipment are not protected by unidirectional airflow, terminal HEPA filters should be located directly over the exposed product.
  • When the central system air conditioning air quantity required to maintain room conditions is not sufficient to provide adequate air changes for recovery or protection over the product, a supplemental ("local") HEPA filtered air recirculating system may be employed. The cooler central system conditioned air may be distributed into the local recirculating AHU or UFH (preferably at the fan inlet) to maintain room temperature. The heat generated from the local recirculating system fan motor should be considered.
  • Airflow patterns within the workspace (inside the UFH) should be uniform with minimum turbulence. Ambient air may not aspirate into the work areas along the perimeter of the unidirectional airflow barrier. The hood filter area should deliver ISO 5 air at a target velocity of 90 feet per minute (0.45 m/sec) with uniformity within plus or minus 20%, measured just below the filter face (6 to 12 inches, 15 to 30 cm). Velocity at the work height also should be measured although it may measure close to zero if it is far from the filter face. The optimal filter face velocity should be determined during qualification of the UFH using airflow visualization ("smoke testing").
  • Room temperatures should be controlled by maintaining constant airflow through a cooling/dehumidification coil, and possibly modulating a heating coil for temperature control. Systems that vary cool airflow to control room temperatures are not recommended because of adverse effects on room pressures.
  • The HVAC system may be required to quickly return room conditions after sanitization.
  • Since a Grade 5 (Grade A) room requires a very large treated airflow to create the unidirectional condition, air from the Grade 5 room is normally recirculated rather than exhausted with some excess supply air to create room DP. A Grade 5 room would be unusual; the Grade 5 area is usually a UFH or RABS inside a Grade 7 (Grade B) room.
  • Dampers on return air may be modulated to maintain room pressure, while CV units on supply air help maintain constant flow to the room.
  • A desiccant dehumidifier may be required for aseptic liquid filling facilities.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：無菌製程 HVAC 的三大設計支柱

無菌製程（Aseptic Processing，無菌製程）的 HVAC 是整本 ISPE HVAC 指引中最關鍵、最複雜的應用場景。三大設計支柱：

  • 終端 HEPA 過濾：天花板安裝的 HEPA 過濾器是無菌屏障（Aseptic Boundary）的一部分——不是可選項，是必要條件
  • 壓差串聯（Pressure Cascade）：從最潔淨區（Grade A）逐級遞減到一般建築環境，形成「空氣只從乾淨處流向不潔淨處」的物理屏障
  • 單向氣流（Unidirectional Airflow，單向氣流）：在 Grade A 區域提供 0.45 m/s 的均勻向下氣流，將微粒和微生物「沖刷」離開暴露產品

重點提示：24/7 運行是法規剛性需求

本節明確指出無菌區的 HVAC 必須 24 小時、7 天運行。為什麼？

  • 停機後壓差立即消失，未受控空氣可能滲入無菌區
  • 重新啟動後需要執行「恢復協議」（Recovery Protocol），與 QA 共同確認房間已回到規格範圍內
  • 可以在非生產時段降低風量（Reduced Airflow），但不可以完全停機

這意味著無菌設施的 HVAC 能源成本是其他區域的 3-5 倍——這是影響 CDMO 運營成本的重要因素。

比喻說明：壓差串聯如同水壩的多級閘門

無菌製程的壓差設計就像水壩的多級閘門系統：

  • Grade A（最高水位）：充填區，壓力最高，水（空氣）只會往外流
  • Grade B（次高水位）：背景環境，接住 Grade A 外溢的水
  • 穿衣區/氣閘（中間水位）：過渡閘門，防止外界水倒灌
  • 一般建築（最低水位）：所有的水最終流到這裡

任何時候如果閘門漏水（壓差失控），上游的潔淨水就可能被下游的污水回灌——這就是為什麼需要連續監控和警報。

UFH 單向氣流關鍵參數

目標風速：0.45 m/s (90 fpm)
均勻度要求：± 20%
量測位置：HEPA 過濾器面下方 15-30 cm（6-12 inches）
確效方法：氣流可視化測試（Smoke Testing）

Grade A 區域 = ISO 5
  ≤ 3,520 particles/m³ (≥0.5μm)
Grade B 區域 = ISO 7 (at rest)
  ≤ 352,000 particles/m³ (≥0.5μm)

重點提示：風管材質與施工標準

本節特別規範：

  • 一般風管：鍍鋅鋼板，按 SMACNA 標準施工，最低 4 英寸水柱靜壓等級
  • 暴露在生產區域或潮濕環境的風管：最低 304 不鏽鋼，可清洗表面
  • 密封等級：SMACNA Class "A"（最高等級）

這些要求直接影響工程造價。不鏽鋼風管的成本是鍍鋅鋼板的 3-5 倍，但在無菌區是必要投資。

實務應用：CDMO 無菌設施的 HVAC 架構選擇

設計無菌充填線的 HVAC 時，你面對兩個基本架構選擇：

  • Grade A UFH + Grade B 背景房（傳統方案）：UFH 提供 Grade A 單向氣流，背景環境維持 Grade B。適用於 RABS（限制進出屏障系統）或開放式充填線
  • 隔離器（Isolator，隔離器）+ 降級背景環境（進階方案）：密閉隔離器內部 Grade A，背景環境可降至 Grade C 甚至 Grade D。HVAC 需求大幅降低，但隔離器本身投資較高

本節稍後 3.15.4 將詳細討論隔離器系統——它是「綠色技術」（Green Technology），能源消耗顯著低於傳統製程。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為什麼 ISPE 不建議在無菌區內使用天花板下方安裝的循環 HEPA/風扇單元？（提示：思考維護和氣流方向）
  2. 無菌區的低位回風口建議設置在至少兩面牆上，這是為什麼？如果只設在一面牆上會有什麼問題？
  3. 如果你的無菌充填線同時處理強效產品（Potent Product），壓差設計會變成什麼樣？（提示：保護產品的正壓 vs 保護人員的負壓之間的衝突）
3.9.2 Aseptic Powder Potent Compounds（無菌粉末——強效化合物）

原文內容 Original Text

[Figure 3.9: Aseptic Potent Powder Processing System Schematic with Pretreatment (Sample)]

  • Processes should be contained in isolators with dedicated HVAC for the containment enclosure.
  • Where the process leaks into a room, protect the HVAC system and other rooms on the system from hazardous compounds by using non-recirculating primary air conditioning systems.
  • The exhaust or return air ducts may be kept clean with HEPA filters protected from physical damage with a pre-filter or equivalent. These filters should be located within the room where properly gowned and protected personnel can service them. Frequent filter change out or active room pressure controllers may be needed to offset loading of return air filters.
  • If return filters are located remote from the room where open processing occurs, they should be housed in a high containment bag-in/bag-out filter housing and identified as such. These filters contain the potentially hazardous compounds and minimize particulate "fall back" during fan failure.
  • Gowning areas should be supplied with HEPA filtered air and maintained at a negative pressure relative to the controlled aseptic area and at a positive pressure relative to the uncontrolled spaces. Local regulations may prefer two-stage gowning. The gowning area should be separated from the Grade 7 (EU Grade B) aseptic filling room by a high pressure Grade 7 (Grade B) airlock.
  • The de-gowning area should be separated from the aseptic filling room by a low-pressure airlock. The de-gowning room shall be maintained negative relative to adjacent spaces on the uncontrolled side.
  • Material entering the aseptic filling room should be transferred via a HEPA filtered, high-pressure tunnel, box, or sterilizer. Contaminated material leaving the aseptic filling room should be transferred via a low-pressure tunnel or box.
  • If aseptic product is a powder, very low RH may be required. Minimize leakage into the AHU after the dehumidification step (blow-through AHU is preferred).

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：無菌 + 強效 = 最高等級 HVAC 挑戰

這是 HVAC 設計中最具挑戰性的場景：同時要求無菌保證（Sterility Assurance，保護產品）和人員防護（Containment，保護操作員）。兩個需求在壓差方向上是矛盾的：

  • 無菌要求：充填區正壓 → 空氣向外流（保護產品不被污染）
  • 密閉要求：操作區負壓 → 空氣向內流（防止毒性粉塵外洩）

解決方案：使用隔離器（Isolator）+ 專屬 HVAC，讓隔離器內部保持無菌正壓，而隔離器周圍的房間可以維持負壓。

比喻說明：進出動線如同太空站的氣閘艙

無菌強效製程的人員與物料進出設計就像太空站的氣閘系統：

  • 穿衣區（高壓氣閘）= 太空人穿上太空衣的準備室——從「地球」（走廊）過渡到「太空」（無菌區）
  • 脫衣區（低壓氣閘）= 太空人回來時的減壓艙——確保太空（製程區）的「毒物」不會帶回地球
  • 物料進入隧道（高壓 HEPA 過濾）= 貨物進入太空站的加壓通道
  • 廢棄物移出（低壓隧道）= 垃圾彈射艙——只出不進

重點提示：乾粉無菌充填的特殊濕度需求

無菌粉末產品（如凍乾粉注射劑）需要極低的相對濕度（RH），否則粉末會吸濕結塊。ISPE 建議：

  • 使用 Blow-through AHU（鼓風式空調機組）——風扇在冷卻盤管之前，減少除濕後的管路漏濕
  • 這與液態無菌充填的 Draw-through AHU（抽風式）設計完全相反

如果你的 CDMO 同時承接液態和粉末無菌產品，HVAC 系統必須有兩種不同的配置能力——這是設施彈性的重要考量。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為什麼隔離器是解決「無菌 vs 密閉」壓差矛盾的最佳方案？如果不使用隔離器，你如何設計壓差系統？
  2. 物料進入無菌充填室使用「高壓隧道」，離開時使用「低壓隧道」——請解釋壓差設計背後的邏輯。
3.10 Packaging/Labeling（包裝/標籤）

原文內容 Original Text

[Figure 3.10: Packaging and Labeling System Schematic]

3.10.1 System Design Considerations

  • Primary packaging for sterile products is covered in the ISPE Baseline® Guide on Sterile Manufacturing Facilities.
  • Primary packaging areas should have the same area requirements as that used for manufacturing. Handling of product exposure and potent compound issues should be addressed. Room pressure or airflow direction may be required.
  • Secondary packaging (e.g., sealed bottles into cartons) is usually CNC with worker comfort conditions. Tight humidity control may be needed for specific labeling operations. Room pressure control usually is not required.
  • Some raw material warehouses in temperate climates need meet only USP storage requirements, sometimes with only supplemental heat in winter provided by steam, gas, or electric unit heaters.
  • As materials, drug substances, and drug products usually are stored closed and isolated from the room environment, humidity control may not be required.
  • Draw-through AHU is considered acceptable with MERV 7 and MERV 13 filtration. Terminal filtration usually is not required.
  • The use of more elaborate HVAC normally would be driven by business needs, where higher HVAC life cycle costs can be offset by the risk of loss of expensive product.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：初級包裝 vs 二級包裝的 HVAC 差異

Primary Packaging（初級包裝）：直接接觸產品的容器（如藥瓶、注射器），HVAC 要求等同製造區。

Secondary Packaging（二級包裝）：將已密封的產品放入紙盒/箱子，通常只需 CNC 環境 + 人員舒適條件。

關鍵決策原則：HVAC 需求跟隨「產品暴露風險」——產品還是暴露的？用製造區標準。產品已密封？舒適條件即可。

比喻說明：包裝就像食品裝盒

壽司師傅把壽司放進餐盒（Primary Packaging）——必須在乾淨的廚房裡完成。但把餐盒放進外帶袋（Secondary Packaging）——在櫃檯就可以了，不需要回到廚房。HVAC 設計的邏輯一模一樣。

重點提示：成本效益導向的 HVAC 升級

本節最後一點特別有商業智慧：「更精細的 HVAC 通常由商業需求驅動，當較高的 HVAC 生命週期成本可以被昂貴產品損失的風險所抵消時。」

換句話說：存放便宜的原料？最低限度的 HVAC 即可。存放每批價值百萬美元的生物製劑？值得投資更好的溫濕度控制。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 如果你的 CDMO 的包裝區同時處理強效化合物產品的初級包裝，HVAC 設計需要增加哪些額外要求？
  2. 標籤作業為什麼可能需要嚴格的濕度控制？（提示：想想標籤黏膠和印刷品質）
3.11 Laboratories（實驗室）

原文內容 Original Text

[Figure 3.11: Laboratory]

[Figure 3.12: Typical Laboratory Room HVAC Detail (with Pneumatic Actuators)]

3.11.2 System Design Considerations

  • There are four exhaust ducts shown for the laboratory, two of which service fume hoods. One services analytical equipment through flexible drops and the other keeps the laboratory at negative pressure in relation to the corridor or other adjacent spaces when fume hood airflow is low or when room cooling requirements require additional supply air. Laboratory facilities may serve these ducts with individual fans or with a single exhaust plenum held at constant pressure and serviced by one or two larger exhaust fans.
  • Laboratories using volatile solvents or radioisotopes should be negative (commonly via airflow tracking) relative to corridors, offices, and adjacent occupied space. Air from offices or technical spaces adjacent to laboratories should transfer into the laboratory. Classified clean laboratory spaces should be positive (via airflow tracking) relative to corridors, offices, and adjacent occupied space. A high pressure bubble airlock should be provided where activities in positively pressurized spaces pose a threat to corridor air quality.
  • Where chemicals or other hazardous materials are handled in the open, air systems should be 100% exhaust. Risks of recirculation of laboratory air should be evaluated if energy costs become prohibitive. Glove boxes to reduce dilution volumes and total airflow may be justified.
  • Recirculation of air in microbial and in-process or materials testing laboratories that do not employ volatile organic solvents may be considered. A ductless laboratory hood may be justifiable. These hoods recirculate air to the room through activated carbon filters that remove vapor contamination. Maintenance is critical to the safety of the user.
  • VAV control systems are recommended for increased safety through monitoring capabilities and decreased energy usage (using hood diversity and variable flow). Airflow tracking (fixed difference between supply and exhaust) is common.
  • Occupancy sensors and night setback can enhance the energy saving potential of VAV systems. Minimum ventilation rates of 8 to 12 are recommended for most laboratories. Minimum ventilation rates below 6 AC/hr for occupied laboratories are not recommended.
  • Non-aspirating type diffusers are recommended to be selected and located to minimize velocity and turbulence near the hood face; design cross drafts should not exceed 30 FPM within 24 inches of the hood opening.
  • Galvanized exhaust ducts, boxes, and attenuators may be used except where process or research activity requires special corrosion resistance. Laboratory hood exhaust ducts and accessories that are inaccessible should be stainless steel (304). Laboratory hood exhaust ducts which handle large quantities of acids should be high grade stainless steel, Hastelloy, FRP, or other suitable material.
  • Perchloric acid digestion hood exhaust requires special handling and cleaning systems. These systems represent an explosion hazard, should be segregated, and designed by experienced professionals.
  • Stack height above a building roof should be maintained at a 10 ft minimum; although a stack height equal to 30% of the building height is preferred. Stacks should be located to avoid re-entrainment of air into HVAC systems.
  • Automatic hood closing systems with obstruction detection should be considered for energy savings. VAV boxes should not be oversized.
  • VAV systems should be sized with a diversity factor to allow for savings in airflow and first cost. A factor of 70% of installed load is common.
  • A general room exhaust should be provided only when the hood flow at minimum sash position requires an air supply rate less than that required to satisfy heat loads or the specified minimum air change rate.
  • Air change rate or exhaust quantity will usually dictate the supply air quantity. Exhaust quantities should be reset upward when additional cooling is required.
  • Mounting VAV laboratory controls in accessible panels either flush in alcove outside laboratory or in dedicated rooms should improve ease of maintenance.
  • Manifolded exhaust systems are considered acceptable, except for perchloric acid hoods which should be on a dedicated exhaust system.
  • Approved exhausted chemical storage cabinets should be considered for solvents and hazardous materials.
  • Heat should be recovered from laboratory utility equipment, wherever possible.
  • Temperature alarms should be provided on refrigerators or freezers. Where critical, these should be connected to the BAS.
  • Where laboratory offices are on the exterior wall, heating at the perimeter wall is recommended.
  • Central draw-through air handlers are common. Distributed AHUs may be justified for areas that are frequently shut down.
  • Supply Air Filtration – MERV 7 and MERV 13/14 (in series). If required by product, HEPA may be needed for classified rooms.
  • Exhaust Air Filtration – As required by application. Where energy recovery is employed MERV 7 filters are required. Scrubbers may be required for some dedicated hoods. HEPA filtration may be required for formulation laboratories, BSL3&4, etc.
  • In-duct silencers can help decrease noise from exhaust manifold valves. Packless type silencers can be used for chemical exhaust applications.
  • Biosafety laboratories are outside the scope of this Guide.

3.11.3 Vivarium

Vivarium facilities should consist of individual suites, each capable of maintaining its own microenvironment for the duration of the product study. Guidances are published by AAALAC, ASHRAE, and others.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：實驗室 HVAC 的核心挑戰——排煙櫃整合

Fume Hood（排煙櫃）：實驗室的核心安全設備，透過向內的氣流防止化學蒸氣逸出。排煙櫃的排氣需求直接決定了整個實驗室 HVAC 的設計。

VAV (Variable Air Volume，變風量) 系統：根據排煙櫃的實際使用狀態自動調節風量的智慧控制系統。核心優勢：

  • 安全性：透過氣流追蹤（Airflow Tracking）確保房間壓差始終符合要求
  • 節能性：只有在排煙櫃打開時才提供全風量，其餘時間降低風量
  • Diversity Factor（多樣性因子）：不是所有排煙櫃會同時全開，因此系統可按 70% 容量設計中央設備

比喻說明：實驗室壓差設計的「兩種人格」

實驗室的壓差設計有兩種完全相反的邏輯，像是同一棟建築裡住著兩種性格的人：

  • 化學實驗室（「向內吸」型）：使用揮發性溶劑或放射性同位素——維持負壓，讓有害蒸氣不會飄到走廊（保護外部人員）
  • 潔淨實驗室（「向外推」型）：微生物檢測或製程中控制——維持正壓，讓走廊的污染不會進入實驗室（保護測試樣品）

如果你的 QC 實驗室同時有化學分析和微生物檢測，就必須做好分區設計，讓這兩種「人格」互不干擾。

重點提示：排煙櫃前方的氣流干擾

一個經常被忽略的設計細節：排煙櫃開口前方 24 英寸（60 cm）範圍內的橫向氣流不得超過 30 FPM（0.15 m/s）。

為什麼？如果供氣出風口產生的氣流經過排煙櫃前方，會擾亂排煙櫃的向內氣流，導致有害蒸氣逸出。這就是為什麼 ISPE 推薦非吸入式出風口（Non-aspirating Diffusers）——它們產生的氣流更柔和、更均勻。

實驗室 HVAC 設計參數速查

最低換氣次數（已佔用實驗室）：6 AC/hr
建議換氣次數：8-12 AC/hr
VAV 多樣性因子：70%（常用值）
排煙櫃面前橫向風速限制：≤ 30 FPM (0.15 m/s)
排氣煙囪最低高度：10 ft (3 m) 或建築高度的 30%
過高酸排氣管：304SS 或 Hastelloy/FRP
夜間模式：佔用感測器 + 降載（Night Setback）

實務應用：CDMO QC 實驗室的 HVAC 投資優化

對 CDMO 而言，QC 實驗室是必要但非營收直接產生的區域。HVAC 優化策略：

  • VAV 系統：初期投資較高，但能源節省 30-50%，2-3 年即可回收
  • 佔用感測器 + 夜間降載：QC 實驗室通常只有日班使用，夜間降低風量可節省大量能源
  • 排煙櫃自動關閉系統：防止人員離開後忘記降下窗框，避免不必要的排氣
  • 熱回收：從排煙櫃排出的空氣中回收熱量（但需注意化學品相容性）

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為什麼過氯酸（Perchloric Acid）的排煙櫃必須使用專用排氣系統，不能與其他排煙櫃共用？
  2. 一個 CDMO 的 QC 實驗室有 12 個排煙櫃，如果使用 70% 多樣性因子，中央排氣設備應按多少個排煙櫃的容量設計？
  3. 無管道型實驗室排煙櫃（Ductless Laboratory Hood）為什麼「不常見」且維護至關重要？活性碳飽和後會發生什麼？
3.12 Sampling/Dispensing（取樣/秤量）

原文內容 Original Text

[Figure 3.13: Sampling and Dispensing]

3.12.1 System Design Considerations

  • Specific product requirements are in the appropriate ISPE Baseline® Guide.
  • Once-through air may be applied for solvent use. Recirculated room air is possible with adequate air filtration. Exhaust air for sampling stations should not be recirculated.
  • Central Filtration should be minimum MERV 7 followed by MERV 13/14. Starting materials for aseptic processing may require HEPA filtration to meet Grade C or D.
  • Airflow Tracking or room pressure control are recommended for room segregation.
  • Powder sampling/dispensing may require low RH.
  • Airlocking between warehousing and sampling spaces is expected.
  • The area classification and environmental conditions in sampling and dispensing should reflect the conditions used when charging materials to the process.
  • Unidirectional Flow modules are, typically, employed as local protection for sampling activities, this gives greater flexibility when multiple materials are sampled or dispensed in a given area.
  • LEV should be considered for sampling or dispensing of hazardous materials. Exhaust hoods designed for this purpose are commercially available.

3.12.2 Downflow Booth

[Figure 3.14: Uni-Directional Flow Booth for Local Protection]

The downflow booth is a "packaged" HVAC system integrated into a booth that has sidewalls, a ceiling plenum, and a low level return inside the booth at the front. The unit is designed to provide operator protection when handling hazardous materials.

The design concepts are shown in Figure 3.14. The discharge HEPA filter (usually a safe change unit) may be mounted in the ductwork from the fan to the ceiling plenum with the ceiling a proprietary material designed to provide uniform laminar downflow of air or the booth ceiling can be made up of HEPA filters. By introducing a material with a high pressure drop, the system creates a uniform high velocity downward flow from the ceiling.

The high air change rate means that heat builds up in the booth from the fan energy; therefore, the system usually has a cooling coil mounted in the airstream controlled to maintain the temperature in the booth.

A small portion of air (usually around 10%) is bled from the plenum in order to create an inward flow of air in the front of the booth to provide containment; usually the systems have vinyl curtains mounted on the front to encourage the inflow at a low level.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：取樣/秤量區的「雙重身份」

取樣和秤量區是倉庫與製程之間的橋樑。HVAC 設計必須反映「下游製程」的要求：

  • 如果材料進入無菌製程：取樣區可能需要 Grade C 或 D 環境
  • 如果材料進入 OSD 製程：CNC 環境通常足夠
  • 如果處理危險材料：需要 LEV 和操作員防護

Downflow Booth（下吹式工作台）：一種整合型 HVAC 設備，從天花板向下吹出均勻的 HEPA 過濾空氣，在工作台前方利用 10% 的排氣量產生向內的氣流，形成「看不見的空氣門」保護操作人員。

比喻說明：下吹式工作台就像「空氣瀑布」

想像站在一道小瀑布後面：水從上方均勻落下（HEPA 過濾空氣），你站在裡面（操作區），粉塵無法穿過水幕到達你的呼吸帶。瀑布底部有排水口（低位回風），帶走所有被水沖下的雜質。前方的水簾（乙烯基帘幕 + 10% 排氣）阻止外部的風吹散水幕。

重點提示：取樣區與倉庫之間必須設氣閘

ISPE 明確要求倉庫和取樣空間之間必須使用氣閘（Airlock）分隔。原因：

  • 倉庫通常是非受控環境（CNC 或更低）
  • 取樣區的潔淨度必須匹配下游製程要求
  • 打開容器取樣時，產品暴露在環境中——此時環境條件直接影響產品品質

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為什麼 ISPE 說取樣站的排氣「不應回流」，但房間一般空氣可以回流？兩者的風險差異是什麼？
  2. 一個同時服務 OSD 和無菌產品線的 CDMO，取樣區應該按什麼等級設計？如何平衡成本與合規？
3.13 – 3.14 Administrative, General Building & Warehouse（行政區、一般建築與倉庫）

原文內容 Original Text

[Figure 3.15: Admin Area System Schematic (Sample)]

3.13.1 System Design Considerations

  • See general design considerations in Section 3.2.
  • Most office areas use VAV HVAC, which is more energy efficient, but may, by its nature, create variable room pressures inside the office. If the administrative area borders on a pressure controlled processing area or the area of common pressure reference, there may be a disturbance of pressures inside the processing area. However, careful HVAC design can solve this problem.

3.14 Warehouse

3.14.1 System Design Considerations

  • Many warehouse facilities do not have central air handling, using only unit heaters. Be sure that their very warm air outlets do not overheat high-stacked material.
  • See product data or the USP or equivalent pharmacopoeia for finished product temperature and RH requirements. The USP defines a limit for "controlled room temperature storage" of 15 to 30°C (59 to 86°F) with a Mean Kinetic Temperature (MKT) below 25°C (77°F).
  • Biological products may require lower temperatures than the USP specifies and may require a "cold chain" or series of cold storage and handling areas to meet stability criteria.
  • The warehouse should be "temperature mapped" to identify normally "hot" and "cold" locations and define the relationship between these locations and the measured temperature at the control sensors.
  • Mapping of temperature extremes in high bay warehouses is recommended.
  • Dedicated exhaust may be required for battery charging areas to remove hydrogen gas emitted by charging lead/acid batteries.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：倉庫 HVAC 的關鍵——溫度分佈圖（Temperature Mapping）

Temperature Mapping（溫度分佈圖）：在倉庫的多個位置安裝溫度感測器，連續記錄至少一年的溫度數據（覆蓋所有季節），以識別「熱點」和「冷點」。

Mean Kinetic Temperature (MKT，平均動力學溫度)：一個加權平均溫度，反映溫度波動對產品穩定性的實際影響。USP 要求 MKT < 25°C。

這意味著即使偶爾超過 30°C，只要 MKT 控制在 25°C 以下，仍然合規——但你必須有數據證明。

比喻說明：溫度分佈圖就像「倉庫的健康檢查報告」

就像人體需要定期健康檢查，了解哪些器官有潛在風險一樣，倉庫也需要「體檢」來了解哪些區域的溫度容易偏高或偏低。靠近天花板的高架區就像「容易發燒的額頭」，靠近大門的進出口就像「容易著涼的鼻子」。

重點提示：行政區 VAV 系統對製程區壓差的干擾

這是一個被低估的設計風險：辦公室的 VAV 系統會根據佔用人數和熱負荷自動調節風量，造成辦公區的壓力波動。如果辦公區緊鄰受控製程區，這些壓力波動可能干擾製程區的壓差控制。

解決方案：在辦公區與製程區之間設置緩衝走廊或氣閘，或使用定風量（CAV）系統供應辦公區中鄰近製程區的區域。

重點提示：生物製劑的冷鏈（Cold Chain）需求

生物製劑（如單株抗體、mRNA 疫苗）通常需要 2-8°C 甚至 -20°C 或 -80°C 的儲存條件。這遠超過 USP 的「受控室溫」標準，需要：

  • 專用冷藏/冷凍庫
  • 連續溫度監控 + 警報系統
  • 備用電力供應（不可中斷）
  • 冷鏈驗證文件

對 CDMO 而言，提供完整冷鏈能力是承接高價值生物製劑合同的先決條件。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的倉庫在夏季最熱的一天，某個區域溫度達到 35°C 持續 4 小時，但年度 MKT 仍為 24°C。這個區域是否仍符合 USP「受控室溫」要求？你需要什麼文件來支持這個結論？
  2. 為什麼電池充電區需要專用排氣？如果不設排氣會有什麼安全風險？
3.15 Process Equipment Integration（製程設備整合）

原文內容 Original Text

There are specific requirements for process equipment. Specific aspects for the area containing process equipment should be considered in the design of HVAC systems.

3.15.1 Dust Extract Systems

Where there is a common dust extract system, aspects to consider in the HVAC system design include:

  • What happens if the unit fails? Does the unit have a damper that closes, preventing air leaving the system? Is the pressure difference between the rooms served by the system adequate to obtain flow with a consequential risk of cross contamination?
  • How does the dust collector clean itself? Some units are cleaned by a shaker mechanism; others use a pulse of compressed air. During this pulse, which is in the opposite direction to the normal airflow, the extract air flow can halt or even reverse for a short period, is this acceptable?
  • One of the advantages of a remote system is that the system heat gain is outside the room and the extract normally is located near an area where the equipment heat gain is high, so heat gains are extracted from the room, reducing the load on the area HVAC system. In addition, duct pressure is negative; keeping contaminants in the system should the duct develop a leak.

3.15.2 Granulators/Coaters/Fluid Bed Dryers

These units typically have dedicated air-handling systems that are independent of the area HVAC. The design should consider what happens during periods of non-use and whether there is potential for moisture to migrate from an outside high-humidity environment into the system. Other aspects to consider include:

  • Assess the risks of corrosion during use – what ductwork materials should be used?
  • What areas of the duct are pressurized? What is the risk of drawing in untreated air? What is the risk of potentially contaminated (with product) air leaking out?
  • If for multiple campaigned products, does the duct need Clean In Place (CIP)?

3.15.3 Glassware Depyrogenation Tunnels

Glassware depyrogenation tunnels generally are located between rooms with different area classifications (grades) and operate intermittently. They present a challenge to HVAC system designers. Area pressure differentials typically need to be held at a consistent level, usually requiring some type of active pressure control.

As tunnels are started up and the temperatures and volumes stabilize, there is a dynamic period in terms of changing airflow during which filling is not in operation. Without a pressurized cool in-feed zone between the washer and the heat zone of the tunnel, held at the same pressure as the sterile filling line, extremely hot air can exit the front of the tunnel, creating a need for local exhaust to remove the excess heat and potentially creating a hazard.

Other issues with depyrogenation tunnels include the testing and integrity of high temperature HEPA filters and the monitoring of particle levels in the hot zone (sometimes over 325°C).

3.15.4 Isolator Systems

[Figure 3.16: Isolator System Schematic 1]

[Figure 3.17: Isolator System Schematic 2]

Where aseptic processing or containment of hazardous materials is required, barrier-isolator technology may be applied. Generally, when a closed isolator is used in aseptic processing and can be pressurized, room air class can be relieved by one or (often) two levels, requiring fewer air class zones, less gowning, and lower HVAC airflow.

When an isolator is used as containment for non-sterile products, potential for product release to the air and cross-contamination often is greatly reduced, lessening risk to product and operators and requiring less rigorous HVAC systems.

Isolators are a microenvironment and the area of greatest risk, and therefore, greatest environmental control is minimized. This design makes isolators a "green" technology with lower energy consumption than conventional processing.

Isolator technology for aseptic processing or containment of hazardous material can reduce the exposure of operators to product and the exposure of product to disinfection chemicals that may be used in a conventional aseptic processing suite.

Isolators may be fed with room air or by dedicated air handlers. The use of dedicated air handlers decreases the risk of VPHP (vapor phase hydrogen peroxide) escape from the isolator and simplifies balancing of the room and enclosure. Use of room air is more energy efficient and potentially demonstrates less impact on isolator pressurization relative to the room because of changes in the isolator operating mode (or phase).

Depending on the source of makeup air to an isolator's air handling system, there may be an effect on room pressure when an isolator is in use. Most isolators have multiple operating modes for open setup with unidirectional flow, CIP, closed-operation. The integration of an isolator to a processing room requires careful consideration of all operating phases and conditions.

VPHP sensors should monitor the room around the isolator and mechanical spaces outside the room as a further safety measure.

The air classification required for the background environment depends on the design of an isolator and the application. Room cooling loads should account for the heat generated by the isolator fan system(s).

3.15.5 Vial Capping

The 2008 revision to EU GMP Volume 4 Annex 1 provides specific requirements for finishing of sterile products, specifically for freeze drying vials:

"Partially stoppered freeze drying vials should be maintained under Grade A conditions at all times until the stopper is fully inserted."
"As the equipment used to crimp vials can generate large quantities of non viable particulates, the equipment should be located at a separate station equipped with adequate air extraction."
"Vial Capping can be undertaken as an aseptic process using sterilized caps or as a clean process outside the aseptic core. Where this latter approach is adopted, vials should be protected by Grade A conditions up to the point of leaving the aseptic filling area, and thereafter, stoppered vials should be protected with a Grade A supply until the cap has been crimped."

Where facilities are being modified to comply with these regulations, the following factors should be considered:

  • A UFH can be used to provide a robust Grade A (Grade 5) local airflow.
  • The loss of air from an area due to an extract system for capper particles should be considered during balancing, unless the air is re-introduced into the room (via a HEPA filter).
  • The heat gains from the fans of unidirectional flow and extract systems should be considered.

3.15.6 Lyophilizer

As the loading and unloading of a freeze dryer should be under Grade 5 (Grade A) conditions, it is usual to place a large UFH over the lyophilizer door. Smoke tests (with and without operator presence) may reveal poor air patterns near the bottom of the door opening. A low level return (with considerable airflow) below the lyophilizer door may help improve patterns.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：製程設備與 HVAC 的「共舞」

製程設備不是孤立運作的——每一台設備都會影響房間的壓差、溫度、濕度和氣流模式。本節涵蓋五類關鍵設備整合挑戰：

  • 除塵系統（Dust Extract）：故障時的交叉污染風險、脈衝清潔時的氣流反向
  • 造粒/包衣/流化床（Granulators/Coaters/FBD）：獨立 AHU、非運作期的防潮、CIP 需求
  • 去熱原隧道（Depyrogenation Tunnel）：跨分級區域、啟動動態期、325°C 高溫 HEPA 測試
  • 隔離器（Isolator）：多操作模式對房間壓差的影響、VPHP 洩漏監控
  • 壓蓋/凍乾機（Capping/Lyophilizer）：Grade A 保護需求、鋁屑排氣

比喻說明：隔離器是「太空站」，RABS 是「無塵室」

回到我們的經典比喻：

  • 隔離器（Isolator，隔離器）= 太空站：完全密閉的微環境，內部自給自足，與外部物理隔離。即使外面是「太空」（低等級環境），內部仍然安全
  • RABS（限制進出屏障系統）= 無塵室：開放式屏障，依賴周圍環境（Grade B 背景）的氣流品質來維持無菌性

ISPE 稱隔離器為「綠色技術」——因為背景環境可以降級，HVAC 風量和能耗大幅減少。這是未來 CDMO 設施的趨勢方向。

重點提示：去熱原隧道的 HVAC 設計陷阱

去熱原隧道是無菌充填線上最容易被忽略的 HVAC 挑戰：

  • 位置特殊：橫跨兩個不同潔淨等級的房間（洗瓶區 vs 充填區）
  • 間歇運行：啟動和穩定期間的氣流動態變化會擾亂壓差
  • 沒有加壓冷進料區：高溫空氣（可達 350°C）會從隧道前端溢出
  • 高溫 HEPA 測試：在 325°C 以上的環境中驗證 HEPA 完整性是極大技術挑戰

解決方案的關鍵：設置與充填線同壓的加壓冷進料區，並使用主動壓差控制來應對隧道啟動期的動態變化。

重點提示：隔離器操作模式切換對房間壓差的影響

隔離器有多個操作模式：開放設定（Unidirectional Flow）、CIP、密閉操作、VPHP 去污染、通風吹掃。每次模式切換都可能改變隔離器與房間之間的氣流關係。

特別注意 VPHP 通風吹掃階段：隔離器排出的空氣不再回到房間（因為含有殘餘 H2O2），這會導致房間壓力下降。HVAC 控制必須預先考慮這些過渡狀態。

實務應用：CDMO 的壓蓋策略決策

EU Annex 1 對壓蓋提供了兩種合規路徑：

  • 方案 A：無菌壓蓋——使用已滅菌的鋁蓋，在無菌核心區內完成。優點：簡化流程。缺點：設備成本高、鋁屑污染管理困難
  • 方案 B：潔淨壓蓋——在無菌核心區外完成，但必須用 Grade A 氣流保護已加塞的藥瓶，直到鋁蓋壓緊。優點：設備相對簡單。缺點：需要額外的 UFH 和排氣系統

多數 CDMO 選擇方案 B + UFH 保護，因為它更容易在現有設施中改造實施。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 除塵系統使用壓縮空氣脈衝清潔時，氣流可能短暫反向——這對多房間共用除塵系統有什麼交叉污染風險？如何降低這個風險？
  2. 隔離器使用「房間空氣」和「專屬 AHU」作為進氣來源，各有什麼優缺點？CDMO 應該如何選擇？
  3. 為什麼凍乾機門前方的 UFH 需要做「有操作人員」和「無操作人員」兩種條件下的煙霧測試？
3.16 Medical Devices（醫療器材）

原文內容 Original Text

[Figure 3.18: Typical AF&ID for "Clean" or Bioburden Reduced, Device Assembly]

[Figure 3.19: Horizontal Flow Clean Workstation]

Requirements for environmental control in medical device facilities are based on the risk to patients from a medical device and downstream processing. For further information on the risks and classification of medical devices, see Appendicies 8 and 10.

Where devices do not contact the patient or contact the patient only externally, cleanliness requirements may be equivalent to those for packaging areas.

Where medical device components are used internally, but are processed downstream (e.g., terminally sterilized) processing typically should be performed in a classified space, as with other sterile products.

Where devices or components of a device are used internally for patients, aseptic considerations should be applied.

Cleanliness of open processing areas should be maintained via control of airflow between product handling area or airlock and surrounding spaces:

  • Isolation via an airlock should be used. Where solvents are used (e.g., for welding plastics), pressure bubble airlocks are recommended to prevent migration of flammable vapors to the building.
  • Monitoring and alarming of direction of airflow (through DP, hotwire velocity sensors, air balance, flow tracking, etc.) to surrounding rooms is recommended.
  • AHU filtration – MERV 7 followed by MERV 13/14 filtration is recommended.
  • Final filtration – 95% DOP/PAO efficiency is recommended in non-aseptic product areas; terminal HEPA filters may be appropriate.
  • Return or exhaust air grilles may be equipped with removable 30% dust stop filters. The effect of filter loading on room pressurization or direction of airflow should be considered.
  • Airflow and makeup air delivery should be directed to protect the product.
  • Dust collection with high transport velocities (3000 to 5000 fpm, 15 to 25 m/s) may be required where plastics or metals are machined.
  • LEV for smoke and solvent vapor removal may be required where machining metal, welding metal and solvent, or heat welding plastics. These should be designed and engineered according to ACGIH standards.
  • The ACGIH Industrial Ventilation Manual decision analysis and design criteria should be consulted for guidance on recirculation.
  • Recirculation of LEV exhaust to the AHU or the general building usually is not acceptable.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：醫療器材 HVAC 遵循「患者風險分級」原則

Medical Device（醫療器材）：涵蓋範圍極廣，從外用繃帶到植入式心臟瓣膜。HVAC 需求完全取決於器材的患者風險等級：

  • 不接觸患者 / 僅外部接觸：HVAC 需求等同包裝區——基本控制即可
  • 內部使用但有終端滅菌：需要分級環境（如 Grade C/D），因為終端滅菌是最後的安全網
  • 內部使用且無終端滅菌：必須使用無菌製程標準——等同注射劑充填的 HVAC 要求

這個分級邏輯完全符合決策層級：無菌保證（患者安全）永遠是第一優先。

比喻說明：醫療器材的 HVAC 就像餐具的衛生標準

不同類型的餐具需要不同等級的清潔：

  • 外帶紙盒（不接觸食物的外包裝）= 基本衛生即可
  • 盤子（接觸食物但會再洗一次）= 需要乾淨環境存放，但最後有清洗步驟
  • 手術刀（直接進入人體）= 必須在無菌條件下處理，容錯率為零

重點提示：醫療器材製造的獨特挑戰——金屬/塑料加工

與製藥設施不同，醫療器材製造可能涉及金屬切削、塑料焊接等工業加工。這帶來獨特的 HVAC 需求：

  • 高速除塵：金屬/塑料碎屑需要 3000-5000 fpm（15-25 m/s）的高傳輸速度才能有效輸送
  • 溶劑蒸氣排除：塑料焊接產生的溶劑蒸氣需要 LEV 排除，且不可回流
  • 壓力泡氣閘：使用溶劑的區域建議採用壓力泡氣閘，防止易燃蒸氣擴散到建築其他區域

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 一個植入式骨科器材（如人工關節）的組裝線應該用什麼等級的 HVAC？如果該器材會在下游進行伽馬射線滅菌呢？
  2. 為什麼醫療器材製造中 LEV 的排氣不可以回流到 AHU？這與 OSD 設施的規定有什麼相似之處？

本節重點回顧 Section Summary

  • OSD 非強效：CNC 環境、MERV 7+13/14 過濾、氣閘隔離、LEV 粉塵控制。多產品併行需雙重 HEPA 和壓力氣閘
  • OSD 強效：密閉容器為首選，100% 直排或雙重 HEPA，BIBO 過濾器，聲光警報。操作員保護與產品保護同等重要
  • 無菌製程：終端 HEPA（天花板安裝）、壓差串聯、單向氣流 0.45 m/s、24/7 運行、304SS 風管。ISPE 的最高等級 HVAC 要求
  • 無菌強效粉末：隔離器 + 專屬 HVAC 解決「正壓 vs 負壓」矛盾。Blow-through AHU 用於低濕控制
  • 包裝/標籤：初級包裝等同製造區標準；二級包裝僅需 CNC + 舒適條件
  • 實驗室：VAV 系統 + 排煙櫃整合是核心。化學實驗室負壓、潔淨實驗室正壓。最低 6 AC/hr，建議 8-12 AC/hr
  • 取樣/秤量：HVAC 等級應匹配下游製程需求。下吹式工作台提供操作員防護
  • 倉庫：USP 受控室溫 15-30°C / MKT < 25°C。溫度分佈圖是基本要求。生物製劑需要冷鏈
  • 製程設備整合：隔離器是「綠色技術」，可降低背景環境等級。去熱原隧道、壓蓋、凍乾機都有特殊 HVAC 考量
  • 醫療器材：HVAC 跟隨患者風險等級——從包裝區標準到無菌製程標準，取決於器材的預期用途和下游滅菌方式
3

Chapter 3: 4. Design Review

設計審查 | p81-p88

ISPE Good Practice Guide: HVAC | p81 - p88

本章學習目標

  • 理解 Design Review（設計審查）與 Design Qualification（設計確效, DQ）的定義、目的及其在 HVAC 系統生命週期中的角色
  • 掌握設計審查的七大目標，以及為何在施工前進行審查能大幅降低成本與風險
  • 了解 GEP 審查與 GMP 審查的區分邏輯，以及何時需要啟動正式的設計確效（DQ）
  • 熟悉 Table 4.1 ~ Table 4.4 四大設計審查 Checklist 的應用場景與關鍵審查項目
  • 學會如何組建跨功能設計審查團隊，並運用 FMEA 等風險工具強化設計穩健性
4.1 Design Review (Design Qualification)（設計審查 / 設計確效）

原文內容 Original Text

This chapter provides guidance on verifying that the design as issued for construction will meet the user requirements.

It is easier and more cost effective to change a design before it is constructed than during or after construction, and therefore, there is less impact on capital cost and schedule. As the design develops, input from all interested parties should be considered to avoid later changes.

The process of reviewing a design (drawings and specifications) as it develops from concept to issued-for-construction status has several objectives:

  • to ensure that a design follows preferred custom and practice
  • to ensure that the concepts proposed are capable of meeting the requirements defined in the User Requirement documentation, i.e., user expectations
  • to ensure that a design minimizes risk to product quality/patient safety
  • to ensure that a design is robust and will perform reliably and that performance can be documented
  • to ensure that a design proposed is cost effective
  • to ensure that design options, the life cycle costs, and the risks have been identified and addressed
  • where an end user has preferred suppliers, the review should confirm that these have been specified

Common practice is for a design to receive comments for the designer (engineer of record) to incorporate or provide a rationale for not incorporating. Users usually do not approve a design to avoid making design responsibility unclear.

For systems that may affect product quality, it is a common practice to split design reviews into two categories:

  1. engineering or technical reviews
  2. quality (or GMP) focused reviews

A final specific "quality" review usually is performed to confirm that the HVAC system (and the specified related conditions for the areas it serves (temperature, humidity, classification, DPs, etc.) comply with GMP regulations/organizational standards. This review may be called Design Qualification, allowing the statement to be formally made that the design as reviewed is fit for its intended use, as well as confirming that the user requirements are met.

It should be noted that there are regulations requiring this review. ICH Q7 suggests a design review and the European regulations state:

"The first element of the validation of new facilities, systems, or equipment could be design qualification (DQ)."
"The compliance of the design with GMP should be demonstrated and documented."

The process of reviewing and accepting a design to be released for construction or the overall review process also may be considered a qualification of the design, because it confirms that the user organization has reviewed the design (documented through the design review process) and agrees to release the design for implementation.

Once a design has been accepted as fit for its intended use, formal change control should be applied. Formal change control should ensure the system remains in compliance and is fit for its intended use. Individual change requests may be reviewed or a single review of the system and all changes may be performed during installation qualification.

Figure 4.1 shows (diagrammatically) an overview of a typical design/design review process.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：Design Review vs. Design Qualification

Design Review（設計審查）：在設計開發過程中，由跨功能團隊對圖紙與規格進行系統性審查，確保設計符合使用者需求與工程最佳實踐。這是一個持續性的活動，從概念階段一直延續到施工發包。

Design Qualification（設計確效, DQ）：最終的「品質審查」環節，正式確認設計符合 GMP 法規要求，並宣告設計「適合預定用途（fit for intended use）」。DQ 是 V-Model 確效生命週期的第一步。

兩者的關係：Design Review 是較廣泛的工程活動，DQ 是其中專注於 GMP 合規的子集。

比喻說明：蓋房子前先看藍圖

想像你要蓋一棟新房子。在開始打地基之前，你會找建築師、水電師傅、室內設計師一起看藍圖：

  • 建築師確認結構安全（= 工程審查）
  • 消防局確認逃生通道符合法規（= GMP 審查）
  • 屋主確認格局符合需求（= URS 比對）

藍圖上改一條線只要幾分鐘，但房子蓋好後要拆牆改管線就要花幾十萬。HVAC 設計審查的邏輯完全一樣——施工前發現問題，成本是施工後的 1/10。

重點提示：DQ 是法規要求，不是可選項目

ICH Q7 與歐盟 GMP 明確要求 DQ。許多公司誤以為 DQ 只是「好的工程實踐」，但實際上：

  • EU GMP Annex 15 明確將 DQ 列為驗證的第一步
  • FDA 期望看到「設計符合 GMP 的書面證據」
  • DQ 報告是稽查員審查的第一份確效文件

在 CDMO 環境中，DQ 更加關鍵——因為 HVAC 系統必須同時滿足多個客戶產品的環境需求。

重點提示：設計核准 vs. 設計審查

原文特別指出：「Users usually do not approve a design to avoid making design responsibility unclear.」

這是一個重要的法規觀念——使用者審查但不核准設計。設計責任仍屬於設計工程師（engineer of record）。使用者的角色是提出意見與確認需求被滿足，而不是承擔設計責任。這在出問題時的責任歸屬至關重要。

實務應用：CDMO 的設計審查策略

對無菌充填 CDMO 而言，HVAC 設計審查必須特別關注：

  • 多產品彈性：不同客戶產品可能需要不同溫濕度範圍，設計是否預留調整空間？
  • 交叉污染防控：壓差梯度與氣流方向是否能防止產品間交叉污染？
  • 法規多軌合規：同一設施可能需要同時符合 FDA、EMA、PIC/S 要求
  • 產能擴張：設計是否為未來新增產線預留 HVAC 容量？

建議在 URS 階段就將這些 CDMO 特有需求明確寫入，避免設計審查時才發現缺口。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 一家 CDMO 的 HVAC 設計通過了工程審查，但尚未進行 GMP 審查就開始施工。如果稽查員發現這個情況，可能會提出什麼 observation？
  2. 設計確效（DQ）完成後，客戶要求將某間潔淨室從 ISO 8 升級為 ISO 7。這個變更應該走什麼流程？為什麼？
4.1 (cont'd) Knowledge Capture & Review Methods（知識管理與審查方法）

原文內容 Original Text

Organizations may develop knowledge capture systems such as:

  • design review checklists (see Table 4.1 to Table 4.4)
  • design guides: defining the preferred way of designing a given system type, e.g., HVAC systems or compendial water systems

These approaches should ensure that organizational experience and preferences are both captured and considered. They also should consider novel concepts.

The review method to be used should be defined and review participants should agree to the method. The method and participants may vary depending on the system type.

A preferred approach for design review is for a multi-disciplinary team to ensure that key viewpoints are considered with SMEs in HVAC, controls, commissioning, and regulatory requirements.

For areas where high system reliability is required, e.g., vivariums, a formal review may be conducted, such as using a Failure Modes and Effects Analysis (FMEA) to ensure that the design is adequately robust. A simplified version of this approach may be beneficial, considering the effect of system failures on adjacent areas to ensure that the design is robust and that system failure will not compromise product.

Notes taken from the design review should be implemented through drawing and specification changes. If the revision cross references the notes, it may not be necessary to formally close out all actions in a GEP environment. SMEs who sign off drawings for construction are expected to check that necessary changes have been incorporated in the drawing.

Large projects usually have multiple reviews at key stages, e.g., a review at the concept stage (to ensure that the user team agrees with the proposals from the A&E design company), interim design reviews, and a final review. These may focus on individual systems, or on specific areas of the design, e.g., HVAC systems or chilled water systems.

For smaller projects, e.g., with one system, there may be fewer reviews, but there should be one review before design release, as a minimum. The project team should agree the approach to be applied to a specific project.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：知識管理系統（Knowledge Capture）

ISPE 在此強調的是組織知識的制度化——不要讓經驗只留在資深工程師的腦袋裡。兩個關鍵工具：

  • Design Review Checklists：標準化的審查清單，確保每次審查都涵蓋所有關鍵面向（見 Table 4.1~4.4）
  • Design Guides：公司內部的設計規範，定義 HVAC 系統的「慣用做法」

這兩個工具的價值在於：即使資深工程師離職，組織知識仍然被保留。對 CDMO 而言，這更是確保多站點一致性的關鍵。

比喻說明：飛行員的 Checklist

波音 737 起飛前，無論飛行員多資深，都必須逐項完成起飛前檢查清單。這不是因為飛行員不夠專業，而是因為：

  • 人腦在處理複雜系統時容易遺漏
  • 清單確保每次都達到同樣的品質標準
  • 這是從歷史事故中提煉出的寶貴教訓

HVAC 設計審查清單（Table 4.1~4.4）就是 HVAC 工程師的「起飛前 Checklist」。

重點提示：FMEA 在 HVAC 設計中的應用

原文提到對高可靠性需求的區域（如動物房 vivarium）可使用 FMEA（失效模式與效應分析）進行正式審查。

在無菌製造環境中，FMEA 特別適用於以下場景：

  • 單一 AHU 故障對相鄰潔淨室壓差的影響
  • 冷卻系統失效時的溫度失控風險
  • HEPA 過濾器洩漏對潔淨度的影響
  • 控制系統故障時的 fail-safe 設計是否足夠

即使不做完整 FMEA，至少要考慮「系統失效對產品品質的影響」——這就是原文所說的 simplified version。

設計審查階段規劃

大型專案（多系統）：
  概念階段審查 → 中期設計審查 → 最終審查（施工前）
  ├── 概念階段：確認 A&E 公司提案符合使用者需求
  ├── 中期審查：檢視詳細設計，識別風險
  └── 最終審查：確認施工圖完整，發行 DQ

小型專案（單一系統）：
  至少一次設計審查 → 施工前發行
  └── 審查範圍與深度由專案團隊決定

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的公司沒有 HVAC Design Guide，每次新專案都從頭設計。這可能造成什麼問題？你會如何建立第一版 Design Guide？
  2. 一位資深 HVAC 工程師即將退休，他 20 年來累積的設計經驗大部分在他腦中。你會用什麼方法在他退休前將這些知識「制度化」？
4.2 Design Review Process（設計審查流程）

原文內容 Original Text

Typically, the review process is formalized to make it more efficient and to ensure that all interested parties are involved the process.

The review may be structured to cover all design aspects or divided into two:

  1. A GEP review: to ensure that GEPs are incorporated; this may include a review of maintainability of the systems.
  2. A GMP review: to ensure that compliance requirements are adequately addressed by the proposed design.

(A Health and Safety review also may be justified for facilities handling potent or flammable compounds.)

The GEP review may be complex, (a significant amount of knowledge and experience within an organization can be captured and used in the review process). A formal audit trail usually is not required for GEP observations. Typically, reviewers ensure that comments have been addressed; therefore, there may be a benefit in keeping separate reviews.

The GMP review typically is simple and consistent; observations should be tracked to ensure that they are addressed and an audit trail established.

A series of typical categorized design challenges may be developed. See Table 4.1 to Table 4.4.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：GEP 審查 vs. GMP 審查

這是本節最重要的架構性概念。ISPE 建議將設計審查分為兩軌：

  • GEP 審查（Good Engineering Practice）：涵蓋工程最佳實踐、可維護性、可靠性、節能等「做得好」的面向。觀察記錄不需要正式的 audit trail。
  • GMP 審查：聚焦於法規合規——溫濕度、分級、壓差、交叉污染等「必須做到」的面向。每項觀察必須被追蹤並建立 audit trail。

為什麼要分開？因為 GEP 觀察可能有數十甚至數百條（包括大量工程偏好），如果全部混在 GMP audit trail 裡，反而會稀釋真正關鍵的 GMP 合規議題。

比喻說明：餐廳驗收的兩層審查

一家新餐廳裝修完畢，驗收分兩個層級：

  • 工程驗收（= GEP）：檢查電線是否整齊、水管是否有漏、排煙效果好不好、設備是否方便維修——做得好不好的問題
  • 衛生局稽查（= GMP）：檢查廚房溫度控制、食材儲存區域隔離、排水系統是否符合食安法規——合不合規的問題

工程驗收有很多建議（「這個插座建議換個位置比較好用」），但衛生局稽查只關注法規紅線（「冷藏溫度超標就不發許可」）。分開處理，效率最高。

重點提示：EHS 審查不要忘記

原文順帶提到：「A Health and Safety review also may be justified for facilities handling potent or flammable compounds.」

這在 CDMO 環境中特別重要，因為：

  • 高效價藥物（high potency compounds）需要負壓控制與專用排氣
  • 易燃溶劑操作需要防爆設計（ATEX 分區）
  • 生物製劑可能涉及生物安全等級（BSL）要求

HVAC 設計審查時，務必確認是否需要額外的 EHS 審查，否則施工後補救代價極高。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 在 GEP 審查中，你發現設計工程師將 AHU 維護空間壓縮到僅 40 公分寬。這不違反任何 GMP 法規，但你認為會造成維護困難。你會如何在審查中記錄這個觀察？需要 audit trail 嗎？
  2. 一項 GMP 審查發現：設計圖上 B 級潔淨室與 C 級走廊之間的壓差設計為 10 Pa，低於建議的 15 Pa。你會如何處理？
Table 4.1: Typical Design Concept Stage GMP Review（概念階段 GMP 審查）

原文內容 Original Text

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：概念階段的「大方向」審查

Table 4.1 是在設計最早期（概念階段）使用的 Checklist。此時重點不在細節，而在大方向是否正確：

  • 關鍵參數是否定義？——如果連溫度、濕度、壓差的設計目標都沒有定義，後面的設計都是白做
  • 區域劃分是否合理？——AHU 分區必須基於製程需求，而非建築便利
  • 氣流方向是否正確？——空氣永遠從潔淨區流向較不潔淨區，這是最基本的 GMP 原則

重點提示：風險評估（Risk Assessment）是 DQ 的基礎

第一個 Design Challenge 就問：「關鍵參數的選定理由（風險評估）是否有文件記錄？」

這揭示了一個關鍵邏輯：DQ 的起點不是設計本身，而是風險評估。你必須先透過 Risk Assessment 識別哪些參數是「關鍵的」，然後才能審查設計是否滿足這些關鍵參數。

沒有 Risk Assessment 支撐的 DQ，在稽查時會被視為「沒有科學依據的表面功夫」。

比喻說明：蓋學校前先確認學區規劃

概念階段審查就像蓋學校前，先確認：

  • 這個學區要服務多少學生？（= 製程需求決定 AHU 分區）
  • 教室要不要空調？圖書館要不要獨立溫控？（= 溫濕度需求定義）
  • 消防通道方向對不對？（= 氣流方向 / 壓差方向）

如果這些大方向搞錯了，等校舍蓋好才發現，改建成本可能是原始建設成本的 30~50%。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你正在審查一個新廠的 HVAC 概念設計。設計圖顯示所有製造區域共用一台大型 AHU。從 GMP 角度，你會提出什麼疑慮？
  2. 設計書上寫著「溫度範圍 18-25°C」但沒有定義哪些區域需要更嚴格的控制。這在概念階段審查中應該如何處理？
Table 4.2: Detail Design or Issue for Construction Stage GMP Review（詳細設計 / 施工階段 GMP 審查）

原文內容 Original Text

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：詳細設計審查的全面性

Table 4.2 是整個設計審查中最長、最詳盡的 Checklist，涵蓋了 HVAC 系統從外部條件到內部細節的所有 GMP 相關面向。可以將其分為幾個主題群組：

  • 外部環境：氣象條件、進排氣口位置、盛行風向
  • 內部環境：溫度、濕度、壓差、分級、感測器位置
  • 空氣處理：AHU 設計、過濾器規格、風管材質
  • 監控與報警：紀錄系統、報警限值、控制安全
  • 失效模式：斷電、AHU 故障、交叉污染風險

重點提示：關鍵數字必須記住

Table 4.2 中包含幾個極為重要的設計數據：

  • 15 Pa：不同潔淨等級之間 Airlock 的最低設計壓差
  • 20 Pa：建議的設計壓差（預留施工誤差的裕度）
  • 99.97%（H13/H14）：分級區域的 HEPA 過濾器效率要求
  • 4 feet（約 1.2 m）：非分級區域軟管最大允許長度；分級區域禁止使用軟管

這些數字在稽查中經常被問到，必須能夠立即回答並引用出處。

重點提示：感測器位置的代表性

原文對 monitoring sensor 位置的論述非常詳細，值得特別關注：

  • 大空間（如倉庫）需要多點 mapping 與監控
  • 小空間 1-2 個監測點通常足夠，但需在確效時證明其代表性
  • 感測器位置要考慮「產品暴露在哪裡」——例如充填區的感測器應該靠近產品暴露點
  • 感測器必須方便維護與校準

感測器位置不當是 FDA 483 常見的觀察項目之一。

比喻說明：溫度計放在冰箱門邊 vs. 食物旁

家裡冰箱如果把溫度計放在門邊（每次開門溫度波動最大的地方），量到的溫度就不能代表食物儲存區的真實溫度。

潔淨室的感測器也一樣——如果放在天花板回風口旁，量到的數值反映的是回風溫度，不是操作人員身旁產品暴露點的溫度。感測器位置的「代表性」是審查中的關鍵質疑點。

實務應用：CDMO 的 Table 4.2 審查要點

對 CDMO 而言，Table 4.2 中有幾個問題特別需要深入審查：

  • Availability requirements（可用性需求）：不同客戶產品可能有不同的生產排程需求，HVAC 的設計外部條件百分比（如 99.6% availability = ASHRAE 0.4% design conditions）直接影響設備選型與成本
  • Multiple AHU failure modes：CDMO 通常有多條產線同時運作，一台 AHU 故障是否會影響其他產線的壓差級聯？
  • Containment provisions：如果某個客戶的產品是高效價藥物，排氣過濾設計必須升級
  • 21 CFR 173.310 steam additives：蒸氣加濕器的水質問題在 FDA 稽查中常被關注

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的工廠位於台灣中南部，夏季高溫多濕。設計工程師使用台北的氣象數據作為外部設計條件。這會造成什麼問題？你會如何修正？
  2. 設計圖上所有 Airlock 的壓差設計為 15 Pa。考慮到施工後可能的偏差，你會建議修改嗎？為什麼 ISPE 建議 20 Pa？
  3. 一條新的無菌充填線，設計中沒有提到 AHU 故障時的 fail-safe 機制。你會在設計審查中提出什麼建議？
Table 4.3: GEP Design Review High Level Challenges（GEP 設計審查高層級挑戰）

原文內容 Original Text

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：GEP 審查的「戰略性」問題

相較於 Table 4.2 那樣的詳細 Checklist，Table 4.3 只有 5 個問題，但每一個都是高層級的戰略性問題。這些問題不是問「有沒有做到某個規格」，而是問「設計的整體思維是否正確」：

  • Reliability：系統能否長期穩定運行？
  • Best practices：是否採用了當前業界最佳做法？
  • Maintainability：日常維護是否方便？校準是否可行？
  • Failure modes：系統故障時會發生什麼？設計是否考慮了 fail-safe？
  • Cleanability：天花板回風腔體（ceiling plenum）如何清潔？

比喻說明：買車的五大核心問題

Table 4.3 就像你買車前問的五個根本問題：

  • 這車耐不耐用？（= reliability）
  • 是不是最新款的安全配備？（= current best practices）
  • 保養方不方便？維修費貴不貴？（= maintenance/calibration）
  • 爆胎或電池沒電時怎麼辦？（= failure modes）
  • 座椅套好不好洗？（= cleanability of ceiling plenum）

這些是「買車決策」層級的問題，不是「這顆螺絲要不要鍍鉻」的細節問題。

重點提示：Ceiling Plenum Return 的清潔問題

最後一個 Design Challenge 特別值得關注：「Are ceiling plenum returns proposed; if so, how would the ceiling void be cleaned?」

許多工廠為了簡化設計，採用天花板上方空間（ceiling plenum）作為回風通道。但這帶來嚴重的清潔問題：

  • 天花板上方空間難以目視檢查
  • 灰塵、微粒會在此處累積
  • 在分級區域，如果 ceiling plenum 無法有效清潔，可能導致微粒從縫隙掉入潔淨室

GEP 的觀點是：如果清潔不可行，就不應該採用這種設計，即使它更省錢。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的設計團隊提出使用 ceiling plenum return 以降低建設成本。品質部門提出清潔疑慮。作為專案經理，你會如何評估這個 trade-off？
  2. Table 4.3 問「Does the design proposed demonstrate current best practices?」——你會用什麼標準來定義 HVAC 的「current best practices」？你會參考哪些指引或標準？
Table 4.4: Detail Design or Issue for Construction Stage GEP Review（詳細設計 / 施工階段 GEP 審查）

原文內容 Original Text

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：GEP 審查的「操作導向」視角

Table 4.4 與 Table 4.2 有許多重疊的審查項目（如感測器位置、風管材質、斷電處理），但審查的出發點不同：

  • Table 4.2 (GMP)：「這個設計是否符合法規要求？」
  • Table 4.4 (GEP)：「這個設計是否方便操作、維護、並能長期可靠運行？」

GEP 審查關注的是系統的長期可持續性——一個技術上合規但維護困難的系統，最終會因為維護不良而變得不合規。

比喻說明：合法但不好用的廚房

一個通過衛生局稽查的廚房（GMP 合規），如果：

  • 清洗區的水龍頭太矮，廚師長期彎腰導致背痛
  • 冰箱放在狹窄角落，搬食材很不方便
  • 排煙管道沒有清潔口，日積月累油垢堵塞

這些都不違法，但會讓廚房運作效率低下。GEP 審查就是在抓這些「合規但不好用」的設計盲點。

重點提示：Building Sickness Syndrome

Table 4.4 提到的 Building Sickness Syndrome（病態建築症候群） 是一個常被忽略但越來越受重視的議題：

  • HVAC 系統設計不當可能導致工作人員頭痛、疲勞、過敏等症狀
  • 常見原因：新鮮空氣比例不足、回風過濾不良、冷凝水排放不當導致微生物生長
  • 對 CDMO 而言，操作人員的健康直接影響操作品質和人員流動率

GEP 審查不只看設備，也看人——系統對操作人員的友善程度。

重點提示：未來擴展性（Future-Proofing）

Table 4.4 有一個獨特的問題：「How does the design allow for future changes in the room layout in terms of sensor locations and zoning?」

這個問題在 GMP Checklist 中不會出現，但對 CDMO 極其重要：

  • 客戶組合會變化，製程需求可能改變
  • 未來可能需要重新分區或增加監控點
  • 設計時如果沒有預留彈性，每次變更都需要大規模改造

好的 GEP 設計不只解決今天的問題，更為明天的變化留出空間。

實務應用：GEP vs GMP 審查的實際操作

在實務中，建議的操作方式：

  • GEP 審查：由 HVAC 工程師主導，邀請廠務維護團隊參與。以工作坊形式進行，記錄在工程文件中，不需要正式的 CAPA 追蹤
  • GMP 審查：由 QA 主導，邀請 HVAC SME 與製程工程師參與。每項觀察都需要在 DQ 報告中追蹤至結案
  • 分開進行：避免 GEP 的大量工程建議淹沒 GMP 的關鍵法規議題

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 比較 Table 4.2 和 Table 4.4 中關於「感測器位置」的審查問題。兩者的審查出發點有何不同？你會如何分別記錄 GMP 和 GEP 審查中的觀察？
  2. 你的工廠有 10 年歷史，當初沒有做 GEP 審查。現在要進行 HVAC 系統升級，你會建議加入哪些 GEP 審查項目來彌補原始設計的不足？

本節重點回顧 Section Summary

  • Design Review（設計審查）是確保 HVAC 設計在施工前符合使用者需求的系統性審查流程；Design Qualification（DQ, 設計確效）是其中專注於 GMP 合規的正式環節，為法規所要求
  • 設計審查應分為 GEP 審查（工程最佳實踐，不需正式 audit trail）和 GMP 審查（法規合規，需要 audit trail），以避免混淆關鍵議題
  • Table 4.1（概念階段 GMP）聚焦大方向：關鍵參數定義、AHU 分區合理性、氣流方向正確性
  • Table 4.2（詳細設計 GMP）是最完整的 Checklist，涵蓋環境設計、監控、過濾、失效模式等所有 GMP 面向。關鍵數字：壓差至少 15 Pa（建議 20 Pa）、HEPA 效率 99.97%（H13/H14）
  • Table 4.3（GEP 高層級）以 5 個戰略性問題審查設計的可靠性、先進性與可維護性
  • Table 4.4（GEP 詳細）關注操作面的長期可持續性，包括清潔性、未來擴展性、人員健康（病態建築症候群）
  • 設計審查團隊應為跨功能團隊（HVAC SME、控制工程師、試車人員、法規專家），高風險區域可運用 FMEA 強化設計穩健性
  • DQ 完成後必須啟動正式變更管控（Change Control），確保後續任何設計變更都經過審查
4

Chapter 4: 5. Equipment Spec & Qualification

設備規格與確效 | p89-p136

ISPE Good Practice Guide: HVAC | p89 - p105

本章學習目標

  • 掌握製藥級 AHU（空氣處理機組）的設計規格與建造要求，包括箱體、面板、地板與排水盤等關鍵組件
  • 理解風扇（Fan）選型的關鍵因素：類型、驅動方式、軸承壽命、振動控制與冗餘設計
  • 了解馬達與變頻器（VFD）在節能與精確控制中的核心角色
  • 熟悉加熱盤管（Heating Coil）與冷卻盤管（Cooling Coil）的規格參數，包括面速度、管徑與排數限制
  • 掌握加濕器（Humidifier）與除濕器（Dehumidifier）的不同技術方案（蒸汽注入、乾燥劑輪、雙路徑系統等）
  • 理解風管（Ductwork）設計、風門（Damper）選型、排氣系統與粉塵收集的 GMP 要求
5.1.1 Introduction 5.1.1 簡介

原文內容 Original Text

This section focuses on the design and specification of components that deliver conditioned air to GMP spaces. The equipment should meet safety, product, and regulatory requirements, while providing environmental comfort and protection to employees. Generally, to optimize life cycle cost, equipment should have:

  • robust capabilities for achieving initial, continuous, and long-term operation
  • ease of maintenance
  • low energy use

The guidance in this section suggests materials and construction that may help to assure reliability and uptime, leading to lower operating and maintenance (life cycle) costs. Economic analysis may justify the use of less expensive or "off the shelf" HVAC equipment.

Installation, startup, and ongoing maintenance aspects should be considered. See Sections 6.3 and 6.4.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念解析 Key Concepts

Life Cycle Cost（生命週期成本）：不只是採購價格，還包括安裝、運轉能耗、維護保養、停機損失到最終報廢的全部成本。ISPE 在這裡開宗明義告訴我們：HVAC 設備選型的核心判斷標準是「全生命週期成本最優」，而非「初始採購價格最低」。

Conditioned Air（調節空氣）：經過溫度、濕度、潔淨度控制的空氣，是 GMP 環境的基本保障。

比喻說明 Analogy

就像買車一樣——一台便宜的車如果油耗高、常常拋錨，長期來看反而更貴。HVAC 設備也是如此：初期多投資在品質上，可以換來更低的能耗、更少的停機維修、更長的使用壽命。這就是 TCO（總擁有成本）的思維方式。

重點提示 Key Notes

ISPE 特別提到「off the shelf」設備在某些情況下也可以接受——這代表 GMP 不等於一定要用最貴的「製藥專用」設備。關鍵是進行經濟分析（Economic Analysis），證明所選設備能滿足 User Requirements。這是一種風險導向（Risk-based）的務實態度。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的工廠在採購 HVAC 設備時，是否有進行全生命週期成本分析？還是只看報價單上的數字？
  2. 「Off the shelf」設備在你的廠區中使用的比例有多高？使用經驗如何？
5.1.2 Air Handler Unit（空氣處理機組）

原文內容 Original Text

HVAC equipment manufacturers may specialize in "pharma grade" air handlers, particularly in large capacity custom units. Smaller HVAC systems have been successful using "off-the-shelf" HVAC equipment from acceptable manufacturers. As both options can satisfy product requirements, the driver usually is economics, not GMP. The selection of equipment should satisfy user requirements; less elaborate equipment may require more maintenance and may be less energy efficient, but may be acceptable.

Aspects that should be considered in creating purchase specifications for bespoke HVAC systems include:

  • GMP air-handlers should be constructed to meet stringent performance, improved reliability, and maintenance requirements for critical areas
  • Air handler components, such as coils, humidifiers, dehumidifiers, dampers, fans, motors, and filters should be designed and constructed to provide 115% of design capacity to accommodate potential increased demand or future expansion

5.1.2.1 Cabinet Construction

In geographic regions of moderate to high ambient humidity levels, condensation on the exterior of the AHU casing may be an issue. Where high humidity is relatively persistent, this condensation may lead to exterior rust, mildew, and mold growth. Consideration should be given to no through metal (a thermal break) on wall, floor, doorframe, ceiling sections, and doors. If thermal breaks are not correctly designed and implemented, there is a potential for exterior condensation.

To minimize leakage of expensive conditioned air, total air leakage rate from the casing may be specified at no greater than 0.5% of rated flow at 150% of the design pressure or 50 CFM (1.42 m³/min), whichever is greater, or the requirements stated in EN 1886 standard for the most severe "leakage class" operation. As eventual deterioration of seals on doors, dampers, and other components will lead to increased air leak rates, the initial measured air tightness can be used as a gauge to identify future loss of air flow.

5.1.2.2 Insulated Wall Panel Construction
