  • AHU zoning diagrams
  • filter test data
  • control diagrams and critical alarms calibration/test records
  • sequence of operation
  • equipment submittal drawings
  • as-built ductwork, piping, and equipment plans

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：主文件的範疇

Master Document（主文件）是「活的文件」——它不是施工結束就封存的歷史記錄，而是持續反映系統當前狀態的參考文件。

與 ETOP 的差異在於：ETOP 是一次性移交的文件包，而主文件必須隨著系統變更而持續更新。

本節列出的 9 項文件，每一項都對應著維護和設施工程的核心需求：

  • AF&ID：系統「全貌圖」，最重要的主文件
  • 序列操作（Sequence of Operation）：記錄 HVAC 系統的控制邏輯，如啟動/停機順序、連鎖條件
  • 過濾器測試數據：HEPA 完整性測試記錄，直接影響環境分級

比喻說明：主文件如同病歷

主文件就像一個人的病歷——它不是出生時寫一次就永遠不變，而是每次就醫都會更新。血壓、體重、用藥記錄都要持續反映最新狀態。

如果醫生看到的是五年前的病歷，就無法做出正確的診斷。同理，如果維護工程師拿到的 AF&ID 還是三年前的版本，在故障排除時就可能做出錯誤判斷。

重點提示：變更管理與主文件同步

主文件維護的最大挑戰是變更管理的執行力。每次 HVAC 系統有變更（新增過濾器、修改風量、調整壓差設定），都必須同步更新相關的主文件。

常見的失敗模式：工程變更已經完成、確效已經簽核，但 AF&ID 還停留在變更前的版本。這在 FDA 稽查中是高頻缺失項目。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 比較 6.3 節的 ETOP 清單和 6.4 節的主文件清單，哪些文件出現在兩份清單中？為什麼這些文件需要同時存在於兩個體系？
  2. 「sequence of operation」（操作序列）為什麼是主文件？如果控制邏輯變更了但操作序列文件沒有更新，可能造成什麼風險？
6.5 GMP HVAC Documents 6.5 GMP 暖通空調文件

原文內容 Original Text

It is important to align with the risk assessment performed in the design process to determine the HVAC documents to be leveraged into commissioning and qualification or become Critical Master Documents. Since the risk assessment varies by project, the following lists of typical documents should be reviewed against project/product needs. See the ISPE Baseline® Guides (Reference 13, Appendix 12).

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：風險評估驅動的文件分類

本節是整章最重要的轉折——從「工程文件」進入「GMP 文件」領域。

ISPE 的核心邏輯是：哪些 HVAC 文件需要成為 GMP 文件，不是由固定清單決定，而是由風險評估（Risk Assessment）驅動。

這意味著同樣一份 AHU 分區圖，在 OSD 廠房可能只是 ETOP 文件，但在無菌充填區就是 Critical Master Document。差異取決於該系統對產品品質的風險影響程度。

重點提示：Decision Hierarchy 在文件管理中的體現

回到 ISPE 的決策層級：

  1. 無菌保證（Sterility Assurance）：直接影響無菌保證的 HVAC 文件（如 Grade A/B 區域的環境分級圖、HEPA 測試記錄）必須是 Critical Master Document
  2. 產品 CQA：影響產品品質屬性的 HVAC 參數文件（如溫濕度記錄）需要 GMP 管控
  3. 營運彈性：僅影響營運效率的文件（如非分級區域的 AHU 圖）可以是非 GMP 文件
6.5.1 Commissioning and Qualification Documents 6.5.1 試車與確效文件

原文內容 Original Text

Documents that typically are leveraged into commissioning and qualification include:

  • as-built AF&IDs
  • room environmental conditions – tables/schedules
  • area classification diagrams (classified spaces)
  • pressure or airflow direction “maps”
  • GMP air filter location map
  • AHU zoning diagrams
  • control diagrams or functional design for direct impact systems
  • critical instrument specifications
  • commissioning documents:
    • filter integrity or overall penetration testing (HEPA/ULPA)
    • airflow patterns for Grade 5 (Grade A) hoods and Grade 7 (Grade B) rooms
    • air balance (air changes OK for classified spaces, airflow direction OK)
    • face velocity testing (HEPA/ULPA for Grade 5/Grade A)
    • pressure relationships (contamination control)
    • particle counts at rest
    • critical monitoring and alarm testing, including calibration and alarm delay verification
    • temperature
    • humidity (if deemed critical)
    • room DP or leakage air velocity (for contamination control)
    • supply airflow (classified spaces)
    • Grade A/Grade 5 hood air flow (DP, sail switch, velocity sensor)
    • temperature mapping (usually for warehouse) – see Appendix 2
    • test protocols and supporting information
    • evidence that acceptance criteria have been met

Documents that are typically produced during commissioning and qualification (or Process Validation) are:

  • airflow visualization for Grade 5 (EU Grade A) or local protection areas in use
  • total airborne particulate testing
  • viable airborne particulate testing
  • room recovery testing (classified spaces)
  • test protocols and supporting information
  • evidence that acceptance criteria have been met

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：C&Q 文件的兩個類別

本節將試車確效（C&Q）文件分為兩大類：

  • 被「槓桿」進 C&Q 的文件（leveraged into）：這些文件原本是工程/設計文件，現在被納入確效體系作為證據。例如 AF&ID、環境條件表等。
  • 在 C&Q 過程中「產生」的文件（produced during）：這些文件是測試活動的直接產出，如氣流可視化測試、微粒計數等。

「槓桿」（leverage）這個詞是 ISPE 的用語特色——意思是不需要重做測試，直接引用已有的工程數據作為確效證據。這是「不重複測試」原則的具體體現。

比喻說明：考試的「免試入學」vs.「參加考試」

Leveraged documents 就像「免試入學」——你在施工階段已經做過的測試（如 HEPA 完整性測試），不需要在確效階段再做一次，直接引用結果。

Produced documents 就像「參加考試」——氣流可視化、微生物測試等，必須在確效階段（或製程驗證階段）才能執行，因為需要在「接近實際運行」的狀態下測試。

重點提示：試車文件清單中的關鍵細節

  • 濕度「if deemed critical」：ISPE 特別標註濕度測試是「如果被判定為關鍵」。這意味著不是每個專案都必須做濕度確效——取決於產品對濕度的敏感性（如吸濕性粉末）。
  • Alarm delay verification：不只測試警報功能，還要驗證警報延遲時間。過短會造成假警報，過長則喪失保護意義。
  • Grade A vs. Grade B 的測試差異：Grade A（層流區）需要做面風速測試和氣流可視化；Grade B（背景環境）主要測試換氣次數和壓差。
  • Particle counts "at rest"：靜態微粒計數在試車階段進行；動態微粒計數（in operation）則在製程驗證階段執行。

實務應用：CDMO 的 C&Q 文件策略

對 CDMO 而言，C&Q 文件的品質直接影響客戶稽查結果和法規申報速度：

  • 建立 C&Q 文件模板庫：標準化每個測試的方案格式和允收標準
  • 善用 Leverage 原則：FAT 在設備供應商端完成的測試數據，可直接引用為 IQ 證據，節省到場測試時間
  • 「at rest」vs.「in operation」的時序規劃：將靜態測試安排在試車階段，動態測試安排在製程驗證階段，避免排程衝突
  • 警報延遲驗證：與 BMS/SCADA 供應商在 FAT 階段就完成，避免到場後反覆調整

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 本節列出了「leveraged into」和「produced during」兩類文件。如果你在製程驗證階段才發現某項測試在試車階段被遺漏了，應該如何處理？重新做測試算是 Commissioning 還是 Qualification？
  2. 為什麼「airflow visualization」不能在靜態（at rest）階段完成，而必須在「in use」狀態下執行？請從污染控制的角度解釋。
  3. 某 CDMO 的新建無菌充填區使用 RABS 技術。請列出該區域 HVAC C&Q 至少需要哪 8 項關鍵測試文件。
6.5.2 Critical Master Documents 6.5.2 關鍵主文件

原文內容 Original Text

Critical Master Documents provide HVAC information for the CMC section of a drug filing and are subject to regulatory review.

Documents that typically are maintained as critical master documents include:

  • as-built AF&IDs
  • risk assessments and traceability matrix
  • room environmental conditions – tables and schedules
  • area classification diagrams
  • pressure or airflow direction “maps”
  • AHU Zoning Diagrams
  • qualification documents:
    • filter integrity testing (HEPA/ULPA)
    • air balance
    • air change calculations or recovery testing for classified spaces
    • face velocity testing (HEPA/ULPA in critical zones)
    • pressure relationships (continuous logging for classified spaces or spot-check?)
    • critical alarm and delay testing (temperature, RH, DP, airflow, etc.)
    • airflow visualization for Grade 5 (EU Grade A) or local protection areas
    • total airborne particulate testing (classified spaces)
    • viable airborne particulate testing (classified spaces)

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：關鍵主文件的法規意義

Critical Master Document（關鍵主文件）是 HVAC 文件體系中層級最高的文件類別。它有兩個關鍵特徵：

  • 提供 CMC 申報資訊：藥品上市申請中，CMC 部分需要描述製造環境條件。HVAC 的關鍵主文件就是這些描述的數據來源。
  • 受法規審查：FDA、EMA 等法規機構有權要求查看這些文件，且期望它們永遠反映系統的當前狀態。

重點提示：關鍵主文件 vs. C&Q 文件的比較

比較 6.5.1 和 6.5.2 的清單，會發現兩者高度重疊但有重要差異：

  • 新增項目：Risk assessments 和 Traceability matrix——這兩項在 C&Q 文件清單中未出現，但在關鍵主文件中必須包含。原因是稽查員需要看到「為什麼選擇這些測試項目」的邏輯依據。
  • 壓差記錄方式的疑問：「continuous logging for classified spaces or spot-check?」——ISPE 刻意用問號，表示這取決於風險評估。對無菌區域，連續記錄（continuous logging）通常是必要的；對非無菌分級區域，定期抽查（spot-check）可能就足夠。
  • 移除項目：溫濕度 mapping、倉庫相關測試等不在此清單中——因為這些通常不屬於「關鍵」範疇。

比喻說明：關鍵主文件如同公司營業執照

關鍵主文件的地位，就像企業的營業執照和重要合約——不是放在抽屜裡偶爾拿出來的東西，而是隨時可能被政府機關要求查看的文件。

如果營業執照上的地址已經搬遷但沒有更新，會怎樣？被罰款、被質疑誠信。HVAC 的關鍵主文件也是同理——如果稽查員發現 AF&ID 上的設備配置與現場不符，後果可能是 FDA 483 觀察項甚至 Warning Letter。

實務應用：良好文件管理規範（GDocP）在關鍵主文件中的應用

GDocP（Good Documentation Practice，良好文件管理規範）對關鍵主文件有特別嚴格的要求：

  • 版本控制：每次修訂必須有版本號、修訂日期、修訂原因、審批簽名
  • 變更追蹤：所有變更必須通過正式的變更控制程序（Change Control）
  • 定期審查：建議每年至少審查一次，確認文件內容是否仍然反映系統現狀
  • 備份與歸檔：紙本和電子版都需要安全保管，符合數據完整性（Data Integrity）要求

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 如果 FDA 稽查員要求查看你的 HVAC 關鍵主文件，你能在多少時間內提供？如果不能在 24 小時內提供最新版本，這反映了什麼問題？
  2. 為什麼 Risk Assessment 和 Traceability Matrix 只出現在「關鍵主文件」清單而不在「C&Q 文件」清單中？這兩份文件在法規稽查中扮演什麼角色？
  3. 「continuous logging or spot-check?」這個問題的答案取決於什麼因素？請為一個 CDMO 的無菌充填區設計你的壓差監控策略，並說明理由。

本節重點回顧 Section Summary

  • 文件生命週期規劃要趁早：在概念設計階段就決定每份 HVAC 文件的最終歸宿（ETOP / 主文件 / 關鍵主文件），不要等到確效完成才分類
  • 竣工圖（As-built）是基礎：所有 HVAC 文件管理的起點是將設計圖更新為竣工狀態，反映實際安裝配置。AF&ID、區域分級圖和壓差圖是最低限度必須保持最新的文件
  • ETOP 精簡但完整：工程移交文件包的設計原則是「給維護團隊他們真正需要的」，而非「把所有文件都丟過去」
  • 主文件是「活的」：Master Document 必須隨系統變更持續更新，而 Critical Master Document 更必須隨時保持在可接受稽查的狀態
  • 風險評估驅動 GMP 文件分類：哪些文件需要成為 GMP 文件，取決於設計階段的風險評估結果，而非一成不變的清單
  • C&Q 文件的「槓桿」原則：善用 leverage 概念，將施工階段已完成的測試數據直接引用為確效證據，避免重複測試
  • 關鍵主文件是最高層級：提供 CMC 申報資訊、受法規審查、包含風險評估和追溯矩陣，必須符合 GDocP（良好文件管理規範）的嚴格要求
6

Chapter 6: App 1: HVAC Fundamentals

附錄1：HVAC 基礎 | p143-p158

ISPE Good Practice Guide: HVAC | p143 - p158

本章學習目標

  • 理解 HVAC 系統的三大基本任務：人員舒適、產品保護、環境安全，以及它們在 GMP 環境中的優先順序
  • 掌握影響產品品質的三個主要環境變數（溫度、濕度、空氣污染）與次要 HVAC 因素（壓差、換氣次數、氣流方向）之間的因果關係
  • 了解 Classified Space（潔淨分級空間）的國際分類體系差異（EU Grade A-D vs. FDA ISO 5/7/8），以及 ISPE 的統一 "Grade" 命名方式
  • 學會區分 Air Change Rate（換氣次數）與 Air Flow Rate（送風量）的本質差異，理解為何任意換氣次數可能不足或過量
  • 認識 Psychrometrics（空氣調節學）的基本概念：Sensible Heat（顯熱）、Latent Heat（潛熱）、Dry Bulb（乾球溫度）、Wet Bulb（濕球溫度）、Dew Point（露點）及其在 HVAC 設計中的應用
  • 理解 Differential Pressure（壓差）如何控制氣流方向，以及計算裂縫氣流速度的基本公式
7.1 Introduction（導論）

原文內容 Original Text

This Appendix introduces the fundamentals of HVAC systems that control a GMP workplace environment.

HVAC aims to make personnel comfortable and to protect both workers inside a facility and the environment outside a facility from airborne materials that could be hazardous. In pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities, there also is a specific requirement to control the impact of the environment on the finished product (to assure product quality).

Three primary room environment variables may affect product and processes (at "critical locations," i.e., locations where product is exposed or where cleaned product contact surfaces are exposed):

  • air temperature
  • RH
  • airborne contamination (viable and non-viable particles)

Some of these variables, such as local airborne contaminants, depend on secondary HVAC factors, for example:

  • room pressure
  • air changes
  • airflow volume
  • airflow direction
  • airflow pattern
  • exhaust location and air velocity
  • air filter efficiency

Tertiary HVAC variables, such as fan speed and duct pressure, may vary to satisfy these secondary HVAC factors, and therefore, have an effect on them.

This appendix explores these variables and how they affect product, patient, and personnel. It also introduces some basic HVAC terminology.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：HVAC 的三層變數架構

ISPE 將 HVAC 變數分為三個層級，這種層級思維對系統設計非常重要：

  • Primary Variables（一級變數）：溫度、濕度、空氣污染 — 這些直接影響產品品質，也是 GMP 法規關注的核心
  • Secondary Variables（二級變數）：壓差、換氣次數、氣流方向等 — 這些是達成一級變數控制的「手段」
  • Tertiary Variables（三級變數）：風機轉速、風管壓力等 — 這些是工程執行層面的操作參數

在 CDMO 日常營運中，品保人員關注一級變數，設施工程師調控二級變數，而維護技師監控三級變數。三者之間的因果鏈若斷裂，就會出現 OOS（Out of Specification）事件。

比喻說明 Analogy

把 HVAC 系統想像成一家餐廳的廚房通風系統：

  • 一級目標：讓客人（產品）不被油煙（污染）影響、保持舒適溫度
  • 二級手段：抽油煙機的位置、排風量、廚房與用餐區的氣壓差
  • 三級執行：馬達轉速、風管尺寸、風閥開度

你不會直接對客人說「我們的馬達是 3000 RPM」，你會說「廚房油煙不會飄到用餐區」— 這就是為什麼法規聚焦在一級變數上。

重點提示 Key Notes

Critical Locations（關鍵位置）是本節最重要的概念之一。指的是產品暴露在環境中、或已清潔的產品接觸面暴露的位置。在無菌充填（Aseptic Filling）中，這就是灌裝針頭到瓶口之間的區域。HVAC 系統的設計必須優先保護這些位置。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 在你的工廠中，哪些位置屬於 "critical locations"？這些位置的環境控制是否都已經被 HVAC 系統涵蓋？
  2. 如果空調系統的風機轉速下降（三級變數），這會如何影響換氣次數（二級）和最終的微粒控制（一級）？
7.2 What Is Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning?（什麼是暖通空調？）

原文內容 Original Text

Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) is the generic name given to a system that conditions the environment through the control of temperature, RH, air movement, and air quality (including fresh air, airborne particles, and vapors). HVAC systems can:

  • increase or decrease temperature
  • increase or reduce the moisture or humidity of the air
  • decrease the level of particulate or gaseous contaminants in the air

These capabilities are employed to protect personnel and product and for human comfort. HVAC systems also can protect the outdoor environment from hazardous material removed from a workplace environment via HVAC exhaust.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：HVAC 的四大功能

HVAC 的名稱本身就揭示了三大功能：Heating（加熱）、Ventilation（通風）、Air Conditioning（空氣調節）。但在製藥環境中，我們需要加上第四個隱性功能：

  • 溫度控制（升溫/降溫）
  • 濕度控制（加濕/除濕）
  • 空氣品質控制（過濾微粒/氣態污染物）
  • 環境保護（防止有害物質排放到外部環境）

最後一點容易被忽略，但在處理高效能活性藥物成分（HPAPI）的 CDMO 設施中，排氣處理是法規審查的重點。

重點提示 Key Notes

注意原文提到 HVAC 可以「保護外部環境」。這在處理高活性化合物、荷爾蒙類或抗癌藥物時特別重要。排氣系統必須配備適當的過濾裝置（通常是 HEPA 過濾器），確保有害粉塵不會排放到大氣中。這也是環保法規（如 COSHH）要求的一部分。

7.2.1 Personnel Comfort（人員舒適）

原文內容 Original Text

The primary role of HVAC systems is to protect personnel and product. The most common role is to make personnel comfortable.

Four criteria usually are considered for personnel comfort and safety:

  • Temperature
  • Humidity
  • Air quality (carbon dioxide (CO2) levels and odors)
  • Air movement (sense of air movement and unwanted "drafts")

7.2.1.1 Temperature and Humidity

Figure 2.1 shows two boxes that define comfort conditions (temperature and humidity) that personnel in the US generally find comfortable in winter and summer (ASHRAE Handbook). This standard varies across the world, e.g., in parts of the tropics, people prefer an office at 78°F (26°C) to one at 72°F (22°C).

Note: these are general guidelines; numerous factors affect these conditions, e.g., the type and variety of work being performed, the extent of personnel gowning, as well as individual preferences.

Generally, non-production areas should fall within the "comfort zone" shown in Figure 2.1.

Where gowning levels are heavier or where work is more intense, room conditions should be cooler and less humid than for office areas. Workers in industrial settings, especially those who are required to wear over-garments (gowning) in pharmaceutical facilities, could be uncomfortable working in office room conditions. Typically, room temperatures are cooler (about 20 to 21°C or 68 to 70°F) with room humidity below 60% to provide "comfort" conditions. In addition, the lower limit on comfort humidity often is set at 30% to minimize static charges and to avoid irritation of the throat that could lead to an increased risk of respiratory illness.

7.2.1.2 Air Movement

A sensation of gentle air movement often is preferable; a typical design figure of 0.1 m/s (0.3 ft/s) is used in an office environment. Greater air velocities (up to 1 m/s or 3.28 ft/s (200 ft/min)) usually are needed for product protection to capture airborne particles. In manufacturing environments, higher velocities may be needed where operators experience discomfort from heavier gowning.

7.2.1.3 Air Quality

Fresh air is required to dilute exhaled carbon dioxide, odors, and other environmental contaminants. The amount of fresh air required depends on personnel activities. Table 2.1 shows typical oxygen use for different levels of activity.

ASHRAE 62 states the amount of fresh air required to provide adequate IAQ in a non-contaminated workspace should be 15 to 20 cubic feet/min (CFM) or 24 to 32 cubic meters per hour per person; unless a complex ASHRAE analysis is performed. Local building codes may require different quantities.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：GMP 環境 vs. 辦公室環境

這一節的關鍵洞見是：製藥潔淨室的「舒適」標準與一般辦公室不同。穿著完整無塵衣的操作人員需要更低的溫度（20-21°C）和更低的濕度（<60%）。

ASHRAE Comfort Zone（舒適區）是美國暖通空調學會定義的人體感覺舒適的溫濕度範圍。但在製藥環境中，因為穿戴防護衣物，操作員的代謝熱散失受阻，需要額外降溫。

比喻說明 Analogy

想像你穿著冬天的羽絨衣在 25°C 的辦公室裡工作 8 小時 — 你一定會覺得非常悶熱。GMP 潔淨室裡的操作員穿著無塵衣（甚至雙層手套、面罩），就跟穿羽絨衣一樣。所以潔淨室溫度要比一般辦公室低 2-4°C 才能達到同等的舒適感。

重點提示 Key Notes

濕度下限 30% 的設計考量：

  • 靜電風險：低於 30% RH 時，靜電積累加劇。在粉末充填或含有易燃溶劑的區域，靜電可能引發安全事故
  • 人員健康：過於乾燥的空氣會刺激喉嚨黏膜，增加呼吸道疾病風險，而生病的操作員是潔淨室的污染源
  • 微粒產生：不舒適的操作員會產生更多微粒（從每分鐘 100,000 顆增加到數百萬顆）

公式與計算 Formula

新鮮空氣需求量（ASHRAE 62 標準）：

每人需要新鮮空氣量 = 15-20 CFM (24-32 m³/hr)

範例：若潔淨室同時有 10 名操作員
最低新鮮空氣需求 = 10 × 20 CFM = 200 CFM
                   = 10 × 32 m³/hr = 320 m³/hr

注意：這只是最低 IAQ 需求，
實際送風量通常遠大於此（因為還需要滿足潔淨度要求）

實務應用 Practical Application

CDMO 場景：某 CDMO 的 Grade B 區域操作員抱怨太熱。工程師發現房間溫度設定為 22°C（辦公室標準），但操作員穿著二級無塵衣。將溫度調降至 20°C 後，操作員的環境微粒監測數據明顯改善 — 因為舒適的人員會產生更少的微粒。這個案例說明：人員舒適不只是福利問題，更是產品品質問題。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的潔淨室溫度設定是多少？是否有考慮操作員的穿衣等級？
  2. 為什麼 ISPE 說「不舒適的操作員每分鐘可能散發數百萬顆微粒」？這對你的環境監測（EM）數據會有什麼影響？
  3. 在辦公室環境中空氣流速 0.1 m/s 就足夠，但產品保護需要 1 m/s — 這兩者之間的平衡在你的設施中是如何處理的？
7.2.2 Product and Process Considerations（產品與製程考量）

原文內容 Original Text

Products may be sensitive to temperature, humidity, and airborne contamination from outside sources or cross-contamination between products.

Process operators may need protection from exposure to hazardous airborne materials.

When considered critical, product environmental requirements normally are listed in a New Drug Application (NDA). Data from process development of a new drug may be available. The affects of conditions outside these ranges will depend on the duration of exposure; prolonged exposure time may affect product quality. Safety requirements for personnel exposure normally are found in Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDSs).

Control of airborne contamination should be considered and frequently is associated with temperature and humidity, e.g., the effect of temperature:

  • Comfortable personnel work more efficiently and are more productive. They also produce fewer environmental contaminants: a typical worker will discharge 100,000 particles a minute doing relatively sedentary work (particles sized 0.3 μm and larger (a human hair is approximately 100 μm in diameter)). A worker who is hot and uncomfortable may shed several million particles per minute in the size range, including a greater number of bacteria.

Additional ways in which environmental conditions inside a building can influence the product include, e.g., high humidity may increase microbial and mold growth rates on surfaces.

If environmental conditions inside are significantly different from environmental conditions outside a building and the fabric of the building has insufficient integrity, condensation can occur in interstitial spaces and can lead to microbial contamination problems and deterioration of the building.

In addition, protection of personnel depends on airflow direction both within and between rooms. Airflow can entrain particles of product or other hazardous materials harmful to operators. DP and the airflow it produces frequently are used to control the migration of airborne particulate between two rooms (to prevent cross-contamination between products).

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：環境條件與產品品質的雙向關係

這一節揭示了 HVAC 設計中一個關鍵的「雙向邏輯」：

  • 環境 → 產品：溫度、濕度、微粒直接影響產品品質
  • 人員 → 環境：不舒適的操作員會產生更多微粒，間接影響產品

Cross-contamination（交叉污染）在 CDMO 尤其重要，因為同一設施可能同時生產多種產品。壓差（DP）控制是防止交叉污染的主要 HVAC 手段。

比喻說明 Analogy

想像醫院的隔離病房：如果病房內的氣壓比走廊低（負壓），打開門時空氣會從走廊流向病房，防止病菌擴散到走廊。HVAC 的壓差控制原理完全相同 — 讓空氣永遠從「乾淨」流向「較不乾淨」的區域。在 CDMO 處理多產品時，每個產品區域就像一間「隔離病房」。

重點提示 Key Notes

人體微粒釋放量的驚人數據：

  • 安靜坐姿：約 100,000 顆/分鐘（0.3 μm 以上）
  • 悶熱不適時：數百萬顆/分鐘

這意味著不適的操作員會讓微粒產生量增加 10-50 倍！這就是為什麼人員舒適度不僅是人力資源議題，更是品質管理議題。CDMO 的環境監測（EM）異常事件中，有相當比例可以追溯到溫度控制不當。

重點提示：建築物結露風險

原文提到一個容易忽略的問題：Interstitial Condensation（牆體內結露）。當室內外溫差大、且建築密封性不足時，水蒸氣會在牆壁、天花板夾層內凝結。這不僅造成建築物損壞，更會成為黴菌和微生物的溫床。在台灣這樣的高濕度氣候下，這個問題尤其嚴重。CDMO 設施的建築維護檢查應包含牆體結露評估。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的設施中，NDA 文件有沒有明確規定產品的環境溫濕度範圍？如果沒有，你是如何決定潔淨室的溫濕度設定點的？
  2. 在多產品 CDMO 設施中，如果兩個相鄰房間都有產品暴露，壓差應該如何設定才能防止交叉污染？
7.2.3 Control of Parameters by HVAC Systems（HVAC 系統的參數控制）

原文內容 Original Text

7.2.3.1 Temperature and Humidity

HVAC systems control the temperature and humidity in a room by supplying that room with air in a condition that produces the desired temperature and humidity when mixed with the room air.

Thermal heat gains and losses to and from a space occur through three types of heat transfer:

  1. radiant
  2. conductive
  3. convective

These may occur as a result of the temperature from sunlight outside the facility and internal heat gains resulting from the process, equipment, lighting, and personnel.

Changes in humidity may arise because of processes, personnel, and the environment. Moisture migration through openings or porous walls into a controlled space from surrounding areas is governed by the difference in vapor pressure (as defined by Dalton's law) and may migrate against an air pressure differential.

7.2.3.2 Air Velocity

Air velocity in manufacturing spaces usually is higher than recommended for human comfort. Adequate velocity is vital to correct mixing (dilution) of air within the room and for the removal of airborne contaminants. The direction of airflow should protect operators as well as product. Air velocity that is too high can create eddies and vortices near operators and increase the risk of exposure to hazardous material.

7.2.3.3 Particulate, Fume, and Vapor Control

Filtration in HVAC systems usually is capable of removing almost the entire mass of particles. Filters vary in efficiency:

  • Low efficiency filters may remove much of the mass of particles, but this represents only 20 to 30% of the number of particles.
  • High Efficiency Particulate Air (HEPA) and Ultra Low Particulate Air (ULPA) filters remove almost the entire number of particles, but a very small number of nearly "massless" particles may pass through.

The majority of airborne particles are non-viable. A fraction (< 1%) of airborne particles are viable, e.g., bacteria and viruses; however, these can multiply. Viable particles travel with non-viable particles; therefore, controlling the total number of airborne particles also controls the number of viable particles.

Airflow patterns have a significant effect on the control of airborne contaminants, which may be removed by dilution or displacement (see Section 2.8), reducing contaminant levels through the use of exhaust and replacement (makeup) fresh air where necessary.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：三種熱傳遞方式

Thermodynamics（熱力學）的基礎知識：

  • Radiant（輻射）：太陽光透過窗戶加熱房間，或設備表面散發的紅外線熱輻射
  • Conductive（傳導）：熱量通過牆壁、地板等固體材料傳遞
  • Convective（對流）：空氣流動帶走或帶來熱量 — 這正是 HVAC 系統的主要工作方式

HVAC 設計師必須計算所有熱源（日照、設備、照明、人員），才能正確選配冷卻能力。

重點提示：水蒸氣 vs. 空氣壓差

原文中一個非常重要但容易被忽略的觀點：水蒸氣可以逆著空氣壓差遷移。這是因為水蒸氣的遷移遵循 Dalton's Law（道爾頓定律），由蒸氣壓差驅動，而非空氣壓差。

實務意涵：即使你的潔淨室維持正壓，水分仍可能從高濕度的外部環境滲透進來。這就是為什麼在高濕度氣候（如台灣）中，除了壓差控制外，還需要良好的建築密封性和除濕系統。

核心概念：HEPA vs. ULPA 過濾器

HEPA（High Efficiency Particulate Air，高效微粒空氣過濾器）：效率 ≥ 99.97%（對 0.3 μm 微粒）

ULPA（Ultra Low Particulate Air，超低微粒空氣過濾器）：效率 ≥ 99.999%（對 0.12 μm 微粒）

關鍵洞見：低效過濾器可能去除了大部分微粒的「重量」（質量），但只去除了 20-30% 的微粒「數量」。這是因為大量微小微粒雖然總質量輕，但數量極多。而 GMP 法規關注的是微粒數量，不是質量。

比喻說明 Analogy

想像你用一般的廚房濾網過濾沙子和水的混合物。濾網會擋住大顆的砂石（佔了大部分重量），但細小的泥沙（數量多但重量輕）全部通過了。HEPA 過濾器就像是用極細密的濾紙，連最小的泥沙都能攔截。這就是為什麼潔淨室必須使用 HEPA 而非一般過濾器。

重點提示：Viable = Non-viable 的搭便車者

原文的一個關鍵聲明：活性微粒（細菌、病毒）附著在非活性微粒上一起移動。因此，控制總微粒數量就等於控制活性微粒數量。這就是為什麼 GMP 法規同時要求監測 viable（活性）和 non-viable（非活性）微粒 — 它們是「一對搭便車的旅伴」。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 在你的設施中，主要的熱源有哪些？是否有進行過熱負荷計算？
  2. 為什麼過高的空氣流速反而可能增加操作員接觸有害物質的風險（提示：eddies and vortices）？
  3. 如果低效過濾器去除了 90% 的微粒質量但只去除了 25% 的微粒數量，這對 GMP 環境監測意味著什麼？
7.2.4 Limitations of HVAC Systems（HVAC 系統的局限性）

原文內容 Original Text

HVAC systems are not a substitute for good process, facilities, and equipment design or for good operating procedures. HVAC systems are not able to:

  • clean surfaces that are already contaminated
  • adequately control processes that generate excessive contaminants
  • compensate for improperly designed or maintained facilities

As viable particles, such as bacteria and viruses, travel with non-viable particles, HVAC systems can only help control biocontamination in a room by delivering clean air to that room. Where an HVAC system has been properly designed, constructed, and maintained, that system is unlikely to be either the cause or the solution for persistent contamination problems.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：HVAC 不是萬靈丹

這一節非常重要，ISPE 明確劃清了 HVAC 系統的能力邊界：

  • HVAC 不能清潔已經受污染的表面 — 這是清潔程序（CIP/SIP）的工作
  • HVAC 不能補償設計不良的設施 — 如果門沒有自動關閉、牆面有裂縫，再好的 HVAC 也無法維持潔淨度
  • HVAC 不能控制產生過量污染物的製程 — 需要從源頭控制（如密閉操作）

比喻說明 Analogy

HVAC 就像家裡的空氣清淨機。如果你不打掃地板、不清理垃圾，光靠空氣清淨機是不可能讓房間乾淨的。空氣清淨機只能處理空氣中的微粒，它沒辦法擦桌子或拖地板。同樣，HVAC 只能控制空氣品質，不能取代適當的設施設計和操作程序。

重點提示 Key Notes

原文最後一句話暗含了一個重要的調查邏輯：如果 HVAC 系統設計、建造和維護都正確，那麼持續性的污染問題不太可能是 HVAC 造成的，也不太可能靠 HVAC 來解決。

在 CDMO 的偏差調查中，人們經常第一反應就是質疑 HVAC 系統。但如果 HVAC 維護記錄和性能數據都正常，調查方向應該轉向：操作人員行為、清潔程序、設備設計或建築完整性。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 在你的設施中，過去一年的環境監測（EM）偏差中，有多少最終追溯到 HVAC 系統？有多少其實是操作或清潔問題？
  2. 如果一個房間頻繁出現微粒超標，而 HVAC 系統運作正常，你會從哪些方向進行調查？
7.3 Air Quality Fundamentals（空氣品質基礎）

原文內容 Original Text

7.3.1 Introduction

Pharmaceutical HVAC should control airborne contamination and needs to help to ensure the "...purity, identity and quality..." of the product (21 CFR Part 211). By introducing conditioned clean air, HVAC can help control temperature, humidity, and airborne particulates in a space within the requirements for a product. In order to determine the equipment needed at the HVAC system level, the purpose of air supplied to a room needs to be predefined.

Both the quality (temperature, humidity, cleanliness) and quantity of air introduced into a room affect its ability to maintain environmental conditions. The effects of physical layout (geometry), air velocity, and air volume should be considered to ensure effective ventilation.

7.3.2 Fundamentals of Ventilation

Ventilation is the movement and replacement of air for the purpose of maintaining a desired environmental quality within a space. The term "Ventilation" has two common uses:

  • It may refer to the movement or exchange of air through a space, which is responsible for the transport of airborne particles, the mixing or displacement of masses of hot or cold air, and the removal of airborne contaminants (e.g., vapors and fumes). In this use, ventilation may supply all outdoor ("fresh") air or may recirculate air from the building combined with some fresh air.
  • It may refer to the supply of "fresh" oxygen-rich air.

This Guide uses the definition only for movement or exchange of air through a space.

For pharmaceutical spaces, both "air change rates" and "ventilation rate" may be used. Appropriate filtration and attention to the physical airflow patterns in a space tends to be successful where pharmaceutical HVAC design.

The locations of air inlets and outlets relative to the location of sources of contamination/heat and to expected airflow obstructions are crucial to controlling contamination. The relationship between these factors is expressed in the "effective ventilation rate" for a space. This measure expresses the efficiency of the dilution air supply at removing contaminants expressed as a percentage of the theoretical performance with perfect (complete) dilution. For further information and calculations, see Appendix 9.

A comparison of the ventilation rates of various designs indicates that good air supply layout, good return/exhaust layout, and effective supply filtration may produce desired airborne particulate levels and recovery rates with less air change rates than used traditionally. The importance of room level airflow patterns in the control of contamination and the performance of classified spaces has lead to the application of computer modeling (Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD)) and testing to assure effective design. The WHO guidance 937 Annex 2 suggest that this be performed for oral dosage spaces, but the practice is common only for the most potent products.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：Ventilation 的雙重含義

Ventilation（通風）在製藥語境中有兩個含義：

  • 空氣交換：移動和替換房間內的空氣以控制污染物（本指引的主要定義）
  • 新鮮空氣供給：提供含氧的新鮮空氣

Effective Ventilation Rate（有效通風率）是衡量通風效率的關鍵指標，表示實際稀釋效果佔理論完美稀釋的百分比。一個 60% 的有效通風率意味著 40% 的送風被「浪費」在短路循環或死角區域。

比喻說明 Analogy

想像你在一個充滿煙霧的房間裡開窗通風。如果窗戶在房間的同一側（進風口和出風口太近），大部分新鮮空氣會直接從一個窗戶流到另一個窗戶，房間角落的煙霧幾乎不受影響 — 這就是低效通風。但如果窗戶在對角線位置，新鮮空氣會穿過整個房間才離開 — 這就是高效通風。HVAC 的進風口和回風口佈局原理完全相同。

重點提示：CFD 模擬的價值

Computational Fluid Dynamics（CFD，計算流體力學）已成為現代 HVAC 設計的重要工具。通過電腦模擬，工程師可以在施工前預測：

  • 房間內的氣流路徑和速度分佈
  • 微粒濃度的分佈圖（識別「熱點」）
  • 不同進風/回風佈局的效果比較
  • 操作員和設備對氣流的干擾影響

對 CDMO 而言，CFD 可以在設計階段就優化 HVAC 系統，避免施工後才發現問題需要昂貴的改造。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的設施在 HVAC 設計階段是否使用過 CFD 模擬？如果沒有，有沒有在試車時進行煙霧測試（smoke study）來驗證氣流模式？
  2. 21 CFR Part 211 要求確保產品的 "purity, identity and quality"。你能說明 HVAC 是如何分別支撐這三個要求的嗎？
7.3.3 Contamination Control（污染控制）

原文內容 Original Text

Pharmaceutical HVAC should help to:

  • prevent unwanted environmental contaminants from adversely affecting product
  • prevent products from contaminating each another
  • limit operator exposure to hazardous pharmaceutical compounds, ingredients, or reagent vapors
  • prevent hazardous materials being spread to the outdoor environment

Room contamination control generally is achieved by filtering the incoming air to ensure that it does not carry unwanted particles, and then introducing the air to the work space to mix with ambient air and dilute any contaminants. Contaminants may be removed more rapidly using displacement airflow of adequate velocity and direction (e.g., in a UFH, local extraction vent, or via non-aspirating diffusers) than with dilution ventilation.

The number and intensity of contamination sources in a room should be considered; if low, a displacement airstream may be more useful in controlling airborne contaminants than dilution.

The orientation of airflows can be aligned to protect product or personnel by sweeping across one or the other (or both) between the supply terminal and the extract point. Local (usually high level) supply or extraction, or complete enclosure of the process also can create a local environment that excludes or removes particulates. Local supply or extraction is considered most effective when located near the point of contaminant generation.

Pharmaceutical HVAC can help control contaminants within a space, but facilities should be designed with physical architectural features, such as airlocks, which limit the migration of contaminants.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：稀釋 vs. 置換

HVAC 控制微粒有兩種基本策略：

  • Dilution Ventilation（稀釋通風）：送入乾淨空氣，與房間內的污染空氣混合，降低整體污染物濃度。就像往一杯墨水裡不斷加清水。
  • Displacement Airflow（置換氣流）：用乾淨空氣從一端「推」走污染空氣，就像用乾淨水把墨水「沖」出去。效率更高，但需要更精確的氣流設計。

UFH（Unidirectional Flow Hood，單向氣流工作台）就是置換氣流的典型應用，空氣從 HEPA 過濾器垂直向下或水平吹過工作區域，把微粒「沖」離關鍵位置。

重點提示：HVAC 的四重保護

注意原文列出 HVAC 需要同時保護的四個面向：

  1. 產品免受環境污染（主要責任）
  2. 產品之間的交叉污染預防（CDMO 的核心挑戰）
  3. 操作人員免受有害物質暴露（職業安全）
  4. 外部環境免受有害物質排放（環境保護）

在設計 HVAC 系統時，這四個需求有時會互相衝突。例如：保護產品需要正壓，但保護操作員免受高活性藥物暴露則需要負壓。如何平衡？— 這就是 Containment HVAC 設計的核心挑戰。

實務應用 Practical Application

CDMO 場景：你的 CDMO 同時為客戶 A 生產青黴素類抗生素，為客戶 B 生產注射用蛋白質藥物。這兩個產品絕對不能交叉污染（青黴素過敏可致命）。HVAC 設計必須確保：(1) 兩個區域完全獨立的空調系統，(2) 走廊對兩個區域都保持正壓，(3) Airlock 作為物理屏障。這就是原文說的 "physical architectural features" 配合 HVAC 的典型案例。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 在你的設施中，哪些區域使用置換氣流（displacement），哪些使用稀釋通風（dilution）？為什麼？
  2. 你能列出至少三個「汙染源應在局部排風口附近」的例子嗎？
7.3.4 Classified Space（潔淨分級空間）

原文內容 Original Text

The concentration of total airborne particles and microbial contamination within the space is a key measurement of room environmental conditions for pharmaceutical operations, particularly for sterile products and some biopharmaceutical APIs. The target maximum reading for these measurements is referred to as the "classification" of the space.

Several similar systems have been communicated for the classification of space; however, there is no consensus between international regulators on a single terminology for classification:

  • the EMEA uses "Grades A to D"
  • the FDA refers to ISO levels (5, 7 and 8), but only "in-use" (there are no "at-rest" limits), and further adds bioburden limits for each ISO class

It would be cumbersome to apply EU and FDA GMP nomenclature to facilities and to explicitly state "ISO X, in-use with colony forming unit (CFU) limits of Y per cubic meter."

This Guide uses the term "Grade" followed by an ISO level number. Therefore, "Grade 7" meets ISO 7 (10,000 0.5 micron particles per cubic foot or 352,000 per cubic meter) in use only with bioburden limits of 10 per cubic meter. By comparison, a Grade 7 space looks much like a European Grade B space, but the European Grade (A, B, C, D) also has at-rest limits.

Notes:

  • There are small differences in numerical values between the US and European air classes.
  • The US particle levels are for the 'in operation' state only, but it is considered GEP to measure periodic at rest particle levels to monitor the overall health of a facility.
  • The US has no equivalent to EU Grade D although the term Controlled Not Classified (CNC) has been used in the pharmaceutical industry and is discussed in the ISPE Baseline® Guides for Sterile and Biopharmaceuticals. A CNC space may meet ISO 8 at rest without the use of HEPA filters if the airborne challenge is low.
  • Air quality for facilities that do not require classified spaces, (e.g., oral dosage, packaging, warehousing, closed biopharmaceutical, most APIs (except aseptic processing), and API intermediates) is described in the relevant ISPE Baseline® Guide.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：國際潔淨度分類體系

製藥界的潔淨度分類系統是一個常見的混亂來源。ISPE 嘗試用「Grade + ISO 數字」來簡化：

  • Grade 5 = ISO 5 in-use = EU Grade A = 舊制 Class 100
  • Grade 7 = ISO 7 in-use = 類似 EU Grade B = 舊制 Class 10,000
  • Grade 8 = ISO 8 in-use = 類似 EU Grade C = 舊制 Class 100,000
  • CNC = Controlled Not Classified = 類似 EU Grade D

重點提示：EU vs. FDA 的關鍵差異

對 CDMO 的實務影響非常大：

  • EU 有 "at-rest"（靜態）和 "in-operation"（動態）兩組限值，FDA 只有 "in-operation"
  • EU 有 Grade D（最低等級的分級空間），FDA 沒有對應等級
  • CDMO 如果同時為歐美客戶生產，必須滿足兩邊的要求 — 通常意味著需要同時滿足 at-rest 和 in-operation 的微粒限值

建議：CDMO 設施設計時應以較嚴格的標準為基準，才能同時滿足歐美法規要求。

比喻說明 Analogy

把潔淨室分級想像成飯店星級。EU 就像一個同時評估「打掃後」（at-rest）和「住客入住時」（in-operation）的評鑑系統。FDA 只評估「住客入住時」— 他們認為實際使用狀態才是真正重要的。ISPE 則說：「我們用 ISO 星級數字，但加上 FDA 的微生物限值」，試圖建立一個統一的語言。

圖表解讀 Table Analysis

Table 7.2 是一張非常重要的速查表，建議你列印出來放在辦公桌上。重點觀察：

  • Grade A/ISO 5 的 0.5 μm 微粒限值在 at-rest 和 in-operation 都是 3,520/m³ — 意味著這個等級幾乎不允許人員活動帶來的微粒增加
  • Grade B 在 at-rest 時微粒限值與 Grade A 相同（3,520），但 in-operation 允許高達 352,000 — 差了 100 倍！這就是為什麼 "recovery time"（恢復時間）這個概念如此重要
  • 活性微生物限值：Grade A 要求 <1 cfu/m³，幾乎是零容忍

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的設施目前使用哪套分類系統（EU Grade 或 ISO Class）？如果需要轉換到另一套系統，有哪些差異需要注意？
  2. 你的 CNC 區域（如果有）是否定期進行微粒監測？它的性能與 EU Grade D 相比如何？
  3. 如果你的 CDMO 要同時為 EU 和 US 客戶生產無菌產品，HVAC 系統應該設計到什麼等級才能同時滿足兩邊的要求？
7.3.5 Total Airflow Volume and Ventilation Rate（總送風量與換氣率）

原文內容 Original Text

The relationship between air change rate, ventilation rate, the air particle concentration in the space, and recovery rates from in-use to at-rest conditions should be considered.

The process that will be operated in a given space should be understood in order to determine the air flow rate (not air change rate) required to meet the stated room air classification (see Chapter 5 of this Guide).

Arbitrary air changes may be either excessive or insufficient. The airborne particle level depends on several factors. After a facility is commissioned, particle levels and room recovery should be sufficiently below specified limits, rather than having an arbitrary number of air changes.

7.3.5.1 Air Change or Air Flow?

Arbitrarily set air change rates often drive decisions regarding room size and airflows. This can have significant cost implications, but does not relate directly to the particle count in a room. Air change rates are more related to a room's ability to recover from an upset, not the room classification. The following is an explanation of this difference:

  • Assume a 1 cubic foot room containing an aseptic process that generates 10,000 particles per minute. If the room is purged with 1 CFM of clean air, the steady state (equilibrium) airborne particle level will be 10,000 particles per cubic foot (PCF). This 1 CFM creates an air change every 1 minute or 60 air changes per hour. This value (60/hr) is often assumed to be more than enough to keep a space well below 10,000 PCF.

Now put the same process into a 100 cubic foot volume and keep the airflow at 1 CFM, assuming good mixing occurs inside the room. Now the room sees an air change every 100 minutes or about 0.67 ac/hr. However, when the dilution is calculated, the equilibrium airborne particle counts are still 10,000 PCF (10,000 particles per minute/1 cubic foot per minute = 10,000 particles per cubic foot). Hence, average airborne particle counts are not determined by the air changes, but by the three factors (assuming perfect mixing, see Appendix 9):

  1. particles generated inside a space (PGR)
  2. quantity of dilution air supplied to a space (cubic volume per time) assuming adequate mixing in the room
  3. cleanliness of dilution air (assumed to be negligible in aseptic processing based on HEPA filtration)

A room receiving only 1 air change per hour will take several hours to recover from in-use to at-rest conditions. With a clean air supply of 20 air changes per hour, a 100-fold reduction in airborne particle levels can occur with less than 20 minutes recovery time, satisfying the European GMP requirement. See the ISPE Sterile Manufacturing Facilities Baseline® Guide.

For recovery, air changes are important; a rate of 20/hr often is used as the minimum for classified spaces.

For applicable oral dosage facilities, the WHO 40th report suggests 6 to 20 AC/hr with recovery of 20 minutes from in-use to at-rest conditions (to be defined by the User and verified at building commissioning).

Although "air change rate" is important, for pharmaceutical HVAC system design, greater benefit may be obtained from correct air filtration and attention to the airflow patterns in a space.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：換氣次數 vs. 送風量 — 關鍵區分

這是本附錄最重要的概念之一。ISPE 用一個精彩的數學例子說明：

  • Air Change Rate（換氣次數） = 送風量 ÷ 房間體積。它取決於房間大小，但不直接決定微粒濃度
  • Air Flow Rate（送風量） = 實際的清潔空氣供應量。這才是真正決定穩態微粒濃度的因素

結論：相同的送風量，放在大房間裡換氣次數低，放在小房間裡換氣次數高 — 但穩態微粒濃度完全相同！

公式與計算 Formula

穩態微粒濃度公式（假設完美混合）：

穩態微粒濃度 = PGR / Q

其中：
  PGR = Particle Generation Rate（微粒產生率，顆/分鐘）
  Q   = Air Flow Rate（送風量，CFM 或 m³/min）

範例一（1 ft³ 房間）：
  PGR = 10,000 顆/min，Q = 1 CFM
  濃度 = 10,000 / 1 = 10,000 PCF
  換氣次數 = 1 CFM / 1 ft³ = 60 次/hr

範例二（100 ft³ 房間）：
  PGR = 10,000 顆/min，Q = 1 CFM
  濃度 = 10,000 / 1 = 10,000 PCF（完全相同！）
  換氣次數 = 1 CFM / 100 ft³ = 0.67 次/hr

結論：換氣次數差了 90 倍，但微粒濃度完全一樣。
送風量（Q）才是控制穩態濃度的關鍵。

比喻說明 Analogy

想像你在浴缸和游泳池裡分別滴入一滴墨水，然後以相同的流量（例如每分鐘 1 公升）注入清水。浴缸的「換水次數」很高（因為體積小），游泳池的「換水次數」很低（因為體積大）。但最終，如果你持續以相同速率滴入墨水、注入清水，兩者的平衡墨水濃度是一樣的。差別在於：浴缸恢復到透明的速度比游泳池快得多 — 這就是為什麼 recovery time（恢復時間）才跟換氣次數相關。

重點提示：換氣次數的迷思

製藥業長期存在一個迷思：「換氣次數越高越好」。ISPE 明確指出這是一個昂貴的誤解：

  • 過高的換氣次數意味著更大的空調設備、更高的能耗、更多的 HEPA 過濾器更換成本
  • 換氣次數主要影響 recovery time（恢復時間），而非穩態微粒濃度
  • 20 次/hr 是分級空間的常見最低值，可在 20 分鐘內實現 100 倍的微粒下降
  • 正確的方法是：先確定 PGR 和目標濃度，再計算所需送風量，最後才知道換氣次數

實務應用 Practical Application

成本影響：某 CDMO 的 Grade 7 房間（50 m³）設計換氣次數為 40 次/hr（送風量 2000 m³/hr）。試車後發現 at-rest 微粒遠低於限值，recovery 只需 8 分鐘。將換氣次數降至 25 次/hr（1250 m³/hr），recovery 時間增加到 14 分鐘（仍在 20 分鐘要求內），但空調能耗降低約 35%。年節省電費和過濾器成本約數十萬台幣。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的分級空間的換氣次數是多少？是根據計算得出的還是「行業慣例」？
  2. 如果將換氣次數降低 25%，recovery time 會增加多少？是否仍能滿足法規要求？
  3. 你是否知道你的潔淨室的 PGR（微粒產生率）？如果不知道，是否有方法估算？
7.3.5.2 Particle Generation Rate（微粒產生率）

原文內容 Original Text

The PGR for an existing process may be calculated if the steady-state room particle count, the room supply airflow, and the supply airflow particle level are known (see Appendix 9). The calculated value of PGR can then be used for the same process in a new facility.

However, equipment manufacturers may not know the PGR of their equipment, necessitating HVAC designers to make conservative estimates and over-design room airflow. Data gathered during "water batching" (process simulation), but before actual production, may offer an opportunity to reduce airflow rates where in-operation airborne particle levels appear to be low (because of over-design).

When using empirical data for airborne particulate monitoring, it should be taken into consideration that particulate of the product being processed is not a contaminant. This is of particular interest in aseptic powder filling operations, where high particle counts may be associated with the filling process, but do not indicate failure of a cleanroom design. If an air volume reduction appears to be feasible, other critical HVAC parameters (temperature, RH, room DP, recovery, and at-rest levels) should be maintained.

Figure 7.2 gives an indication of particulates generated by personnel within a cleanroom. Although equipment in operation can generate many times more particles per minute, personnel are a primary source of viable contamination. Increased control of total particles released from personnel leads to an increased control of viable particles in a room.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：PGR 的實務意義

Particle Generation Rate（PGR，微粒產生率）是 HVAC 設計的關鍵輸入參數。如果你知道 PGR，就能精確計算所需的送風量。但現實中：

  • 設備廠商通常不提供 PGR 數據
  • HVAC 設計師被迫做保守估計，導致過度設計（over-design）
  • 過度設計 = 更大的設備 + 更高的能耗 + 更多的成本

好消息是：在 water batching（水模擬批次）階段可以實測 PGR，如果發現比估計低，有機會降低送風量來節省成本。

重點提示：產品粉塵不是污染物

這是一個非常重要但容易混淆的概念。在無菌粉末充填（Aseptic Powder Filling）操作中，微粒計數器可能顯示非常高的讀數，但這些微粒是產品本身的粉末，不是環境污染物。

這不代表潔淨室設計失敗。但要注意：即使產品粉塵本身不是污染物，高濃度的粉塵仍可能干擾環境監測的判讀。CDMO 需要建立明確的數據解讀標準，區分「產品相關微粒」和「環境污染微粒」。

比喻說明 Analogy

PGR 就像一個水龍頭漏水的速率。如果你知道漏水速率（PGR），你就能計算需要多大的排水管（送風量）來防止積水（微粒超標）。但如果你不知道漏水速率，你只能裝一根「應該夠大」的排水管 — 這就是過度設計。試車階段就像第一次開水龍頭測試：你終於知道實際漏水速率，可以決定是否可以換一根較小（較省錢）的排水管。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的設施在試車或 APS（無菌製程模擬）階段是否有收集 PGR 數據？這些數據是否被用來優化 HVAC 設計？
  2. 如果你的粉末充填線的微粒計數在操作中遠超 ISO 7 限值，但全部是產品粉塵，你會如何處理這個環境監測數據？
7.3.5.3 Impact of Unidirectional Airflow Hoods on Air Change Rates（單向氣流罩對換氣次數的影響）

原文內容 Original Text

Air leaving the processing space inside a hood is often significantly cleaner than the air of the room into which it moves. The relatively clean air from the hood may help, along with the supply air from the HVAC system to dilute airborne particles in the room.

In addition to reducing airborne particles, air flow from a hood may accelerate the recovery time of a room from in-use to at-rest conditions. The entire air flow from a hood may not be available to include in air change calculations because:

  • Short-circuiting of the air leaving the hood zone back (upward) to the hood inlet; the added dilution will affect only areas near the airflow path. If the hood inlet is near a room air supply outlet, that air could also short circuit into the hood without helping to mix air in the room.
  • Hood air may not be as clean as HVAC supply air. Even though the critical location under the hood might be rated as ISO 5, the air leaving the Grade 5 work space has collected additional contaminants from equipment and personnel outside the critical zone before passing back into the hood inlet.
  • Similar increases in room air cleanliness and recovery can be accomplished with HEPA-equipped FFUs operating inside a room. Short circuiting of filtered air back to the air intake may create only localized "super-clean" areas, as with UFHs.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：UFH/FFU 的換氣次數貢獻

Unidirectional Flow Hood（UFH，單向氣流罩）和 Fan Filter Unit（FFU，風機過濾單元）會在房間內產生額外的清潔氣流。但是，不能簡單地把這些氣流加到房間的換氣次數計算中。原因有三：

  • Short-circuiting（短路循環）：UFH 排出的空氣可能直接被自己的進風口吸回，沒有真正稀釋整個房間的空氣
  • 污染物積累：離開 Grade 5 工作區的空氣已經收集了設備和人員的微粒，不再是「乾淨」的
  • 局部效應：FFU 可能只在其正下方創造一個「超潔淨」區域，而非均勻改善整個房間

比喻說明 Analogy

想像你在一個充滿灰塵的房間裡放了一台桌上型空氣清淨機。清淨機會讓它附近的空氣變得很乾淨，但如果出風口和進風口太近，乾淨空氣會在清淨機周圍循環，房間的另一端還是很髒。你不能說「清淨機的風量 + 中央空調的風量 = 房間的總換氣量」，因為清淨機的風量只在局部有效。

重點提示 Key Notes

在 HVAC 確效（Qualification）時，需要驗證 UFH/FFU 對整個房間的實際影響：

  • 在 UFH 運作和關閉兩種狀態下分別測量房間微粒
  • 測量 UFH 出風口不同距離處的微粒濃度，繪製衰減曲線
  • 進行煙霧測試確認是否存在短路循環

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的潔淨室中有 UFH 或 FFU 嗎？在計算換氣次數時，你是否把它們的風量包含在內？
  2. 你有沒有做過煙霧測試來確認 UFH 的氣流是否存在短路循環？
7.3.6 Room Air Distribution and Quality of Incoming Air（房間空氣分佈與進氣品質）

原文內容 Original Text

Optimal layout for air inlet and outlet, and adequate filtration, can produce desired airborne particulate levels and recovery rates using lower air change rates than those used traditionally.

Using the recovery example in Section 2.3.5.1 of this Guide, faster recovery can be accomplished in a classified room where clean air supply is distributed over a high percentage of the ceiling (or via multiple non-aspirating diffusers) than by supplying the same total supply volume from one conventional air outlet. It is not necessary to create a "laminar flow ceiling." Numerous air outlets equally spaced, with equal flow rates, can create a "plug flow," a situation where air generally moves downward from ceiling to floor, but not at constant velocity. This can lead to faster recovery (often less than 10 minutes for 20 ac/hr) and also prevent "hot spots" of high particle count in a room. The resultant downward velocities are much less than those used in a unidirectional flow (Grade 5) area, and activity in the room may cause undesirable airflow patterns that should be investigated during commissioning. Numerous well-located low wall air returns can help prevent "dead zones" near the floor. (Generally, pharmaceutical facilities do not use perforated floors for air returns.)

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：Plug Flow vs. Laminar Flow

ISPE 提出了一個重要的設計理念：你不需要建造昂貴的「層流天花板」（laminar flow ceiling）來獲得良好的空氣分佈。

  • Laminar Flow（層流）：所有空氣以均勻、恆定的速度向同一方向移動。昂貴，需要整個天花板都是 HEPA 過濾器
  • Plug Flow（柱塞流）：空氣大致從天花板向地面移動，但速度不恆定。通過均勻分佈的多個出風口實現，成本更低

Non-aspirating Diffuser（非引射式散流器）是實現 plug flow 的關鍵設備，它將空氣以低速垂直向下送出，不會捲入周圍的空氣。

重點提示：Dead Zones 的風險

Dead Zones（死角區域）是指房間中空氣幾乎不流動的區域，通常在角落和地板附近。微粒會在這些區域積累，成為潛在的污染源。解決方案：

  • 在低牆位置設置多個回風口
  • 避免在角落堆放物品阻擋氣流
  • 在試車時使用煙霧測試識別死角

注意：製藥設施通常不使用穿孔地板作為回風口（與半導體晶圓廠不同），因為清潔和維護困難。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的潔淨室使用什麼類型的送風散流器？是傳統的方形散流器還是非引射式散流器？
  2. 你的設施在試車時是否進行過煙霧測試來識別 dead zones？
7.3.7 Airflow Direction and Pressurization（氣流方向與壓力控制）

原文內容 Original Text

Constructing a space that is absolutely airtight is considered impractical using normal construction techniques; therefore, alternative approaches are required to prevent airborne particulate migrating into or out of a space. A continuous flow of air in the desired direction through the cracks in building construction (door gaps, wall penetrations, conduits, etc.) can reduce transport of airborne particulates. A velocity of 100 to 200 FPM (0.5 to 1.0 m/sec) usually will capture and transport light powders and bioburden, assuming there are no strong drafts nearby (such as a worker passing quickly in front of a laboratory hood).

One method to control the direction of airflow is to control the relative pressurization of adjacent spaces, i.e., the DP between the spaces.

A simplified method (neglecting the orifice coefficient for the opening) to calculate the expected velocity of airflow through a "crack" (e.g., around a closed door) resulting from a given pressure differential is:

V = 4005 √VP   or   VP = (V/4005)²

Velocity (V) can be broken down to volume divided by area, giving V = Q/A:

VP = (Q/4005A)²

For metric units: VP (Pascals) = (Q/110A)²
(where Q is in m³/hr and A is in square meters)

Where:

  • 4005 is a conversion factor
  • V is velocity in ft/min
  • VP is velocity pressure, here assumed to be the room DP in inches wg
  • A is area of the opening in square feet
  • Q is airflow in Cubic Feet per Minute

Assuming room DP converts fully to VP through an opening (a conservative assumption), calculating the opening area, such as the crack area around a closed door between rooms, allows calculation of the airflow (CFM) required to create a pressure across that door, or the velocity that results from a known DP.

For A=1 sq foot (0.1 sq m) opening, 890 CFM (about 1500 m³/hr or 0.45 m³/sec) will create 0.05 inch wg (12.5 Pa) DP (V = Q/A = 890 FPM = 4.5 M/s)

Other "cracks" in the fabric separating a pressurized room from lower pressure spaces may not be accounted for using this calculation. This often leads to a requirement for more airflow to the room than predicted by the calculation to satisfy DP requirements. A common method which accounts for this additional airflow is to allocate 0.05 to 0.5 CFM per square foot of room surface, depending on construction and DP. This airflow can be minimized by constructing rooms tightly and testing for (and correcting) leaks during commissioning.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：Differential Pressure（壓差，DP）控制

Differential Pressure（壓差）是製藥 HVAC 中最重要的控制參數之一。核心原理：

  • 完全氣密的房間不可能用一般建築工法實現
  • 門縫、管路穿牆處、電線管路等都會有「裂縫」
  • 通過維持房間之間的壓差，讓空氣永遠從高壓（潔淨）側流向低壓（較不潔淨）側
  • 裂縫處的空氣流速需達到 100-200 FPM（0.5-1.0 m/s）才能有效阻擋微粒和微生物遷移

公式與計算 Formula

壓差-流速-風量關係公式：

Imperial:  V = 4005 × sqrt(VP)
           VP = (Q / (4005 × A))²

Metric:    VP(Pa) = (Q / (110 × A))²
           Q = m³/hr, A = m²

實例計算：
假設門縫面積 A = 0.1 m²（約 1 ft²）
目標壓差 VP = 12.5 Pa (0.05 inch wg)

Q = 110 × A × sqrt(VP)
Q = 110 × 0.1 × sqrt(12.5)
Q = 110 × 0.1 × 3.54
Q ≈ 39 m³/min ≈ 1500 m³/hr

即：僅一個門縫就需要約 1500 m³/hr 的
「額外」送風來維持 12.5 Pa 的壓差！

比喻說明 Analogy

壓差控制就像氣球：如果氣球有小孔，你需要不斷充氣來維持氣球的膨脹狀態（正壓）。小孔越多或越大，需要充氣的速率就越高。房間的門縫、管路穿牆孔就是「小孔」，送風系統就是「打氣筒」。建築施工越密封，需要的「充氣量」就越少，也就越省能源。

重點提示：隱藏的成本因素

壓差維持所需的「滲漏補償風量」是 HVAC 能耗的一個重要但經常被低估的來源：

  • 常見的估算方法：每平方英尺房間表面分配 0.05-0.5 CFM 的滲漏補償風量
  • 建築密封性越差，所需補償風量越大，能耗越高
  • 在試車期間測試並修補滲漏點是降低長期營運成本的最佳時機

CDMO 在評估廠房租賃或新建時，建築的氣密性品質直接影響 HVAC 營運成本。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的設施中，各潔淨室之間的壓差設定值是多少 Pa？這些值是如何決定的？
  2. 你是否知道你的潔淨室門縫的大致面積？這對維持壓差所需的送風量有什麼影響？
  3. 在試車階段，你的設施是否進行過氣密性測試並修補滲漏點？
7.3.8 Control of Operator Exposure to Potent and Hazardous Compounds（操作員暴露控制）

原文內容 Original Text

Modern pharmaceutical processing increasingly requires handling of potent active ingredients, requiring small dosages to produce a pharmacological effect, and may require the handling of other materials that can be hazardous to human health. The prevalence of these challenges creates a burden on the HVAC system and process engineers to control operator exposure and possible environmental discharge. Worker protection standards vary by jurisdiction, but Control of Substances Hazardous to Human Health (COSHH) regulations (this is a terminology coined in the UK, but common in the industry) are ubiquitous. These standards may specify 8 hour Operator Exposure Limits (OELs) for commonly used substances and require that manufacturers specify OELs for proprietary compounds. A hierarchy of control is expected; with substitution and control at the source being considered before Local Exhaust Ventilation (LEV) and other HVAC methods, followed by Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and administrative controls.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：操作員暴露控制的層級

處理高活性藥物成分（HPAPI）時，ISPE 強調一個控制層級（Hierarchy of Control）：

  1. Substitution（替代）：能否使用較不危險的替代物質？
  2. Source Control（源頭控制）：密閉容器、隔離器、封閉式轉料系統
  3. LEV（Local Exhaust Ventilation，局部排風）：在產塵點設置排風口
  4. HVAC 方法：整體房間的壓差和換氣控制
  5. PPE（Personal Protective Equipment，個人防護裝備）：面罩、呼吸器等
  6. Administrative Controls（行政管控）：輪班制度、暴露時間限制

重點：PPE 是最後一道防線，不應該是第一選擇。

重點提示：OEL 與 HVAC 設計的關係

Operator Exposure Limit（OEL，操作員暴露限值）直接影響 HVAC 設計。OEL 越低（藥物越強效），HVAC 系統需要提供的保護就越高：

  • OEL > 100 μg/m³：一般通風 + 基本 PPE 可能就足夠
  • OEL 10-100 μg/m³：需要 LEV + 負壓控制
  • OEL < 10 μg/m³：需要隔離器或密閉系統
  • OEL < 1 μg/m³：需要完全密閉的隔離器系統

對 CDMO 而言，接受高活性藥物訂單前，必須評估現有 HVAC 系統是否能提供足夠的保護。

實務應用 Practical Application

CDMO 商業決策：某 CDMO 收到一個 HPAPI 產品的生產詢價，OEL 為 0.5 μg/m³。現有設施沒有隔離器，只有開放式操作台和 LEV。投標前需要評估：(1) 改造 HVAC 系統的成本，(2) 購買隔離器的投資，(3) 操作員健康監測的額外成本。這些成本必須反映在報價中，否則會虧錢或違反職業安全法規。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的設施目前能處理的最低 OEL 等級是多少？如果客戶要求更低的 OEL，你需要什麼升級？
  2. 控制層級中，你的設施目前主要依賴哪一層？是否過度依賴 PPE 而不是工程控制？
7.4 Psychrometrics（空氣調節學）

原文內容 Original Text

7.4.1 Introduction

Psychrometrics is the science involving the properties of moist air (a mixture of dry air and water vapor) and the process in which the temperature or the water vapor content of the mixture are changed. A psychrometric chart is used to identify conditions of air and to illustrate the process of achieving the desired state of the controlled space. For further information, see Appendix 1. See sources such as the ASHRAE Fundamentals Handbook for a detailed discussion. This section contains a high level overview of psychrometrics.

7.4.2 Basic Properties of Air

Dry air is normally comprised of 78.1% nitrogen, 21% oxygen, and has trace amounts of 10 additional elements totaling 0.9%. Air is normally a mixture of dry air and water vapor. When moist air reaches a level at which it cannot hold more moisture, it is said to be, "saturated." The colder the air, the less moisture it can hold. Warmer air can hold larger quantities of moisture.

The moisture in air (its specific humidity) is measured in:

  • grains of moisture per pound of air (7,000 grains equal 1 pound)
  • grams of moisture per kg of air (SI units)

Air at 75°F (24°C) and 60% RH has a specific humidity of 78 grains of water per pound (7000 grains) of dry air (11g/kg).

A psychrometric chart (see Appendix 3) provides an overview of thermodynamic properties of air-water mixtures. If any two properties of the air mixture are known, the chart allows an engineer to determine its remaining properties. Air-water vapor mixtures have interrelated psychrometric properties that can be plotted on the chart. The values on the chart are for a given barometric pressure (usually assumed to be at sea level); the values will be different at substantially different elevations.

7.4.2 (continued) Sensible and Latent Heat

Sensible (dry) heat causes a change in the temperature of a substance. Sensible heat can be "sensed" or felt and quantified by measurement with a dry bulb thermometer. Addition or removal of sensible heat will cause the measured air temperature to rise or fall.

On the psychrometric chart, sensible heat shows as temperature, as a horizontal line with a scale increasing from left to right. As mixed air is heated with only sensible heat, there is no resulting change in the amount of water vapor in the air.

Latent heat is the heat of vaporization carried by the moisture in the air/water mixture. Changes in latent heat are not detected with a dry bulb thermometer. The addition of water vapor to air may increase the humidity of the air without changing the temperature of the air, for example:

  • If sufficient latent heat is added to water in its liquid state, it will change state into a vapor (or steam) by evaporation:
    • The change of state caused by heating a liquid to steam is called the latent heat of vaporization.
    • The change of state caused by cooling from a steam to a liquid is called the latent heat of condensation.
    • The change of state from a liquid to a solid (ice) is called the latent heat of fusion.
    • The change of state from a solid to liquid is called the latent heat of melting.

Latent heat (mass of water per mass of dry air) appears on the psychrometric chart as horizontal lines on a vertical scale.

RH is the amount of moisture in the air versus the air's capacity to hold moisture. As warmer air can hold more moisture, sensible heating of air reduces the RH, and vice versa. Generally, at room temperature, it is assumed that each degree Fahrenheit change in temperature will yield a 2% change in RH. RH is a useful measure as it is related to the vapor pressure of water in the air, and therefore, reflects the tendency of water (in its liquid state) or moist surfaces to lose moisture to the air. Human comfort is more closely associated with RH than specific humidity, because of this drying effect on skin and mucus membranes.

7.4.3 Psychrometric Properties of Air

See Appendix 3 for a discussion of the terms used in Psychrometrics and for an explanation of the Psychrometric Chart.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：Psychrometrics 空氣調節學

Psychrometrics（空氣調節學）是研究濕空氣（乾空氣 + 水蒸氣）性質的科學。對 HVAC 工程師來說，這是設計空調系統的數學基礎。核心概念：

  • Dry Bulb Temperature（乾球溫度）：普通溫度計測量的溫度，反映 Sensible Heat（顯熱）
  • Wet Bulb Temperature（濕球溫度）：包裹濕布的溫度計測量的溫度，反映蒸發冷卻效果
  • Dew Point（露點）：空氣冷卻到開始凝結水滴的溫度
  • Specific Humidity / Humidity Ratio（含濕量）：每公斤乾空氣含有多少克水蒸氣
  • Enthalpy（焓）：空氣中的總能量（顯熱 + 潛熱）
  • Relative Humidity, RH（相對濕度）：目前含水量與飽和含水量的比值

關鍵工具：Psychrometric Chart（焓濕圖）— 只要知道任意兩個參數，就能從圖上讀出所有其他參數。

核心概念：Sensible Heat 顯熱 vs. Latent Heat 潛熱

這是 HVAC 設計中最基礎也最重要的區分：

  • Sensible Heat（顯熱）：改變溫度但不改變含水量的熱量。你「感覺」得到溫度變化。在焓濕圖上是水平移動。
  • Latent Heat（潛熱）：改變含水量（相態變化）但不改變溫度的熱量。你「感覺」不到，只有濕度計能測到。在焓濕圖上是垂直移動。

潛熱的四種形態：

  • Latent Heat of Vaporization（蒸發潛熱）：液態 → 氣態
  • Latent Heat of Condensation（凝結潛熱）：氣態 → 液態
  • Latent Heat of Fusion（融化潛熱）：液態 → 固態
  • Latent Heat of Melting（熔化潛熱）：固態 → 液態

比喻說明 Analogy

顯熱 vs. 潛熱的最佳比喻：

想像你在煮一鍋水。從冷水加熱到 100°C 的過程中，你可以看到溫度計一直上升 — 這就是顯熱。但水到了 100°C 後，你繼續加熱，溫度計不動了！所有的熱量都用來把水變成蒸氣 — 這就是潛熱。在 HVAC 中，空調的冷卻盤管需要同時處理顯熱（降溫）和潛熱（除濕），這就是為什麼 HVAC 設計需要分別計算這兩種熱負荷。

公式與計算 Formula

溫度-相對濕度近似關係：

經驗法則（室溫範圍）：
  溫度每升高 1°F → RH 下降約 2%
  溫度每下降 1°F → RH 上升約 2%

範例：
  房間初始條件：72°F, 50% RH
  加熱至 77°F（升高 5°F）
  新的 RH ≈ 50% - (5 × 2%) = 40%

這就是為什麼冬天暖氣開得越大，
空氣越乾燥 — 溫度上升，RH 自動下降。

含濕量範例：
  75°F (24°C), 60% RH 的空氣
  Specific Humidity = 78 grains/lb = 11 g/kg
  （7,000 grains = 1 pound）

重點提示：高海拔的影響

原文提到焓濕圖的數值假設在海平面氣壓下。在高海拔地區（如墨西哥城 2,240m、昆明 1,890m），氣壓較低，空氣密度較小：

  • 相同體積的空氣含有較少的氧氣和較少的水蒸氣容量
  • 風機需要更大的體積流量來達到相同的質量流量
  • 標準焓濕圖的數值需要修正

對在不同海拔地區設有工廠的 CDMO 而言，HVAC 設計不能直接複製 — 需要根據當地氣壓重新計算。

實務應用 Practical Application

CDMO 場景 — 除濕設計：你的潔淨室需要維持 20°C / 45% RH。外部空氣是 35°C / 80% RH（台灣夏天）。空調必須：(1) 先大幅冷卻空氣到露點以下，讓水蒸氣凝結（處理潛熱），(2) 再加熱到 20°C（處理顯熱）。這個「先冷後熱」的過程消耗大量能源，是台灣 CDMO 能耗的一大來源。了解 Psychrometrics 可以幫助你評估不同除濕策略（轉輪除濕 vs. 冷卻除濕）的能效差異。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你能從焓濕圖上讀出 20°C / 50% RH 空氣的露點溫度嗎？這對冷卻盤管的設計溫度有什麼意義？
  2. 為什麼人體的舒適感更接近 RH 而非含濕量（Specific Humidity）？
  3. 在你的設施中，HVAC 的能耗中，顯熱和潛熱處理各佔大約多少比例？

本節重點回顧 Section Summary

  • HVAC 三層變數架構：一級（溫度、濕度、微粒）直接影響產品，二級（壓差、換氣次數、氣流方向）是控制手段，三級（風機轉速、風管壓力）是操作參數。三者之間存在明確的因果鏈。
  • HVAC 的局限性：HVAC 不能清潔已受污染的表面、不能補償不良的設施設計、也不能控制產生過量污染物的製程。HVAC 只是污染控制策略（CCS）的一部分。
  • 換氣次數 vs. 送風量：穩態微粒濃度由 PGR（微粒產生率）和送風量（Q）決定，與換氣次數無直接關係。換氣次數主要影響 recovery time（恢復時間）。任意設定的換氣次數可能導致過度設計和不必要的成本。
  • 潔淨分級差異：EU 使用 Grade A-D（含 at-rest 和 in-operation 限值），FDA 使用 ISO 5/7/8（僅 in-operation）。CDMO 同時服務歐美客戶時，應以較嚴格標準為設計基準。
  • 壓差控制：通過維持房間間的壓差（DP），迫使空氣從潔淨區流向較不潔淨區。建築密封性直接影響維持壓差所需的額外送風量和能耗。
  • Psychrometrics 空氣調節學：Sensible Heat（顯熱）改變溫度，Latent Heat（潛熱）改變含水量。Psychrometric Chart（焓濕圖）是 HVAC 設計的核心工具，只需知道任意兩個參數即可確定空氣的所有性質。
  • 操作員暴露控制層級：從源頭控制（替代、密閉）到工程控制（LEV、HVAC）再到 PPE，層級越高越優先。PPE 永遠是最後一道防線，不應作為第一選擇。
7

Chapter 7: App 2: HVAC Applications

附錄2：HVAC 應用 | p159-p198

ISPE Good Practice Guide: HVAC | p158 - p178

本章學習目標

  • 了解 Air Handling Unit（AHU，空調箱）的組成元件及各元件對環境參數（溫度、濕度、壓差、氣流量、空氣潔淨度）的影響
  • 掌握加熱盤管（Heating Coil）、冷卻盤管（Cooling Coil）、加濕器（Humidifier）、除濕器（Dehumidifier）的功能與設計注意事項
  • 理解空氣過濾系統的分級：Pre-filter → Intermediate Filter → Final Filter → Terminal HEPA 的保護邏輯
  • 比較三種基本 HVAC 系統類型：Once-Through（全新風）、Recirculated（再循環）、Exhaust（排氣）的優缺點與適用場景
  • 了解 AHU 的 Blow-Through 與 Draw-Through 兩種配置的特性差異
  • 掌握壓差控制策略：Cascade（梯級）、Pressure Bubble（正壓氣泡）、Pressure Sink（負壓阱）三種氣鎖壓力配置
  • 理解送風分配設計：Dilution（稀釋式）vs. Displacement（置換式）的選擇與 GMP 分級要求

本節目錄 Table of Contents

8.1.1 Introduction 8.1.2 AHU 8.1.7 Heating Coil 8.1.8 Cooling Coil 8.1.9 Humidifier 8.1.10 Dehumidifier 8.1.11 Air Filtration 8.2.2 Number of AHUs 8.2.3 System Types 8.2.4 AHU Configurations 8.3 Pressure Control 8.3.3 Airlocks 8.3.4 DP & Air Leakage 8.4 Ventilation Strategies
8.1 Equipment / 8.1.1 Introduction（設備總論）

原文內容 Original Text

8.1.1 Introduction

HVAC equipment helps to meet the user requirements for room environmental conditions. HVAC equipment serving GMP areas is intended to work in conjunction with associated controls and sequences of operation systems to:

  • maintain room temperature
  • maintain room pressurization and DP relationships; therefore, assisting in the prevention of contamination and cross-contamination
  • minimize airborne contamination delivered to the conditioned space by HVAC systems
  • provide make-up air for ventilation and room pressurization
  • maintain RH by adding to or removing from the moisture content of the air
  • provide required air flow volumes to maintain room cleanliness classification and recovery rate, when required

Figure 8.1 illustrates the possible arrangement of components in an HVAC system for a draw-through, recirculating air handler with nearly all possible components. (Note that the arrangement is not considered preferable; it is for illustration only.)

8.1.2 Air Handling Unit

An Air Handling Unit (AHU) is an equipment package that includes a casing box (usually metal), a fan or blower, heating and cooling coils, air filtration, etc. to provide HVAC to duct systems and then to a building. Access doors or panels are usually provided for maintenance of each component.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：HVAC 設備的六大任務

HVAC 設備在 GMP 環境中不只是「吹冷氣」，它承擔六項關鍵任務：

  1. 溫度控制——維持製程和人員舒適所需溫度
  2. 壓差維持——透過 DP（Differential Pressure，壓差）關係防止交叉污染
  3. 空氣潔淨——最小化 HVAC 系統本身帶入的微粒污染
  4. 新風補充——提供通風和壓差所需的外氣
  5. 濕度調節——控制相對濕度（RH, Relative Humidity）
  6. 換氣量——維持潔淨等級和恢復率（Recovery Rate）

核心概念：什麼是 AHU？

AHU（Air Handling Unit，空調箱）是一個整合式設備包，包含：

  • 金屬外殼箱體（Casing Box）
  • 風機或鼓風機（Fan/Blower）
  • 加熱和冷卻盤管（Heating/Cooling Coils）
  • 空氣過濾器（Air Filtration）
  • 維護用檢修門/面板（Access Doors/Panels）

AHU 就像是 HVAC 系統的「心臟」，將空氣調節到需要的狀態後，透過風管系統輸送到各個房間。

比喻說明：AHU 就像一台多功能淨水器

想像一台大型淨水器：水進來先過濾（空氣過濾），然後加熱或冷卻到適當溫度（盤管），再用泵浦（風機）把處理好的水送到各個水龍頭（房間）。AHU 對空氣做的事情和淨水器對水做的事情完全一樣——過濾、調溫、調濕、加壓輸送。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為什麼 ISPE 特別強調 HVAC 系統要「最小化自身帶入的微粒污染」？HVAC 系統本身可能產生哪些污染？
  2. AHU 的檢修門/面板（Access Doors）為什麼對 GMP 很重要？如果維護困難會有什麼後果？
8.1.3-8.1.6 Fan, Mixing Box, Energy Recovery, Fume Exhaust（風機、混合箱、能量回收、排氣系統）

原文內容 Original Text

8.1.3 Fan

A fan is a driven air moving device used to supply, return, or exhaust/extract air to or from a room through ductwork to move air in sufficient amounts to provide ventilation, heating, or cooling or to overcome room air pressure losses.

8.1.3.1 Supply Fan: Air handlers have a supply fan to provide the motive force to distribute air throughout an HVAC system.

8.1.3.2 Return Fan: Most large recirculating air systems use a return air fan. This fan allows return duct pressure and flow to be managed independently from the supply. This is particularly important if the duct system has volume controllers on both the supply and return (such as for controlled supply air volume and room pressure control). It also allows the return air to be diverted to exhaust when outside air conditions are closer to desired discharge conditions than return air (economizer cycle) or when return air contains flammables.

8.1.4 Mixing Box

Common in recirculating air systems, the return air is mixed with outside air for pressurization and fresh air ventilation. The resulting air stream is referred to as mixed air. In very cold environments, the mixed air may "stratify" and not mix well with return air, leading to errors in temperature readings and potential for partial freeze-up of heating coils (even a steam heating coil can freeze). An internal turbulence-inducing device (air blender) can assure thorough mixing and avoid temperature stratification.

8.1.5 Energy Recovery Coil

Once-through air systems, or other systems with high amounts of expensive exhausted air, may employ an energy recovery coil to return a portion of the energy lost in the exhausted air to the incoming air. These coils typically are upstream of other supply air conditioning coils, and may be placed upstream of the intake air filters to melt snow in cold climates. These systems also may employ a bypass damper to decrease pressure drop caused by the coil when energy recovery is not advantageous.

8.1.6 Fume Exhaust/Extraction System

This is a system made up of ductwork, fans, and possibly air cleaners (filters, dust collectors, scrubbers, carbon adsorbers, etc.) that discharges unwanted or contaminated air to the outside atmosphere to a safe distance to avoid re-entrainment of exhausted materials in other HVAC systems, and to avoid exposure to people.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：Supply Fan vs. Return Fan

送風機（Supply Fan）提供空氣流動的動力，將調節後的空氣送到各房間。回風機（Return Fan）則獨立管理回風側的壓力和流量。

為什麼需要 Return Fan？在大型再循環系統中，如果只靠 Supply Fan，回風側的壓力和流量無法獨立調節。特別是當系統有變風量控制器（VAV）時，回風機可以獨立應對房間壓差變化。

Economizer Cycle（節能器循環）：當室外空氣的溫濕度條件接近需要的送風條件時，可以增加外氣比例，減少冷卻能耗——直接用「免費冷氣」。但此模式通常只用於非壓差控制區域（辦公室、倉庫）。

重點提示：混合箱的溫度分層風險

在寒冷環境中，混合箱內的冷外氣和暖回風可能無法均勻混合，形成溫度分層（Temperature Stratification）：

  • 上層暖空氣 + 下層冷空氣 → 溫度感測器讀數不準確
  • 局部低溫 → 加熱盤管局部結凍（即使是蒸汽盤管也可能結凍！）
  • 解決方案：安裝 Air Blender（空氣混合器），利用渦流強制混合

這在台灣較少遇到，但如果你的 CDMO 在北美或歐洲有工廠，冬季防凍設計是關鍵。

比喻說明：能量回收盤管就像熱交換型抽油煙機

在全新風系統中，你花了大量能源把室外空氣調節到 22°C / 45% RH，然後全部排出去——就像把洗好的熱水直接倒掉。Energy Recovery Coil（能量回收盤管）就像家用熱交換型全熱交換器（HRV/ERV），用排出的空氣先「預熱」或「預冷」進來的新空氣，回收 50-80% 的能量。

重點提示：排氣系統的 Re-entrainment 風險

Re-entrainment（再吸入）是排氣系統設計中最容易被忽略的風險——排出的污染空氣被其他 HVAC 系統的進氣口吸回來。ISPE 要求排氣要排到「安全距離」，實務上意味著：

  • 排氣口與進氣口之間有足夠的距離和高度差
  • 排氣口應在屋頂以上，且遠離新風口
  • 考慮主導風向的影響
8.1.7 Heating Coil（加熱盤管）

原文內容 Original Text

A Heating Coil is a heat transfer device consisting of a coil of piping, covered with heat-transfer fins, which increases the sensible heat transfer into an air stream, using steam, hot water, glycol, or sometimes hot refrigerant gas as the heating medium. An electric air-heating element also can be called a "heating coil."

8.1.7.1 Preheat Coil

Once-through air systems or other systems with high amounts of cold outside air may employ a preheat coil to condition the incoming or mixed air. These coils are positioned upstream of cooling coils to protect them from freezing and may be placed upstream of the filters to melt airborne snow. As these coils do not typically impose a large pressure drop, a bypass damper is not common. Care should be taken to avoid freezing preheat coils if the temperature of the air mixture entering them is below freezing. In warm weather, the coil's heating is turned off.

8.1.7.2 Reheat Coil

Systems that require over-cooling for humidity control (in place of desiccant dehumidification) also may employ a reheat coil to avoid overcooling of the space. By heating air leaving cooling coils, reheat coils decrease the RH of air leaving the AHU to avoid condensation in air filters or in the ductwork.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

比喻說明：Reheat 的邏輯——先冷再熱

「先冷卻再加熱」聽起來很浪費，但想像一下：你洗完澡後浴室鏡子都是霧氣（高濕度），你用除濕機（冷卻盤管）降溫把水分凝結出來，但空氣變太冷了。這時用 Reheat Coil 再加熱，就能得到溫暖又乾燥的空氣。

藥廠的許多製程（如吸入劑、乾粉產品）對濕度要求嚴格，「先冷後熱」是最簡單的除濕方式。

重點提示：Preheat Coil 防凍設計

ISPE 特別警告：如果進入預熱盤管的空氣溫度低於冰點，必須採取防凍措施。即使是蒸汽盤管（通常被認為不會凍）也可能因為控制閥故障或蒸汽壓力下降而在局部結凍。常見防凍方法包括：

  • 使用乙二醇循環而非純水
  • 安裝 Freezestat（防凍溫控開關）
  • 確保蒸汽盤管有足夠的凝結水排放能力

核心概念：三種加熱盤管的角色

加熱盤管看似簡單，但在 HVAC 系統中有三種不同角色：

加熱介質可以是蒸汽（Steam）、熱水（Hot Water）、乙二醇（Glycol）、熱冷媒氣體，或電加熱元件。

8.1.8 Cooling Coil（冷卻盤管）

原文內容 Original Text

A cooling coil is a heat transfer device consisting of a coil of piping, covered with heat-transfer fins, which reduces the sensible heat and possibly latent heat (via condensation of water vapor) in the air stream using chilled liquid or refrigeration gas as the cooling medium. Cooling to maintain environmental conditions is common in pharmaceutical applications. Cooling coils may be located upstream or downstream of the fan (draw-through versus blow-through). A cooling coil is a common method of lowering air humidity; therefore, air velocity and drainage from these coils are key design issues. Mist eliminators may be employed to eliminate carryover of liquid water droplets that condense on the coil. These coils impose a large pressure drop, but a bypass damper (used when cooling is not needed) can add a risk of unconditioned air leakage around the coil when maximum cooling is needed.

8.1.8.1 Re-cool Coil (Post-cooling Coil)

These coils may be installed downstream of desiccant dehumidifiers to eliminate excess sensible heat in the supply air. They may provide additional dehumidification downstream of a condensing cooling coil, operating below chilled water temperature using a refrigerant or a low temperature brine (typically water and glycol (ethylene or propylene)). Mist eliminators may be employed on these coils. Coils operating at sub-freezing internal temperatures can eventually ice over, and an alternation bypass/de-icing scheme is normally needed.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：冷卻盤管的雙重任務

Cooling Coil（冷卻盤管）同時處理兩種熱負荷：

  • Sensible Heat（顯熱）：降低空氣溫度
  • Latent Heat（潛熱）：將水蒸氣凝結成液態水，降低濕度

這就是為什麼冷卻盤管是 AHU 中最常見的除濕方式。關鍵設計議題：

  • Air Velocity（風速）：過快會把凝結水滴吹向下游
  • Drainage（排水）：凝結水必須順暢排出，否則滋生微生物
  • Mist Eliminator（除霧器）：攔截被氣流帶走的水滴

重點提示：Bypass Damper 的兩面刃

冷卻盤管壓降大，不冷卻時用 Bypass Damper 旁通可以節省風機能耗。但 ISPE 警告：需要最大冷卻時，旁通風門可能發生未調節空氣洩漏——部分空氣繞過盤管直接進入送風系統，導致溫度和濕度控制失敗。

在 GMP 環境中，建議優先考慮：不使用 Bypass Damper，或確保其密封性能經過確認。

重點提示：Re-cool Coil 的結冰風險

Re-cool Coil（再冷卻盤管）運行在冰水溫度以下（使用冷媒或低溫鹽水），盤管內部溫度可能低於冰點，導致盤管表面結冰。解決方案是交替旁通/除冰循環（Alternation Bypass/De-icing Scheme）——兩組盤管輪流工作，一組除冰時另一組繼續冷卻。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 一間乾粉吸入劑（DPI）生產間要求 RH < 25%，冷卻盤管的除濕能力是否足夠？什麼時候需要加裝乾燥劑除濕器（Desiccant Dehumidifier）？
  2. 冷卻盤管的凝結水排水如果堵塞，對 GMP 合規性有什麼影響？
8.1.9 Humidifier（加濕器）

原文內容 Original Text

Humidifiers increase the humidity within a controlled space by the discharge of water vapor (steam or water mist) into a supply air stream or directly into a room. Systems in cold or arid climates may employ a humidifier to inject water vapor to increase the moisture level of the air supply. These devices typically are downstream of the preheating coil, and may be mounted in ductwork where air turbulence and high velocity promote absorption of water vapor. When employed in an AHU, mounting upstream of a cooling coil provides a natural baffle to prevent carryover of liquid water droplets, as it is unlikely that both humidifier and dehumidification through cooling will be in use at the same time.

Generally, the water source is steam, potable water, or demineralized water (produced via reverse osmosis (RO), ion exchange resins, or distillation) that will not introduce objectionable contaminants into a room. It is common practice to use steam that is free of volatile additives in pharmaceutical manufacturing HVAC systems. Volatile additives that meet USP requirements can be considered, allowing direct use of plant steam in pharmaceutical manufacturing HVAC systems. Plant steam (which may contain amines or other volatile additives, necessary to prevent corrosion in steam piping) frequently is used for non-production spaces.

Facilities may use clean steam, generated from USP purified water or Water For Injection (WFI) (also called pure steam) for humidification. This practice is considered wasteful, as steam of adequate cleanliness for classified spaces may be obtained from copper, brass, or stainless steel re-boilers fed with water of adequate quality and heated by higher pressure plant steam. However, it may make sense to use clean steam if it is the most readily available form of steam. Maintenance should be considered, as purified steam or steam free from additives can corrode piping. Softened or demineralized water are commonly used for this service, as minerals in potable water can be left behind in the steam generator.

Water mist humidifiers are usually fed with deionized or purified water to prevent the carryover of dissolved mineral solids into an air stream. When a humidifier is not in use, additional care is required to prevent growth of bacteria in an unprotected water system.

It is common practice to install a length of welded, liquid tight stainless steel (or other corrosion resistant) ducting downstream of in-duct humidifiers to prevent damage and rust from condensation. Generally, duct humidity should be kept significantly below saturation level; 80% RH is a common upper limit.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：加濕器的水源選擇——GMP 的關鍵決策

加濕器向空氣中噴入水蒸氣或水霧，水源品質直接影響產品安全。ISPE 列出的水源選項（從最嚴格到最寬鬆）：

  1. Clean Steam（潔淨蒸汽）：由 USP 純化水或 WFI 產生。ISPE 認為用於 HVAC 加濕是浪費（wasteful）
  2. Re-boiler 蒸汽：用銅/黃銅/不鏽鋼再沸器，以適當品質的水為原料，被工廠蒸汽加熱產生。這是 ISPE 推薦的折衷方案
  3. 無揮發性添加劑的工廠蒸汽：可直接用於製藥生產區
  4. 含添加劑的工廠蒸汽（Plant Steam）：含胺類防腐劑，只能用於非生產區

比喻說明：蒸汽品質就像飲水標準

想像你要在餐廳噴水霧降溫：用自來水就好，不需要用瓶裝礦泉水。但如果你是在手術室噴水霧，至少要用 RO 水。用 WFI 蒸汽做 HVAC 加濕，就像拿著 Evian 礦泉水洗地板——品質夠好但實在浪費。ISPE 建議用 Re-boiler 產生的蒸汽，就像在手術室用品質足夠的 RO 水——既安全又經濟。

重點提示：加濕器停用時的微生物風險

ISPE 特別提醒：加濕器不使用時，殘留水分系統如果沒有適當保護，極容易滋生細菌。這在季節性使用的加濕器上尤其危險——冬天用完，春夏閒置的管路變成微生物培養皿。

最佳做法：不使用時排空系統、定期消毒、或使用 SIP（Steam In Place）設計。

重點提示：風管內加濕的上限——80% RH

風管內的相對濕度不能接近飽和點，否則會在風管壁面、過濾器上形成凝結水，導致：

  • 金屬風管腐蝕（所以加濕器下游要用不鏽鋼風管）
  • 過濾器吸水後效率降低且可能滋生微生物
  • 常見設計上限：80% RH

實務應用 Practical Application

一家 CDMO 發現冬季送風濕度不足（RH < 20%），考慮安裝加濕器。關鍵決策點：

  • 水源選擇：工廠是否有不含胺類的蒸汽？是否需要 Re-boiler？
  • 安裝位置：在 AHU 內冷卻盤管上游，還是在風管內？
  • 下游風管材質：加濕器後至少 3-5 公尺改用焊接密封的不鏽鋼風管
  • 閒置管理：夏季不用時的消毒和排空 SOP
8.1.10 Dehumidifier（除濕器）

原文內容 Original Text

A dehumidifier is a device that removes water vapor from the air to reduce humidity, either by condensation of water vapor from the air using a cooling coil or by absorption or adsorption using a desiccant (when room RH below 30 to 40% is required). Desiccant dehumidifiers often are located downstream of a cooling coil that removes much of the moisture challenge at lower energy cost and increases RH to increase desiccant efficiency. However, care should be taken to assure saturation or carryover of liquid water droplets does not damage the desiccant.

The choice of desiccant depends on the application. Desiccants are regenerated using heat; therefore, air leaving the dehumidifier is both drier and hotter than upon entering. A re-cooling coil may be needed. It may be necessary to keep the wheel regenerated even when dehumidification is not needed to prevent damage to some desiccants. Appropriate expertise is required to design and commission the control of desiccant dehumidifiers. Manufacturers should be consulted before attempting a dehumidification control scheme.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：兩種除濕方式的臨界點

HVAC 系統有兩種除濕方式，選擇取決於目標 RH：

  • 冷卻盤管除濕：經濟實惠，但只能將 RH 降到約 30-40%。原理：空氣冷卻到露點以下，水分凝結在盤管上
  • 乾燥劑除濕（Desiccant）：可將 RH 降到 10% 以下。原理：吸附劑（如矽膠、分子篩）吸收空氣中的水分，然後用熱空氣再生

最佳實務是兩者串聯：冷卻盤管先去除大部分水分（低成本），再用乾燥劑做精密除濕。

重點提示：Desiccant Dehumidifier 的三大注意事項

  1. 出風溫度升高：乾燥劑吸附水分會釋放熱量，出風比進風更熱更乾→通常需要 Re-cool Coil
  2. 乾燥劑保護：液態水滴會損壞乾燥劑，所以上游冷卻盤管需要 Mist Eliminator
  3. 閒置時仍需再生：某些乾燥劑在不使用時也需要保持再生狀態，否則會損壞

ISPE 強調：設計和調試乾燥劑除濕器的控制系統需要專門的專業知識，應諮詢設備製造商。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 一間生產吸入劑乾粉（DPI）的房間要求 RH < 15%，只用冷卻盤管除濕可以嗎？為什麼？
  2. 為什麼 ISPE 建議乾燥劑除濕器設在冷卻盤管下游，而不是上游？
8.1.11 Air Filtration（空氣過濾）

原文內容 Original Text

Air filters remove particulate material from an airstream by means of various media types. Pre-filters typically are provided upstream of coils in an air handler to protect the coils from fouling with dirt or debris. Pre-filters use low efficiency dust stop filters followed by a medium or high efficiency intermediate filter. For further information, see Chapter 2 of this Guide.

Air filters of activated carbon or other materials may be used to absorb some vapors. This is common where objectionable aromas or small quantities of volatile organics need to be decreased.

8.1.11.1 Final Filter

Filters may be provided as the last air treatment step in an air handler. The use of high efficiency filters (typically 95% DOP or HEPA) can assure air quality (with reference to particles) inside supply air ductwork and can protect terminal (ceiling mounted) filters from fouling with dirt or debris; therefore, extending the life of the terminal filters and preventing differential blinding. See the ISPE Baseline Guide on Sterile Manufacturing Facilities.

In systems without terminal filtration, final filters in AHUs can provide adequate filtration, perhaps for some classified spaces, as long as ductwork is clean. High efficiency final filters are more commonly used in systems that employ fan drive belts that shed particulate into the airstream.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：空氣過濾的三道防線

HVAC 空氣過濾系統採用層層保護的策略：

  1. Pre-filter（預過濾器）：低效率的粗塵過濾，位於盤管上游，保護盤管不被灰塵堵塞
  2. Intermediate Filter（中效過濾器）：中高效率，進一步減少微粒
  3. Final Filter（終端過濾器）：95% DOP 或 HEPA（High Efficiency Particulate Air），確保送風風管內的空氣品質
  4. Terminal HEPA（末端 HEPA）：安裝在天花板上的最後一道過濾，確保進入房間的空氣達到潔淨等級

每一層都在保護下一層——延長壽命、降低更換成本、防止差異性遮蔽（Differential Blinding）。

比喻說明：過濾系統就像多層淨水

想像家用淨水系統：PP 棉濾芯（Pre-filter）擋住大顆粒 → 活性碳（Intermediate）去除異味 → RO 膜（Final Filter）去除微量雜質。每一層都在保護更精密的下一層。如果你跳過 PP 棉直接用 RO 膜，膜會快速堵塞、成本暴增。

重點提示：活性碳過濾器的特殊用途

ISPE 提到活性碳過濾器用於吸收氣味和揮發性有機物（VOC）。在 CDMO 環境中，這可能用於：

  • 處理溶劑型製程（如流化床造粒使用的異丙醇、乙醇）的排氣
  • 防止不同產品間的氣味交叉影響
  • 保護操作人員免受低濃度有害蒸氣暴露
8.1.12-8.1.15 Ductwork, Damper, Diffuser, UV Light（風管、風門、散流器、紫外光）

原文內容 Original Text

8.1.12 Ductwork

This is a network of air conduits distributed throughout a building, connected to a fan or AHU to supply, return, or exhaust/extract air to or from zones and rooms in a building. These ducts may be constructed of metal, plastic, building construction materials, fiberglass boards, or a combination of these materials. Metal ductwork is recommended for most pharmaceutical applications.

8.1.13 Damper and Louver

8.1.13.1 Damper: A damper is a valve for controlling airflow in ductwork, which consists of a movable element plate, plunger, or bladder that opens and closes to regulate airflow. Dampers may be used to regulate airflow to and from specific rooms (to "balance" airflow) and to control fresh air or return air to the AHU.

8.1.13.2 Louver: A louver is an assembly of sloping vanes, usually fixed in position, intended to permit air to pass through, while inhibiting passage of water droplets from outdoors into air systems. A louver also may be found in return air ductwork at room interfaces.

8.1.14 Diffuser, Register, and Grille

Air distribution inlet or outlet grilles are composed of a combination of blades, plates, or vanes intended to direct airflow entering or leaving a space in a desired pattern. Grilles generally are characterized by their pattern of distribution from 1 to 4 directions and projection distance at a given airflow (the throw).

Registers are grilles with an added airflow control damper close coupled to the concealed side.

Diffusers are air outlets, assembled from a series of vanes and plates, generally designed to mix supply air with room air to minimize drafts and maximize dilution. An exception is the non-aspirating diffuser, which is designed to provide equal velocity of discharge in all directions with minimal mixing. Diffusers are usually found at ceilings and located to distribute the air as uniformly as possible throughout a space.

8.1.15 Ultraviolet Light

Ultraviolet (UV) lights (a form of ionizing radiation) at 254 nm may be employed in an airstream to disrupt microorganisms. The energy flux required to achieve destruction at typical duct or AHU velocities is prohibitively high, because of the relationship between the energy level and exposure time needed for this purpose. UV lights may be mounted in conjunction with filters to increase exposure time; however, this is considered of little value in pharmaceutical HVAC systems.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

重點提示：UV 燈在藥廠 HVAC 中「幾乎無價值」

ISPE 的立場很明確：UV 燈在製藥 HVAC 中價值有限。原因是：

  • 風管內風速太高（通常 5-15 m/s），微生物通過 UV 照射區的時間太短
  • 要達到有效殺菌劑量，需要極高的 UV 能量密度——不經濟
  • 即使搭配過濾器增加暴露時間，效果也有限

藥廠的微生物控制應依靠 HEPA 過濾和壓差控制，而非 UV 燈。

重點提示：為什麼藥廠推薦金屬風管？

ISPE 推薦金屬風管（Metal Ductwork），原因包括：

  • 不會釋放纖維——玻璃纖維板風管會釋放纖維微粒
  • 內壁光滑——易清潔，減少微粒和微生物附著
  • 耐用——不會因清潔劑或消毒劑而劣化
  • 不吸潮——塑膠或玻璃纖維材料可能吸收水分

核心概念：Grille、Register、Diffuser 的區別

這三個英文詞在中文常常混用，但定義不同：

在潔淨室中，Non-Aspirating Diffuser（非吸引式散流器）特別重要——它提供等速向下的氣流，最小化混合，適用於需要單向流或低混合度的場所（如 HEPA 過濾器面板）。

Table 8.1: System Components and Their Influence on Environmental Parameters（系統元件與環境參數影響矩陣）

原文內容 Original Text

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：如何閱讀這張矩陣？

這張表是理解 HVAC 系統設計的「速查表」——每個元件影響哪些環境參數，一目了然。

關鍵觀察：

  • Air Handler（AHU）是唯一影響所有五個參數的設備——這就是為什麼它是系統的核心
  • Cooling Coil同時影響溫度和濕度——它是 AHU 中最「多功能」的元件
  • Air Filter只影響 Air Cleanliness——但這是 GMP 最關鍵的參數
  • 風管和風門影響壓力和風量——它們是「輸送系統」，不做空氣調節

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 如果一間潔淨室的溫度偏高但濕度正常，問題最可能出在哪個元件？如果溫度正常但濕度偏高呢？
  2. 使用這張表，設計一個「最小化」的 HVAC 系統（只需控制壓差和空氣潔淨度），需要哪些元件？
8.2 HVAC System Configuration / 8.2.2 Number of Air Handling Units（HVAC 系統配置 / AHU 數量決策）

原文內容 Original Text

8.2.1 Introduction

This section gives a brief overview of the key factors to consider, the options available in HVAC system design, and the factors influencing decisions to choose a particular system type. This section should be read in conjunction with Chapter 3 of this Guide.

8.2.2 Number of Air Handling Units

A manufacturing area often is divided into zones with a separate AHU for each zone. In the pharmaceutical industry, a zone is usually considered to be an area with one type of manufacturing process or area cleanliness classification, e.g., a tablet compression suite in an oral solid dosage facility or all classified areas for aseptic product.

When dividing a facility into zones, advantages include:
