  1. 一間 3m × 4m × 3m 的潔淨室，送風量為 2,000 CFM。計算換氣次數（AC/hr）。（提示：先換算體積為 ft³）
  2. 兩間潔淨室之間的壓差為 0.05 inch wg，門縫面積為 0.2 ft²。使用簡化公式計算洩漏風量（CFM）。
  3. 為什麼粒子濃度公式說房間大小不影響穩態粒子水平？直覺上這似乎不對，請解釋其物理原因。
17.2

17.2 Pressure Control When Airlocks are not Possible

無氣鎖條件下的壓差控制 | p253-p254

原文內容 Original Text

New facilities, particularly those with classified rooms, should have cascaded airlocks between air classes, but it may not be possible to retrofit airlocks into older facilities. The FDA Sterile Drug Products GMP suggests a measurable DP between rooms of different classes and air velocity through open doors sufficient to keep airborne contaminants out.

Sufficient air velocity often leads to airflow volumes that are too great to pass around the cracks of a closed door and may prevent the door from closing against the very high pressure the airflow would create. For separations without airlocks, an alternative design (see Figure 17.1) can permit high airflows through open doors, while keeping DP below 15 Pa when the door is closed.

Figure 17.1: DP without an Airlock (diagram referenced in original text)

教學解析

無氣鎖壓差控制的兩難

老舊設施改造時經常遇到的困境：

  • FDA 要求不同潔淨等級之間需有可測量的壓差
  • 開門時需要足夠的風速來阻止汙染進入
  • 但大風量在關門時會產生過高壓差（超過 15 Pa），導致門無法關閉或密封條過載

CDMO 改造實務

在老舊 CDMO 廠房改造時，無法增設氣鎖是常見挑戰。替代方案包括：

  • 安裝可變風量系統：開門時自動增加風量維持保護風速，關門時降低風量控制壓差在 15 Pa 以下
  • 使用門位感應器連動 BMS 控制邏輯
  • 確保替代方案經過風險評估並獲得品質部門核准
17.3

17.3 HEPA Filter Arrangements

HEPA 過濾器佈置策略 | p254-p256

17.3.1 Air Filtration Arrangements

It is common practice for aseptic manufacturing facilities to recirculate air back to the air handler unit. Generally, this is good practice as it limits the particle load on the filters, reduces the cost of conditioning outdoor air, and optimizes control. However, there are other factors to account for:

  • potential for cross-contamination in multi-purpose facilities
  • accidental recirculation of product-contaminated air affecting operators or plant maintenance staff

These factors may be overcome by the use of return air filters. If the logic is that these are to capture airborne contamination; however, they need to be of the “safe change” type to protect maintenance personnel.

The environmental standards in the FDA Sterile Drug Products GMP identify a 0.5 μm particle size as the reference point. Only HEPA and ULPA filters have quantified performance ratings against a most penetrating particle size, usually sizes smaller than 0.5 μm. When looking at sub-micron particle reduction by filtration, only HEPA and ULPA filters should be considered as effective.

The location of HEPA filters within a system should be at points such that there is no opportunity for the air to become re-contaminated. The use of terminal supply (ceiling) HEPA filters is recommended for classification of Grade 7 and cleaner. They also have the additional advantage of maintaining the sealed envelope of the processing area should the supply fan fail.

Figure 17.2: A Possible Aseptic Area Filter Arrangement (diagram referenced in original text)

DOP and PAO (and Sodium Flame) penetration tests use a high concentration of particles of a known spectrum (usually in the range of 0.3 μm diameter). Efficiency results then can be related to specific size of airborne particle. Only filters tested by such methods have reliable data for this size of particle. Filters grades lower than HEPA are tested by averaging methods in which specific particle size efficiencies are not identified.

Note: European H14 air filters can pass a penetration scan test, but the purchaser must request that H13 filters do so.

教學解析

空氣過濾策略的核心考量

  • 空氣回風再利用：是常見且良好的做法 — 降低外氣處理成本，但在多品種設施中需注意交叉汙染風險
  • 回風過濾器：若用於捕捉產品粉塵，必須使用「安全更換型」（Safe Change / Bag-in Bag-out），保護維護人員免受暴露
  • 末端 HEPA 位置原則：HEPA 應安裝在空氣不會再被汙染的位置 — 因此推薦 Grade 7（ISO 7）及更高等級使用天花板末端 HEPA

末端 HEPA 的額外安全效益

末端天花板 HEPA 在送風機故障時仍能維持製造區的「密封外殼」效應 — 過濾器本身就是一道物理屏障，阻止管道中的未過濾空氣進入潔淨室。這是單純在 AHU 中安裝 HEPA 無法提供的保護。

H13 vs. H14 過濾器（EN 1822）

  • H14：必須通過穿透掃描測試（Penetration Scan Test），確保整個過濾面無局部洩漏
  • H13：預設不需通過掃描測試 — 但採購時可以特別要求
  • 藥廠通常要求所有 HEPA 在安裝後進行現場完整性測試（In-situ Integrity Test），無論是 H13 或 H14

17.3.2 Prefiltration for Terminal HEPAs

A single HEPA filter bank in normal circumstances is adequate to reduce supply particulate concentration below that of a reasonable “at rest” design classification. An important consequence of using a single HEPA bank is that the supply air 0.5 μm particle count is unlikely to be near zero. This could affect the calculations of air change volume flow rate to offset particulate gains, and in particular, recovery periods.

Another potential problem to be addressed if only terminal HEPA filters are used, is that of filter blinding that can result in reversed DPs putting environmental conditions at risk.

Figure 17.3: Example of the Effects of Terminal Filter Differential Blinding

Terminal filter blinding can result in design DPs being reversed. There are a number of possible solutions:

  • Install a main bank of HEPA filters.
  • Install a main bank of high efficiency non-HEPA filters (minimum MERV 13/F7 or better) in the AHU.
  • Employ active pressure or constant air volume control.
  • Frequently replace terminal HEPA filters.
  • Install airflow or pressure drop indicators to indicate filter performance.

The option to use high efficiency pre-filters in series with the HEPA has a number of advantages:

  • The effects of differential blinding will be minimized. The performance of the terminal filters will be maintained constant for years longer, balancing the ratio between supply and extracts, and in turn, DPs.
  • Because the bank of filters handles the main particulate load and acts as a prefilter in the AHU, only these will require regular replacement. This reduces replacement maintenance costs and downtime.

It is recommended that high efficiency non-HEPA pre-filtration be installed in all new or renovated facilities employing HEPA terminal filtration.

教學解析

過濾器阻塞（Filter Blinding）的連鎖效應

當末端 HEPA 過濾器逐漸阻塞時：

  1. 通過該過濾器的風量下降
  2. 該房間送風減少 → 壓力降低
  3. 其他房間的末端 HEPA 阻塞速率不同 → 不同房間風量比例改變
  4. 設計壓差可能被反轉 — 較低等級房間壓力反而高於較高等級房間！

這是一個嚴重的 GMP 風險。

預過濾設計建議（強烈推薦）

指引明確建議：所有新建或改造設施，若使用末端 HEPA，都應在 AHU 中安裝高效預過濾器（至少 MERV 13 / F7）。

好處包括：

  • 末端 HEPA 壽命延長數年，壓降變化極小
  • 差異化阻塞最小化 → 各房間風量比例穩定 → 壓差穩定
  • 只需定期更換 AHU 中的預過濾器 → 降低維護成本和停機時間
  • 減少對恆風量（CV）或主動壓力控制裝置的依賴

比喻說明：預過濾如同泳池的粗篩網

游泳池的過濾系統通常有粗篩網（攔截樹葉）和精濾器（過濾細小雜質）。如果沒有粗篩網，精濾器很快就會堵塞。HVAC 中的預過濾與末端 HEPA 正是這個關係 — 預過濾攔截大部分粒子，讓 HEPA 只處理最細小的粒子，大幅延長其使用壽命。

17.4

17.4 Recovery Period versus Air Change Rates

恢復時間與換氣次數的關係 | p256-p257

原文內容 Original Text

If a minimum recovery period is required, this factor may be the deciding criterion for the air change rate. Figure 17.4 is a simplified model for calculating the relationship between air change rate and recovery period. This model is based on two major assumptions (good mixing efficiency with clean supply air).

Figure 17.4: Recovery Period versus Air Change Rates

(Note that the curves and equation are merely basic exponential decay expressions)

Figure 17.4 shows how by assuming a simple exponential decay, the “recovery period” changes greatly with air change rate: a 100-fold recovery, from ISO 7 to ISO 5, with 20 air changes per hour, takes approximately 14 minutes; with 30 air changes per hour, it takes approximately 9 minutes.

In general, it is considered more important to achieve target recovery than to achieve target air change rate.

教學解析

恢復時間優先於換氣次數

這是指引的一個關鍵設計原則：

  • 換氣次數（如 20 AC/hr）只是一個手段
  • 真正的目標是「恢復時間」（如 15 分鐘內從運轉態恢復到靜態）
  • 如果 15 AC/hr 就能在 15 分鐘內完成 100 倍恢復，就不需要刻意追求 20 AC/hr

100 倍恢復的實際意義

CDMO 在設計時應以目標恢復時間反推所需的最低換氣次數，而不是直接套用固定的換氣次數要求。

17.5

17.5 Additional Controls Information

控制系統補充資訊：致動器與控制閥 | p257-p260

17.5.1 Electrical/Electronic Actuators

The actuator uses a low voltage control signal to drive an electric motor; the units can be on/off or proportional. These systems are used where the speed of actuation can be slower, typical times for a valve to go from fully open to fully closed are in the range of 1 to 2 minutes.

Installation is simple, as all signals are by cable, e.g., control signal, power supply, and feedback, such as valve position or open/closed signals. The actuators can be supplied as fail open, fail closed, or with a manual override.

17.5.2 Pneumatic Actuators

An electronic control signal (from a stand-alone controller, BMS, or Process Controller) varies the output air pressure from a pneumatic controller, which is fed to a pneumatic actuator on the controlled component.

The system requires the use of an I/P (control signal to pneumatic) converter, an instrument quality air supply, and local tubing to the actuator. In order to get the best response time, the converter should be as close as possible to the actuator.

The units use air pressure in one direction with an opposing spring to return the controlled item to the fail position. The system is proportionally controlled. These units typically have a faster response time than an electric or electronic unit. The all-pneumatic system also is ideal for hazardous areas requiring intrinsically safe installations.

教學解析

Fail Position 的重要性

「失效位置」是 HVAC 安全設計的關鍵概念。當供電或供氣中斷時，閥門必須自動回到安全位置：

  • 冷卻盤管閥：通常設為 Fail Closed（失效關閉），避免過度冷卻
  • 加熱盤管閥：在寒冷氣候可能設為 Fail Open（失效開啟），防止盤管凍裂
  • 每個閥門的 Fail Position 都應在設計階段明確定義並記錄

17.5.3 Control Valves for Liquids

There are two types of control valve: the three port valve (mixing or diverting) and the two port valve (directly controls flow). The three-port valve was the industry standard; however, the use of two port valves with variable flow rate systems is becoming more common.

17.5.3.1 Valve Characteristic

The valve characteristic is the ratio of flow through the valve to the valve lift (opening) at a constant DP across the valve. There are three main types:

  • Fast opening – typically used for only on/off control
  • Linear – flow rate directly proportional to the amount it is open; commonly used for diverting applications
  • Equal percentage – flow rate has a logarithmic relation to the amount it is open; more commonly used in water flow and steam control applications

17.5.3.2 Flow Coefficient (Cv)

A simple flow coefficient calculation for liquids:

In English Units: Cv = gpm × (SG/dp)1/2

Where gpm = water flow (US gallons per minute), SG = specific gravity (1 for water), dp = pressure drop (psi)

In SI Units: Cv = 11.7 × CMH × (SG/dp)1/2

Where CMH = water flow (Cu.Meter/hour), dp = pressure drop (KPa)

Select a valve where the required Cv is in the 30 to 80% range of the stroke. A valve that is too small or too large would not have the ability to control the flow accurately, i.e., will not have adequate “authority.”

17.5.3.3 Valve Authority

Valve Authority is the percentage of total system pressure drop assigned to the valve. If the valve’s pressure drop is small in comparison to the pipe and heat exchanger losses, the valve will have less ability to control effectively.

教學解析

控制閥選型三要素

  1. 閥門特性（Characteristic）：根據應用選擇
    • 快開式：僅用於開/關控制
    • 線性式：流量與開度成正比 → 適合分流/混合應用
    • 等百分比式：流量與開度呈對數關係 → 適合蒸汽和水流控制
  2. 流量係數 Cv：選閥時所需 Cv 應在閥門行程的 30-80% 範圍內
  3. 閥門權限（Authority）：閥門壓降占系統總壓降的比例，比例太低則控制能力不足

Cv 計算公式

英制：Cv = gpm × √(SG / dp)
  gpm = 流量（美制加侖/分鐘）
  SG  = 比重（水 = 1）
  dp  = 閥門壓降（psi）

SI 制：Cv = 11.7 × CMH × √(SG / dp)
  CMH = 流量（立方米/小時）
  dp  = 閥門壓降（KPa）

選型規則：
  所需 Cv 應在閥門額定 Cv 的 30-80% 行程處
  閥門太小（< 管線尺寸一半）→ 控制粗糙
  閥門太大（≥ 管線尺寸）→ 控制不穩定

比喻說明：閥門權限如同水龍頭控制

想像你家的水龍頭（控制閥）和整條水管系統的水壓損失。如果水管本身的摩擦損失很大（比如管線很長、很細），即使你把水龍頭從全開轉到半開，出水量變化也不大 — 因為水龍頭的壓降只占總壓降的一小部分。

HVAC 控制閥的「權限」（Authority）就是同樣概念。閥門壓降至少要占系統總壓降的 30-50%，才能有效控制流量。

17.6

17.6 Sample Controls Description

HVAC 控制系統範例描述 | p260-p262

原文內容 Original Text

The following is a sample description of the controls operation for one HVAC system. It should be used only as a guide.

1. Control Description

1.1 Plant Operation

  • On plant startup, the fresh air damper (MD 1) and the recirculation damper (MD 2) should be fully opened.
  • On normal plant shutdown, the supply fan should be stopped and all dampers fully closed.
  • In the event that the mixed air temperature (TX 1) falls below 5°C, an alarm should be raised and the plant stopped.
  • All control valves should be closed on plant shut down.

1.2 Temperature Control

  • The supply air temperature (TX 2) should be maintained by modulation in series of the cooling coil control valve (CVM 1) and the heating coil control valve (CVM 2).
  • The supply air temperature set point (TX 2) should be adjusted (min 12°C) to ensure that the reheat control valve output is minimized.

1.3 Humidity Control

  • The return air humidity (HX 1 and HX 2) should be maintained at the required set point (60% maximum) by providing override control of the cooling coil control valve (CVM-1) to achieve dehumidification.

1.4 Fan Speed Control

  • The supply fan speed (SCM 1) should be modulated to maintain a constant supply airflow rate to the dictates of the fan mounted flow airflow measuring device (AFD 1).
  • When required to operate, the supply fan (SF 1) run status should be confirmed to start and run at a variable speed as dictated by the fan speed control loop.

1.5 Monitoring/Alarm

  • A general alarm should be raised at the central supervisor for all conditions as shown on the HVAC point schedule.

1.6 Software Set Point Adjustments

  • system operation status (on/off)
  • minimum supply air temperature set point (10 to 25°C)
  • return air temperature set point (18 to 24°C)
  • return air humidity set point (60% maximum)
  • supply fan DP set point (0 to 100 Pa)
  • supply fan high speed alarm (50 to 100%)

3. I/O Point Schedule (Table 17.1)

Key: AI = Analog Input, AO = Analog Output, DI = Digital Input, DO = Digital Output, M = Monitor, C = Control, CA = Critical Alarm, GA = General Alarm

教學解析

HVAC 控制描述文件的結構

一份完整的 HVAC 控制系統描述通常包含五個部分：

  1. 控制描述：系統啟停邏輯、溫度控制、濕度控制、風速控制、監控警報
  2. 設計筆記：特殊設計考量
  3. I/O 點表：所有輸入/輸出信號的清單
  4. 系統示意圖：管路、設備、感測器位置
  5. 空氣線圖：Psychrometric Chart

關鍵控制邏輯要點

  • 低溫保護：混合空氣溫度（TX 1）低於 5°C 時必須報警並停機 — 這是防止加熱盤管凍裂的安全聯鎖
  • 序列控制：冷卻盤管閥（CVM 1）和加熱盤管閥（CVM 2）串聯調節 — 先冷後熱，避免同時加熱和冷卻浪費能源
  • 除濕策略：當濕度超標時，冷卻盤管閥接受「覆蓋控制」（Override Control），優先除濕而非溫控
  • 恆風量控制：風機轉速由風量測量裝置（AFD 1）反饋調節，確保送風量穩定

I/O 點表解讀

I/O 點表（I/O Point Schedule）是 BMS（Building Management System，樓宇管理系統）程式設計的基礎文件：

  • AI（類比輸入）：溫度、濕度等連續信號 → 需要 4-20mA 或 0-10V 輸入卡
  • AO（類比輸出）：閥門開度、風機轉速等連續控制 → 需要類比輸出卡
  • DI（數位輸入）：開關狀態、壓差開關 → 乾接點信號
  • DO（數位輸出）：風機啟停 → 繼電器輸出
  • CA（關鍵警報） vs. GA（一般警報）：決定警報的優先級和響應要求

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為什麼混合空氣溫度的低溫保護設定為 5°C 而不是 0°C？考慮感測器精度和響應延遲。
  2. 解釋為什麼濕度控制使用「覆蓋控制」（Override）而非獨立控制迴路。在什麼氣候條件下這種設計特別重要？
  3. 如果 HEPA 過濾器壓差開關（PDI 3）報警但風量正常，可能的原因是什麼？
17.7

17.7 Temperature Mapping

溫度分佈驗證 | p262-p265

原文內容 Original Text

The purpose of temperature mapping helps confirm that the area as a whole remains within its defined limits and determine the locations representing temperature extremes within the area.

The second objective defines the locations for permanently installed sensors that provide data for the quality system of record for the area, the information from which the MKT (Mean Kinetic Temperature) is calculated (if required).

There are benefits to a smaller number of sensors for this purpose, while it is necessary to use a sufficient number of sensors to ensure that the mapping study is robust.

There is very little guidance on temperature mapping; the French Standard (NF X15-140 October 2002) provides some guidance on sensor locations for mapping of environmental chambers, as summarized below.

The method is based on the size of the chamber and the area potentially occupied by product. For chambers over 700 cubic feet (20 cubic meters), the standard suggests considering:

  • door openings
  • location of the cooling system
  • position of the control sensor

Figure 17.6: Temperature Mapping for Chambers up to 2 m³ and up to 20 m³

Note: The inner box represents the working area (where product is stored); the dots represent the sensor locations.

An approach that has been used with some success is to consider the area around each HVAC outlet as a zone and apply the sensor placement logic with a sensor adjacent to the temperature control sensor as well as others monitoring the supply and return temperatures.

For an area bounded by an external wall, the area requires mapping in the seasonal extremes in order to determine the influences of heating and cooling supply temperatures from the HVAC system, and the heating or cooling effect of the external walls and roof adjacent to the racking.

Mapping Considerations

  • Product temperatures – if upon delivery these are significantly different from defined storage conditions, the product may require monitoring.
  • Loading and unloading – will the product be at a different temperature, how much is loaded/unloaded at any one time, will there be significant ingress/egress of conditioned air?
  • Is there an outside door that can be left open or is it monitored to a defined maximum time?
  • Storage locations nearest the heating/cooling system inlet/outlet
  • Internal loads – lighting, equipment, and personnel
  • Conditions during minimum (empty) and full storage capacity

Example Plan

External Heat Gains and Losses: The major heat gains and losses to the cold room are due to the influences of the external environment with the most significant being through the walls and ceiling.

Internal Heat Gains and Losses:

  • Lighting – low energy fluorescent lamps, left on during the working day
  • Product – supplied at storage temperature, will not represent additional load
  • Equipment – stacking fork truck, a relatively small intermittent load
  • People – 2 people occasionally working in the area; heat gains are small and transient

HVAC: The room is conditioned through two registers with a common return air grille located at high level in the center of the room.

Mapping Sensor Placement: The areas considered during the mapping are only the areas where product will be stored. The proposed sensor locations are based on: considering the chamber as two zones with each zone supplied by a register; placing a temperature sensor at the lowest and highest storage point at each corner on the racking nearest the perimeter; and one sensor at the center at the midpoint level.

Load Testing: The unit will be mapped during peak summer and winter seasons, considering two load scenarios: empty chamber and one half of the warehouse kept empty with the other half filled with full boxes simulating the largest stored pallet (40″ × 48″ × 46″ tall).

Note that the HVAC has constant flow rate and with the room empty, airspeed will be low. When the room is not empty, the airflow will be at a higher speed, as the same volume of air must travel through a smaller area.

教學解析

溫度分佈驗證的雙重目的

  1. 確認區域整體合規：驗證整個儲存區域在定義限值內
  2. 決定永久感測器位置：找出溫度極值點，作為品質記錄系統中永久監測感測器的安裝位置，用於計算 MKT（Mean Kinetic Temperature，平均動力學溫度）

感測器佈局策略（NF X15-140 法國標準）

  • 小型腔室（≤ 2 m³）：最少數量的感測器放置在工作區角落
  • 中型腔室（≤ 20 m³）：增加感測器數量，覆蓋更多位置
  • 大型區域（> 20 m³）：以每個 HVAC 出風口為一個「區域」，在每區應用標準佈局
  • 必須在控制感測器附近放一個驗證感測器，以確認控制點的代表性

季節性測試的必要性

有外牆的區域必須在夏季和冬季極端條件下各做一次溫度分佈驗證，原因：

  • 夏季：外牆傳熱使靠牆區域偏熱 → HVAC 冷卻負載增加
  • 冬季：外牆散熱使靠牆區域偏冷 → 可能出現局部低溫
  • 結果可能截然不同，最高溫點和最低溫點的位置可能互換

CDMO 倉庫溫度驗證實務

對於藥品倉庫的溫度驗證，以下實務要點尤其重要：

  • 負載測試：空倉 vs. 滿倉的氣流模式完全不同 — 滿倉時貨架阻擋氣流，風速增加但分佈可能不均
  • 進出貨影響：大量進出貨時外門開啟，會大量引入未調節外氣 → 需評估最長開門時間
  • 產品溫度：冬天從室外運入的產品溫度可能遠低於儲存條件 → 需要額外的回溫監測

比喻說明：溫度驗證如同冰箱體檢

家用冰箱也有「溫度分佈」問題：門邊比較溫暖、後壁比較冷、上下層溫度不同。溫度驗證就像對冰箱做徹底體檢 — 找出最熱和最冷的點，確保所有儲存位置都在安全範圍內，然後在極端位置放一個溫度計長期監控。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 你的 CDMO 要驗證一間 15°C-25°C 的成品倉庫。倉庫有一面是外牆。請列出你的溫度驗證計劃中至少 5 個要考慮的因素。
  2. 為什麼指引建議在空倉和半滿倉各做一次測試，而不是空倉和全滿倉？（提示：考慮空氣流動路徑）
  3. MKT 和簡單的算術平均溫度有什麼不同？在什麼情況下 MKT 的計算更有意義？

本節重點回顧 Section Summary

  • 醫療器材 HVAC：FDA 三級分類（Class I/II/III）決定 HVAC 需求等級。環境控制是「裁量性要求」，由製造商透過風險評估自行定義。Class III 植入式器材可能需要分級潔淨空間，常參照 FDA 無菌藥品 GMP 作為環境標準
  • 九大潔淨室方程式：從理想氣體定律到串聯 HEPA 計算，這些公式構成 HVAC 設計的數學基礎。最實用的是 Q=VA（風量）、AC/hr=60Q/V（換氣次數）、Cavg=Cs+PGR/Q（粒子濃度）
  • 壓差與風速的二次方關係：風速加倍需壓差增四倍、馬力增八倍。這解釋了為什麼開門狀態難以維持壓差（需 17,800 CFM），也是氣鎖存在的物理基礎
  • 恢復時間優先：設計應以目標恢復時間反推換氣次數，而非直接套用固定值。20 AC/hr 可在 14 分鐘內完成 100 倍恢復（EU Grade B 要求）
  • 串聯 HEPA 雙重優勢：兩個 99.97% HEPA 串聯可達 99.9991% 效率，且 AHU 主 HEPA 承擔大部分負載使末端 HEPA 壓降穩定數年，簡化空氣平衡
  • 預過濾強烈建議：所有使用末端 HEPA 的設施應安裝高效預過濾（MERV 13/F7），防止差異化阻塞導致壓差反轉
  • 控制閥選型：需同時考慮閥門特性（快開/線性/等百分比）、Cv 流量係數（30-80% 行程範圍）和閥門權限（Authority）
  • 溫度驗證：雙重目的（確認區域合規 + 決定永久感測器位置），有外牆區域需在夏冬極端條件下各做一次，空倉與半滿倉各做一次負載測試
ISPE Good Practice Guide: HVAC | p269 - p288

本章學習目標

  • 瞭解 HVAC 系統設計與確效的完整參考標準體系，包含 ICH、WHO、ISO、EU GMP、FDA 及各國工程標準
  • 掌握 HVAC 領域常用縮寫與首字母縮略詞，建立專業溝通的共同語言基礎
  • 理解關鍵 GMP 術語的正式定義，如 Acceptance Criteria、Critical Parameter、Direct Impact System 等
  • 建立跨法規、跨標準的索引能力，能快速定位特定技術要求的原始出處
Appendix 12 – References（附錄12 – 參考文獻）

原文內容 Original Text

1. ICH

International Conference on Harmonisation of Technical Requirements for Registration of Pharmaceuticals for Human Use (ICH), www.ich.org.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：參考文獻的架構分類

本附錄的31項參考文獻可分為以下幾大類：

  • 法規與指引（Regulatory）：ICH（Q7-Q10）、EU GMP Annex 1、PIC/S、US FDA CFR 21 — 這些是合規的「法源」
  • 國際標準（Standards）：ISO 14644 系列（潔淨室分級）、ISO 14698（生物污染控制）、ASTM E2500（設備驗證）
  • 工程實務（Engineering Practice）：ASHRAE（暖通空調工程師學會）、SMACNA（風管施工）、NEBB（環境平衡）、IEST（潔淨室測試）
  • ISPE 自有文件：Baseline Guides Vol.1-6、GAMP 校正管理
  • 地區性標準：EN（歐洲過濾器標準）、CIBSE（英國建築設備）、NFPA（美國消防）

比喻說明 Analogy

把這份參考文獻清單想像成一位 HVAC 工程師的「工具箱目錄」。就像木工需要不同的鋸子、鑿子和砂紙來完成不同工序，HVAC 設計師需要從不同來源找到對應的技術要求：

  • ICH/FDA/EU GMP = 建築法規（必須遵守）
  • ISO 14644 = 度量衡標準（怎麼量、怎麼分級）
  • ASHRAE/SMACNA = 施工手冊（怎麼做）
  • ISPE Baseline = 藥廠特有的「最佳實務食譜」

重點提示 Key Notes

ICH Q9 Quality Risk Management（品質風險管理）是本指引反覆引用的核心概念。HVAC 系統的設計、確效與維護決策都應以風險為基礎，而非套用一成不變的規範。

ISO 14644 系列是潔淨室設計的國際「通用語言」。Part 1（分級）和 Part 3（測試方法）是 HVAC 工程師最常查閱的兩個部分。

ASTM E2500 提供了藥廠設備「設計 → 規格 → 驗證」的系統化方法，與 ISPE Baseline Vol.5 C&Q 指引高度互補。

ASHRAE 與 SMACNA 的實務意義

ASHRAE（美國暖通空調工程師學會）提供的不僅是理論，更是可執行的試車程序和節能標準。特別是：

  • Guideline 0-2005：試車流程的標準化方法，與 GMP commissioning 的精神一致
  • Standard 90.1：能源效率標準，在藥廠「永續製藥」趨勢下日益重要

SMACNA 則提供風管施工、洩漏測試等「動手做」的技術規範。對於維護團隊和施工品質管理特別實用。

EN 1822 過濾器標準的重要性

EN 1822 系列（Part 1-5）是歐洲 HEPA/ULPA 過濾器的分級與測試標準。在 GMP 環境中，HEPA 過濾器的完整性測試（leak test）是確保潔淨室空氣品質的關鍵驗證項目。Part 4 的「掃描法」（Scan Method）是業界最常用的洩漏偵測方法。

實務應用 Practical Application

情境：你正在為一座新的無菌充填廠房設計 HVAC 系統。客戶問你：「你的設計依據是什麼標準？」

建議回答架構：

  • 法規要求：EU GMP Annex 1（潔淨室等級定義）+ FDA Aseptic Guidance（無菌製程要求）
  • 分級標準：ISO 14644-1（粒子分級）+ ISO 14644-3（測試方法）
  • 工程實務：ASHRAE Handbooks（系統設計）+ SMACNA（風管施工）+ IEST RP-CC006.3（潔淨室測試）
  • 藥廠專用：ISPE Baseline Vol.3（無菌製造設施）+ 本 HVAC 指引

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 為何 ICH Q9 (Quality Risk Management) 對 HVAC 系統設計如此重要？風險管理如何影響空調分區決策？
  2. ISO 14644-1 和 EU GMP Annex 1 在潔淨室分級上有什麼差異？為何需要同時參照兩者？
  3. 如果你要對 HEPA 過濾器進行完整性測試，應該參照 EN 1822 的哪一個 Part？為什麼 Scan Method 是首選？
Appendix 13 – Glossary（附錄13 – 詞彙表）
19.1 Abbreviations（縮寫）

原文內容 Original Text

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：組織縮寫分類

這些縮寫可以按功能分為幾大類，幫助你快速定位：

  • 藥品法規機構：FDA（美國食藥署）、EMEA（歐洲藥品管理局）、ICH（國際醫藥法規協和會）、PIC/S（國際藥品稽查協約組織）、WHO（世界衛生組織）
  • 工程標準組織：ASHRAE（美國暖通空調工程師學會）、SMACNA（北美板金與空調承包商協會）、NEBB（美國環境平衡局）、IEST（環境科學與技術研究所）
  • 國際/國家標準：ISO（國際標準化組織）、ANSI（美國國家標準學會）、ASTM（美國測試與材料學會）、NEMA（美國電氣製造商協會）
  • 安全與環保：OSHA（職業安全與衛生署）、EPA（環境保護署）、NFPA（美國消防協會）、COSHH（危害物質管控，英國）
  • 藥廠專業：ISPE（國際製藥工程學會）、USGBC/WGBC（綠建築委員會）

重點提示 Key Notes

在藥廠 HVAC 專案中，你最常接觸的組織縮寫是：

  • ISPE — 本指引的發行者，藥廠工程的權威來源
  • ASHRAE — 所有 HVAC 工程設計的技術基礎
  • ISO — 潔淨室分級的國際標準
  • FDA/EU GMP — 法規合規的兩大支柱

記住：藥廠 HVAC 是「GMP 法規」+「工程標準」的交叉領域，兩邊都要熟。

19.2 Acronyms（首字母縮略詞）

原文內容 Original Text

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：技術縮略詞分類與中文翻譯

這些縮略詞涵蓋了 HVAC 設計、GMP 品質與工程管理的方方面面。以下按主題分組並提供中文翻譯：

HVAC 核心系統：

  • AHU（空調箱 / 空氣處理單元）— HVAC 系統的核心設備
  • HEPA（高效微粒空氣過濾器）— 效率 ≥99.97%，0.3μm 粒子
  • ULPA（超低穿透空氣過濾器）— 效率 ≥99.999%，0.12μm 粒子
  • FFU（風機過濾單元）— 將風扇與 HEPA 整合的獨立單元
  • UDAF（單向氣流）— 即過去稱為 Laminar Flow 的概念
  • VAV（可變風量）— 節能型風量控制方式
  • CV/CVD（定風量/定風量擋板）

控制與監測：

  • BMS（建築管理系統）— 監控環境條件的核心平台
  • BAS（建築自動化系統）— 與 BMS 概念相近
  • DCS（分散控制系統）
  • DDC（直接數位控制）
  • PLC（可程式邏輯控制器）
  • SCADA（監控與資料擷取系統）
  • EMS（環境監測系統）
  • PI/PID（比例積分/比例積分微分控制）— 溫濕度控制的基本演算法

GMP 品質相關：

  • GMP/CGMP（良好製造規範/現行良好製造規範）
  • GEP（良好工程實務）
  • CQA（關鍵品質屬性）
  • CPP（關鍵製程參數）
  • QRM（品質風險管理）
  • QA（品質保證）
  • SOP（標準作業程序）
  • CIP（就地清洗）
  • RABS（限制進出屏障系統）
  • VPHP（氣態過氧化氫）— 隔離器除污的主要方式

測試與驗證：

  • FAT（工廠驗收測試）— 設備出廠前在製造商處進行
  • SAT（現場驗收測試）— 設備安裝到現場後進行
  • TAB（測試、調整與平衡）— HVAC 試車的核心工作
  • DOP/PAO（鄰苯二甲酸二辛酯/聚α烯烴）— HEPA 過濾器洩漏測試用氣溶膠
  • FMEA（失效模式與效應分析）— QRM 常用工具
  • RCFA（根本原因失效分析）

量測與參數：

  • DP（壓差）— 潔淨室區域隔離的核心參數
  • RH（相對濕度）
  • MKT（平均動力學溫度）— 藥品穩定性評估用
  • CFU（菌落形成單位）— 微生物監測的基本單位
  • MERV（最低效率報告值）— 過濾器效率分級
  • MPPS（最高穿透粒徑）— 過濾器最難捕捉的粒子大小
  • PGR（粒子生成率）— 潔淨室換氣次數計算的關鍵輸入

比喻說明 Analogy

想像你是一位剛加入藥廠 HVAC 團隊的工程師，第一天開會就聽到：「AHU 的 VAV 需要透過 BMS 做 PID 控制，DP 讀數由 EMS 回饋，TAB 報告要在 SAT 前完成。」

這就像第一次去日本旅行，到處都是看不懂的路標。這份縮略詞表就是你的「翻譯字典」— 先熟悉高頻詞彙，很快就能聽懂同事在說什麼。

重點提示：最高頻縮略詞 Top 15

在日常 HVAC 專案溝通中，以下是你最常遇到的縮略詞：

  1. AHU — 空調箱
  2. HEPA — 高效過濾器
  3. DP — 壓差
  4. BMS — 建築管理系統
  5. TAB — 測試調整與平衡
  6. FAT/SAT — 工廠/現場驗收測試
  7. GMP/CGMP — 良好製造規範
  8. VAV — 可變風量
  9. RH — 相對濕度
  10. SOP — 標準作業程序
  11. VFD — 變頻驅動器
  12. FFU — 風機過濾單元
  13. EMS — 環境監測系統
  14. UDAF — 單向氣流
  15. P&ID / AF&ID — 管路儀表圖/氣流儀表圖
19.3 Definitions（定義）

原文內容 Original Text

Acceptance Criteria

The limits of conditions of critical parameters that may affect the product quality. These conditions may include temperature, humidity, and room air quality. For example, if humidity or particulates are not critical parameters affecting product quality they are not included in acceptance criteria.

Action Limit

Criteria established based on possible impact to product quality, outside the operating range (acceptance criteria). A documented response is usually required.

Air Change Rate

The number of times the total air volume of a defined space is replaced in a given unit of time. This is computed by dividing the total volume of the subject space (in cubic volume) into the total volume of air exhausted from (or supplied to) the space per unit of time.

Airflow and Instrument Diagram (AF&ID)

Based on the AFD, it shows HVAC instrumentation and controls, with critical GMP components highlighted.

Air Flow Diagram (AFD)

Schematically shows rooms and zones served by an air handler, with air balance requirements, and often room pressures.

Alert Limit

Criteria established with the intent of notification and possible corrective action prior to exceeding action limits; alert when a parameter is drifting toward extremes of the operating range.

Arrestance

A measure of the ability of an air filter to remove synthetic dust from the air when tested as described in ASHRAE Standard 52.2-2008, Method of Testing General Ventilation Air Cleaning Devices for Removal Efficiency by Particle Size. Arrestance test results typically relate to larger size particles.

As-Built Drawings

Construction drawings that represent the physical condition of the plant or system at turnover from the designer or installer at satisfactory operation. These documents supplement and complement the system manuals and protocols.

Building Management System (BMS)

A computerized system that controls, monitors, and optimizes environmental conditions, through functions and facilities such as heating, air-conditioning, lighting, and security.

Change Control

A formal system by which qualified representatives of appropriate disciplines review proposed or actual changes which might affect validated status. The intent is to determine the need for action which would ensure that the system is maintained in a validated state.

Closed

Not exposed to the room, capable of holding some pressure or purge. PDA TR-28 provides a rigid definition of closed.

Closed Process

A process condition when the product, materials, critical components or container/closure surfaces are contained and separated from the immediate process environment within closed/sealed process equipment. A process step (or system) in which the product and product contact surfaces are not exposed to the immediate room environment.

Commissioning

Commissioning is a quality oriented process for verifying and documenting that the performance of facilities, systems and assemblies meets defined objectives and criteria.

Critical Component

A component within a system where the operation, contact, data, control, alarm, or failure may have a direct impact on product quality or the ability to know product quality.

Critical Location

The location where product is exposed; the location where a cleaned product contact surface is exposed.

Critical Parameter

A processing parameter (temperature, pressure, pH, etc.) which directly influences the drug substance characterization or impurity profile in or after a critical step.

Critical Process Parameter

A process parameter whose variability impacts a quality attribute and therefore needs to be controlled to ensure the process produces the desired quality. A critical process parameter remains critical even if it is controlled.

Critical Quality Attribute

A physical, chemical, biological or microbiological property or characteristic that needs to be controlled (directly or indirectly) to ensure product quality.

Design For Impact

The practice of making design decisions related to the impact of the system in operation, at the beginning of design development.

Design Point

Provided in the basis of design as the nominal set point around which the parameter is expected to be controlled, +/- a given tolerance value (the Design Tolerance) to provide the Design Target.

Design Qualification (DQ)

Documented verification that the proposed design of the facilities, equipment, or systems is suitable for the intended purpose.

Design Target

A value for a critical parameter that is more conservative than its acceptance criterion, used by designers to assure that the system is capable of meeting the acceptance criterion. Design Targets should not be used for system qualification; they are "wishful" values that may not be achieved in reality.

Design Tolerance

The expected (design) upper and lower points of the normal operating range.

Direct Impact System

A system that is expected to have a direct impact on product quality. These systems are designed and commissioned in line with Good Engineering Practice and also are subject to Qualification Practices that incorporate the enhanced review, control and testing against specifications or other requirements necessary for GMP compliance.

DOP (Dioctyl Phthalate)

Material used in the past to generate aerosols comprising particles in the 0.1 to 0.7 micron range (typically with an average size of 0.3 micron) used to test HEPA and ULPA filters. This chemical is believed to have carcinogenic properties and has been generally replaced by a mineral oil, e.g. Emery 3004/Durasyn 164, or Shell Ondina EL.

The smoke generated from this has very similar characteristics and the term DOP testing remains, although it is now understood to mean 'Dispersed Oil Particulate.'

Dust Spot Efficiency

The measure of a the ability of a filter to remove synthetic dust when tested as described in ASHRAE Standard 52.2-2008, Method of Testing General Ventilation Air Cleaning Devices for Removal Efficiency by Particle Size.

Enhanced Design Review (EDR)

A documented review of the engineering design, at an appropriate stage in a project, for conformance to operational and regulatory expectations.

Exfiltration

Leakage of air out of a room through cracks in doors and pass-throughs through material transfer openings, etc. due to a difference in room pressures.

Exhaust Air

Air removed mechanically from the space and discarded.

Functional Design Specification

Description of acceptance criteria, in terms of ranges and logic of system operation, etc. In HVAC, a description of HOW the HVAC will meet the acceptance criteria (room air class, DP, airflow, recovery time, level of air filtration, etc.).

Good Engineering Practice (GEP)

Established engineering methods and standards that are applied throughout the project life cycle to deliver appropriate, cost-effective solutions.

Grade

Applied to air cleanliness, "grade" implies airborne particle count limits in operation, with associated bioburden limits. The number of the grade is the upper limit in operation as defined by ISO 14644-1 (i.e., Grade 5 has 0.5 micron upper particle limit of 3500/cubic meters). In the European GMP, Grades A through D also involve particle limits at rest.

High Efficiency Particulate Air (HEPA) Filter

High efficiency particulate air filter, a filter with DOP efficiency in excess of 99.97% on 0.3 μm particles.

Indirect Impact System

A system that is not expected to have a direct impact on product quality, but typically will support a Direct Impact system. These systems are designed and commissioned following Good Engineering Practice only.

Infiltration

The entry of air from an adjoining room or from outdoors through wall and ceiling openings due to a difference in air pressure between the two areas.

Laminar Flow – see Unidirectional Flow

Latent Heat

Latent heat refers to the amount of energy released or absorbed by a chemical substance during a change of state, meaning a phase transition such as the melting of ice (latent heat of fusion) or the boiling of water (latent heat of vaporization). In an HVAC application typically used to condense water from humid air.

"Nearly Massless" Particles

Particles smaller than 0.5 μm, which typically constitute less than 1% of the total mass of particles typically found in outside air, but represents a large percentage of the total number of particles in a typical sample of air. (Due to the exponential increase in the mass of a particle caused by an increase in the diameter, very small particles typically represent a small fraction of the total mass of particles in an airstream.)

Normal Operating Range

The actual observed values of the critical parameter during operations, collected over time. This range may be larger than the design tolerance, but should be well within Action Limits.

Operating Range

The validated acceptance criteria within which a control parameter must remain, wherein acceptable product is being manufactured.

PAO (Polyalphaolefin)

A synthetic oil used in lieu of DOP for HEPA filter testing.

Particle Count

Airborne particle count of both viable (living) organisms and non-viable (inert) particles. [Measured in PCF (particles per cubic foot) (multiply by ~35.3 to obtain particles per cubic meter)].

Particles Generation Rate (PGR)

Particles generated within a space by a process and occupants per unit time.

Plenum

An enclosed space used for HVAC airflow, typically at low velocity. A plenum may be constructed of duct materials (e.g., sheet metal) or may utilize interstitial spaces in building construction such as below floors, above ceilings, or in vertical chase-ways. A plenum accomplishes air distribution via static pressure difference between the plenum and surroundings, rather than using velocity and associated diversion/distribution devices, as in ductwork.

Process Validation

Establishing documented evidence that provides a high degree of assurance that a specific process will consistently produce a product meeting its predetermined specifications and quality attributes.

Psychrometry

Determination of the properties of gas-vapor mixtures. The air-water vapor system is the most commonly encountered.

Requirement Specifications

A detailed document used to specify the requirements of the client for individual aspects of the facility, item of equipment, utility, and systems in terms of function, throughput, operability, and applicable local standards.

Return Air

Air removed mechanically from the space and returned to the air handling system serving that space for recirculation.

Supply Air

Conditioned air supplied mechanically to the space (room), measured here in cubic feet per minute (CFM) (Divide by 0.6 to get cubic meters per hour CMH).

Ultra Low Penetration Air (ULPA) Filters

Extended media dry filters in a rigid frame that have a minimum particle-collection efficiency of 99.999% for particles greater than or equal to 0.12 μm in size.

Unidirectional Airflow

Controlled airflow through the entire cross-section of a clean zone with a steady velocity and approximately parallel streamlines.

Ventilation Rate

The number of times in an hour that the air volume of a room is replaced; see Air Change Rate.

導師解析 Tutorial Commentary

核心概念：關鍵定義中文翻譯與解析

以下將所有定義詞彙提供完整中文翻譯：

  • Acceptance Criteria（允收標準）：可能影響產品品質的關鍵參數條件限值。注意：只有「關鍵」參數才需要納入允收標準。
  • Action Limit（行動限值）：超出操作範圍的標準，需要有書面回應。
  • Air Change Rate（換氣次數）：單位時間內空間總氣量被替換的次數。
  • AF&ID（氣流與儀表圖）：顯示 HVAC 儀表控制的圖面，標註 GMP 關鍵元件。
  • AFD（氣流圖）：示意圖，顯示空調箱服務的房間、風量平衡和壓差。
  • Alert Limit（警戒限值）：在達到行動限值前的預警通知標準。
  • Arrestance（捕集率）：過濾器移除合成粉塵的能力指標。
  • As-Built Drawings（竣工圖）：反映施工完成後實際狀況的圖面。
  • BMS（建築管理系統）：控制、監測與優化環境條件的電腦系統。
  • Change Control（變更管制）：審查可能影響驗證狀態變更的正式系統。
  • Closed（封閉的）：不暴露於房間環境，能承受一定壓力或沖洗。
  • Closed Process（封閉製程）：產品及接觸面與室內環境隔離的製程狀態。
  • Commissioning（試車）：驗證並記錄設施系統性能符合既定目標的品質導向過程。

關鍵品質術語群組

  • Critical Component（關鍵組件）：其運作、接觸、資料、控制、警報或故障可能直接影響產品品質的組件。
  • Critical Location（關鍵位置）：產品暴露處，或已清潔之產品接觸面暴露處。
  • Critical Parameter（關鍵參數）：直接影響藥品特性或雜質的製程參數。
  • Critical Process Parameter / CPP（關鍵製程參數）：其變異會影響品質屬性的參數，即使受控仍為「關鍵」。
  • Critical Quality Attribute / CQA（關鍵品質屬性）：需被控制以確保產品品質的物理、化學、生物或微生物特性。

這五個「Critical」相關定義是 HVAC 系統影響評估（Impact Assessment）的基礎。HVAC 系統是否為 Direct Impact System，取決於它是否控制任何 Critical Parameter 或 CQA。

重點提示：Direct vs. Indirect Impact System

Direct Impact System（直接影響系統）：預期會直接影響產品品質的系統。除了需遵循 GEP，還需接受 Qualification（確效），包含強化審查、管控與測試。

Indirect Impact System（間接影響系統）：不會直接影響產品品質，但通常支援直接影響系統。只需遵循 GEP 即可。

這個分類決定了 HVAC 系統各組件的驗證深度與文件要求。例如：服務無菌充填區的 AHU 是 Direct Impact，而服務辦公區的 AHU 是 Indirect Impact。

設計相關術語群組

  • Design For Impact（為影響而設計）：在設計開發初期就考慮系統運作時的影響。
  • Design Point（設計點）：基礎設計中的標稱設定點，加減容差值。
  • Design Qualification / DQ（設計確效）：書面驗證提議的設計適合預定用途。
  • Design Target（設計目標）：比允收標準更保守的值，供設計師確保系統有足夠能力。注意：設計目標不應用於確效！
  • Design Tolerance（設計容差）：正常操作範圍的預期上下限。

比喻說明：Action Limit vs. Alert Limit

想像你在高速公路開車：

  • Operating Range（操作範圍） = 時速 90-110 公里（正常行駛區間）
  • Alert Limit（警戒限值） = 時速 115 公里（儀表板開始閃燈提醒你）
  • Action Limit（行動限值） = 時速 120 公里（超過就要被開罰單，需要書面記錄）
  • Design Point（設計點） = 時速 100 公里（設計師期望的最佳巡航速度）

在 HVAC 中，Alert Limit 是「早期預警」，讓你有機會在真正出問題前修正偏移。

HVAC 系統特有術語

  • Exfiltration（外滲）：空氣因壓差從門縫等處「漏出」房間。
  • Infiltration（滲入）：外部空氣因壓差「漏入」房間。在潔淨室中必須防止非受控空氣滲入。
  • Exhaust Air（排氣）：被機械排出且不再回收利用的空氣。
  • Return Air（回風）：被機械排出但回到空調系統再循環利用的空氣。
  • Supply Air（送風）：經過調節後機械送入房間的空氣。CFM ÷ 0.6 = CMH。
  • Plenum（靜壓箱/氣壓室）：利用靜壓差（非風速）分配空氣的封閉空間，可位於地板下、天花板上或垂直管道中。
  • Latent Heat（潛熱）：物質相變時釋放或吸收的能量。HVAC 中用於從濕空氣中冷凝水分。
  • Psychrometry（濕空氣學）：研究氣體-蒸氣混合物性質的學問。焓濕圖（psychrometric chart）是 HVAC 設計的基本工具。

重點提示：DOP 已被 PAO 取代

DOP（鄰苯二甲酸二辛酯）過去是 HEPA 過濾器完整性測試的標準氣溶膠材料，但因被認為具致癌性，已被 PAO（聚α烯烴）取代。但業界仍習慣稱之為「DOP 測試」，現在理解為「Dispersed Oil Particulate」的縮寫。

這是一個很好的例子，說明為何理解術語的歷史背景很重要 — 如果你在現場聽到「做 DOP test」，實際使用的材料幾乎肯定是 PAO。

過濾器與粒子相關術語

  • HEPA Filter（高效微粒空氣過濾器）：對 0.3 μm 粒子效率 >99.97%。這是無菌區的基本配備。
  • ULPA Filter（超低穿透空氣過濾器）：對 ≥0.12 μm 粒子效率 ≥99.999%。用於需要更高潔淨度的特殊場景。
  • Dust Spot Efficiency（塵斑效率）：評估過濾器去除合成粉塵的能力。
  • Arrestance（捕集率）：與 Dust Spot Efficiency 類似，但通常用於較大粒子。
  • MPPS（最高穿透粒徑）：過濾器最難捕捉的粒子大小，通常約 0.1-0.3 μm。
  • "Nearly Massless" Particles（幾乎無質量的粒子）：<0.5 μm 的粒子，佔室外空氣總質量 <1%，但佔粒子總數量的大部分。
  • Particle Count（粒子計數）：包含活性（viable）與非活性（non-viable）粒子。PCF × 35.3 = 每立方公尺粒子數。
  • PGR（粒子生成率）：空間內製程與人員每單位時間產生的粒子量，是計算所需換氣次數的關鍵輸入。

重點提示：Grade 的雙重標準

Grade（等級）的定義提到了一個關鍵差異：ISO 14644-1 只定義「運轉中」(in operation) 的粒子限值，而 EU GMP 的 Grade A-D 同時要求「靜態」(at rest) 和「運轉中」兩種狀態的粒子限值。

這意味著在歐盟法規環境下做 HVAC 設計，你需要確保系統在兩種狀態下都能達標 — 這對系統設計有直接影響。

驗證與文件術語

  • Enhanced Design Review / EDR（強化設計審查）：在專案適當階段對工程設計進行的書面審查，確認符合運營與法規期望。
  • Functional Design Specification / FDS（功能設計規格書）：描述 HVAC 如何達到允收標準的文件（房間等級、壓差、風量、恢復時間、過濾層級等）。
  • GEP（良好工程實務）：在專案生命週期中應用的既定工程方法與標準，交付適當且具成本效益的解決方案。
  • Process Validation（製程驗證）：建立書面證據，證明特定製程能一致地生產符合預定規格的產品。
  • Requirement Specifications（需求規格書）：詳細說明客戶對設施、設備、公用系統需求的文件。
  • Change Control（變更管制）：審查可能影響驗證狀態變更的正式系統 — 任何 HVAC 系統的修改都必須經過此程序。

比喻說明：Closed Process

想像一個密封的太空人頭盔（Closed Process）vs. 一個敞開的安全帽（Open Process）。太空人頭盔內的空氣完全與外界隔離，即使外面環境再惡劣也不影響裡面。藥廠的 Closed Process 也是同樣概念 — 產品和產品接觸面被密封設備完全包裹，不與房間環境接觸。

這直接影響 HVAC 設計：如果製程是 Closed 的，對房間潔淨等級的要求可以較低；如果是 Open 的，房間必須達到更高的潔淨等級。

公式參考：換氣次數計算

Air Change Rate (ACR) = Supply Air Volume (CFM) x 60
                        ─────────────────────────────
                           Room Volume (cubic feet)

換氣次數 = 送風量 (CFM) x 60 / 房間體積 (立方英尺)

單位轉換：
  CFM / 0.6 = CMH (cubic meters per hour)
  PCF x 35.3 = particles per cubic meter

實務應用 Practical Application

情境：在 HVAC 系統的影響評估（Impact Assessment）會議上，品質部門問你：「為什麼送風系統是 Direct Impact，而排風系統是 Indirect Impact？」

建議回答：根據本指引的定義，Direct Impact System 是「預期會直接影響產品品質」的系統。送風系統（Supply Air System）直接提供經過 HEPA 過濾的潔淨空氣到製造區，控制著 Critical Parameter（溫度、濕度、粒子等級）。排風系統（Exhaust Air System）將空氣排出且不回收，它支援送風系統維持正壓差，但不直接接觸或影響產品品質，因此屬於 Indirect Impact。

練習思考 Practice Questions

  1. 說明 Design Target、Design Tolerance、Acceptance Criteria 和 Action Limit 之間的層級關係。在 HVAC 系統中，以溫度控制為例說明各層級的數值可能是什麼？
  2. 為什麼 CPP 的定義特別強調「即使受控仍為關鍵」？這對 HVAC 系統的變更管制有什麼啟示？
  3. Exfiltration 和 Infiltration 如何影響潔淨室的壓差管理？如果一間 Grade B 房間發生 Infiltration，可能的後果是什麼？
  4. 比較 HEPA 和 ULPA 過濾器的效率規格。在什麼情況下需要使用 ULPA 而非 HEPA？
  5. Closed Process 和 Open Process 對 HVAC 設計要求有什麼差異？舉一個 CDMO 中常見的例子。

本節重點回顧 Section Summary

  • 參考文獻體系：HVAC 系統設計涉及 31 項標準，橫跨法規（ICH/FDA/EU GMP）、國際標準（ISO 14644）、工程實務（ASHRAE/SMACNA）與藥廠專用指引（ISPE Baseline），專案團隊需要跨領域的標準知識
  • 組織與縮寫：40+ 個組織縮寫和 100+ 個技術縮略詞構成了 HVAC 專業溝通的「共同語言」。最高頻的包括 AHU、HEPA、DP、BMS、TAB、FAT/SAT
  • Critical 術語家族：Critical Component、Critical Location、Critical Parameter、CPP、CQA 是系統影響評估的基礎，決定了哪些 HVAC 元件需要接受 Qualification
  • Direct vs. Indirect Impact：這個分類是 HVAC 驗證策略的核心 — Direct Impact 系統需要 GEP + Qualification，Indirect Impact 只需 GEP
  • 設計參數層級：Design Target < Design Tolerance < Operating Range < Alert Limit < Action Limit < Acceptance Criteria — 這個層級關係適用於所有 HVAC 關鍵參數
  • DOP 已更名為 PAO 測試：由於 DOP 的致癌疑慮，現在使用 PAO（聚α烯烴）作為 HEPA 過濾器完整性測試的氣溶膠材料，但「DOP 測試」的名稱仍沿用
  • Grade 的雙重要求：EU GMP 的 Grade A-D 同時要求靜態與運轉中的粒子限值，比 ISO 14644-1 更嚴格，這對 HVAC 系統設計有直接影響

SterileGMP Knowledge Hub — 僅供學習參考
